gmw_00012777.name: Pegasus III
gmw_00012777.overview: Pegasus III is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located in the outer halo of the Milky Way [arXiv:2204.01917]. It has an elliptical half-light radius of approximately 118 parsecs and may have experienced a close passage with the dwarf galaxy Pisces II just over 1 Gyr ago [arXiv:2204.01917].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST photometry of Pegasus III, measures its structure and luminosity, finds no clear interaction morphology, and explores possible past association with Pisces II.

quotes.quote: We present deep Hubble Space Telescope (HST) photometry of the ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies Pegasus III (Peg III) and Pisces II (Psc II), two of the most distant satellites in the halo of the Milky Way (MW). We measure the structure of both galaxies, derive mass-to-light ratios with newly determined absolute magnitudes, and compare our findings to expectations from UFD-mass simulations. For Peg III, we find an elliptical half-light radius of a_h = 1 88^ +0.42 _ 0.33 ( 118^ +31 _ 30  pc) and M_V = 4.17 ^ +0.19 _ 0.22 ; for Psc II, we measure a_h = 1 31^ +0.10 _ 0.09 ( 69 8  pc) and M_V = 4.28 ^ +0.19 _ 0.16 .

Using proper motions (PMs) from Gaia early Data Release 3, we investigate the possibility of any past association by integrating orbits for the two UFDs in a MW-only and a combined MW and Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC) potential. We find that including the gravitational influence of the LMC is crucial, even for these outer-halo satellites, and that a possible orbital history exists where Peg III and Psc II experienced a close ( 10--20 kpc) passage about each other just over 1 Gyr ago, followed by a collective passage around the LMC ( 30--60 kpc) just under 1 Gyr ago.

gmw_00012778.name: Pisces II
gmw_00012778.overview: Pisces II is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located in the outer halo of the Milky Way [arXiv:2204.01917]. It has a half-light radius of approximately 69 parsecs and may share a past orbital association with the Pegasus III dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2204.01917].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST photometry of Pisces II, measures its size and luminosity, finds no clear interaction morphology, and models possible orbital association with Pegasus III.

quotes.quote: We present deep Hubble Space Telescope (HST) photometry of the ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies Pegasus III (Peg III) and Pisces II (Psc II), two of the most distant satellites in the halo of the Milky Way (MW). We measure the structure of both galaxies, derive mass-to-light ratios with newly determined absolute magnitudes, and compare our findings to expectations from UFD-mass simulations. For Peg III, we find an elliptical half-light radius of a_h = 1 88^ +0.42 _ 0.33 ( 118^ +31 _ 30  pc) and M_V = 4.17 ^ +0.19 _ 0.22 ; for Psc II, we measure a_h = 1 31^ +0.10 _ 0.09 ( 69 8  pc) and M_V = 4.28 ^ +0.19 _ 0.16 .

Using proper motions (PMs) from Gaia early Data Release 3, we investigate the possibility of any past association by integrating orbits for the two UFDs in a MW-only and a combined MW and Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC) potential. We find that including the gravitational influence of the LMC is crucial, even for these outer-halo satellites, and that a possible orbital history exists where Peg III and Psc II experienced a close ( 10--20 kpc) passage about each other just over 1 Gyr ago, followed by a collective passage around the LMC ( 30--60 kpc) just under 1 Gyr ago.

gmw_00012779.name: host galaxy of GRB 210112A
gmw_00012779.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 210112A is detected in stacked optical bands, including the r', i', and z' bands [arXiv:2308.16121]. Modeling at a fixed redshift of z=2 indicates a high stellar mass, a star formation rate of 40 solar masses per year, and modest dust extinction [arXiv:2308.16121].

sources.summary: The GRB 210112A host galaxy is detected in stacked optical bands; CIGALE modeling at fixed z=2 gives high stellar mass, strong star formation, and modest dust extinction.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of GRB 210112A is detected clearly within the 3- limit in the stacked frames of ', i' , and z' bands. The photometric analysis is conducted using Source Extraction and Photometry SEP, Barbary2018 , a python Package built on the Source-Extractor SExtractor, Bertin1996 software suitable for performing faint-galaxy photometry, and SDSS standard stars were used to perform the calibration. The estimated calibrated ' , i' , and z' band magnitudes of the host galaxy of the GRB 210112A are 23.89 0.12 , 23.67 0.24 and 23.35 0.25 AB mags, respectively, before accounting for the expected Galactic extinction of E(B-V) = 0.01 Schlafly2011 .

CIGALE creates a grid of models across the combined parameter space of the component templates and uses Bayesian inference to derive the most likely values for each parameter. We initially allowed the redshift to vary between 0.5 and 2.5, finding it tended towards a lower redshift of z 1.3 but this is poorly constrained and likely to be unreliable. We therefore assumed a fixed redshift of z=2 from gcnKann2021a and found it to be compatible with the host data. We infer a stellar mass of (M_*/M_ ) 10.9 , a star formation rate of 40 M_ yr ^ 1 and a dust extinction of E(B-V) 0.1 , properties generally consistent with the known LGRB host population e.g. Levesque2014,Stanway2014 . We note, however, that these values are generally not tightly constrained due to the narrow wavelength range of our host galaxy photometry.

gmw_00012780.name: HUDF 472
gmw_00012780.overview: HUDF 472 is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.9 [arXiv:0810.2795]. Its estimated effective radius varies significantly depending on whether it is observed in optical or near-infrared wavelengths [arXiv:0810.2795].

sources.summary: The paper treats HUDF 472 as a z 1.9 early-type galaxy whose NICMOS effective-radius estimate is twice previous values, possibly because optical and F160W data sample different rest-frame light.

quotes.quote: For four galaxies of our sample, namely HUDF 472, HUDF 996, HDF 1031 and HDF 1523, the estimate of the effective radius derived from HST images in the optical bands (F814W and F850W filters) sampling the rest-frame wavelength _ rest <4000 , is also available from the literature.

For the galaxy HUDF 472 at z 1.9 our estimate is a factor two both the estimate of Daddi et al. (2005) and that of Cimatti et al. (2008). It should be noted that, given the redshift of this latter galaxy, the filters F850W and F160W sample the profile at _ rest 2900 \ and at _ rest 5700 \ respectively, two wavelength ranges which differ substantially for the contribution from the young and the old stars respectively. Thus, in this case the different estimates could reflect a real difference of the galaxy profile if observed at such different wavelengths (e.g. McGrath et al. 2008).

gmw_00012781.name: SDSS J121824.98+463700.5
gmw_00012781.overview: SDSS J121824.98+463700.5 is a star-forming host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.1665 [arXiv:1502.06964]. It contains both an old stellar population and a recent stellar population with a moderate star formation rate [arXiv:1502.06964].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J121824.98+463700.5 as PS1-13arp's host, measuring z=0.1665 from extended emission lines and fitting a massive star-forming galaxy with old and recent stellar populations.

quotes.quote: PS1-13arp was identified as an optical transient in PS1 field MD06 by photpipe on UT 2013 April 26 at R.A. 12:18:25.108, Dec. +46:37:01.30 (J2000), with a spatial offset of 1.577 arcsec East and 0.275 arcsec North of its host galaxy (SDSS J121824.98+463700.5). Figure fig:diff shows the PS1 difference image detection in the i band, as well as a color image of the deep reference template with only the host galaxy flux.

The 2D spectrum displays strong, spatially extended emission lines (H , [ N II ] 6583, 6548 , [ S II ] 6716,6731 ) indicative of star-formation in the host galaxy, from which we measure a redshift of z=0.1665 0.001 .

The fit requires both an old stellar population with an exponentially declining star formation rate with = 6.7 2 Gyr as well as a recent stellar population with a moderate star formation rate of 3.6 1.2 M_ yr ^ 1 , with a total stellar mass of (M/M_ ) = 10.4 0.1 . Note that this mass is consistent with the gas-phase metallicity that we estimate from the ratio of the strong nebular emission lines in the host galaxy spectrum. We measure ( [ N II ] 6584/H ) = -0.37 0.06 , which corresponds to a metallicity of (O/H) + 12 9 Kewley2002 , and is in good agreement with the mass-metallicity relation for star-forming galaxies Tremonti2004 .

gmw_00012782.name: BCG
gmw_00012782.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of Abell 2495 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:2312.12855]. It exhibits a spatial offset between its nucleus, the peak of the intracluster medium's X-ray emission, and its warm H-alpha emitting gas [arXiv:2312.12855].

sources.summary: The paper presents the Abell 2495 BCG as an offset FR-I radio galaxy whose AGN activity inflates cavities and can balance ICM cooling despite displaced X-ray, H, and galaxy peaks.

quotes.quote: This object is known for the presence of a triple offset between the peaks of the intracluster medium (ICM), the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG), and the warm gas glowing in H line. The new Chandra data confirm that the X-ray emission peak is located at a distance of 6.2 kpc from the BCG, and at 3.9 kpc from the H emission peak. Moreover, we identify two generations of X-ray cavities in the ICM, likely inflated by the central radio galaxy activity. Through a detailed morphological and spectral analysis we determine that the power of the AGN outbursts ( P_ cav = 4.7 1.3 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) is enough to counterbalance the radiative losses from ICM cooling ( L_ cool = 5.7 0.1 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ).

They characterized the radio activity of the BCG, finding that it is an FR-I radio galaxy with 1.4 GHz luminosity of 2.18 10^ 23 W Hz ^ 1 and size of 14 kpc. The X-ray analysis of the ICM suggested that this cluster has a cool core, where a significant spatial offset between the X-ray peak and the BCG ( 6 kpc) is present. An H nebula encasing a dust filament connects the peak of the X-ray emission to the peak of the BCG radio-optical continuum, where the dust peak is located. Interestingly, the X-ray emission peak is also slightly offset ( 3.5 kpc) from that of the H emission, suggesting that a merger or some other strong disturbance has separated the BCG nucleus from both the hot gas and the nebular emission.

gmw_00012783.name: GOODS 850-7
gmw_00012783.overview: GOODS 850-7 is a submillimeter galaxy that appears to be a chain galaxy consisting of three knots with highly varied colors [arXiv:1209.1626]. The system has a spectroscopic redshift of z=2.578, though the submillimeter emission arises specifically from one of the redder components [arXiv:1209.1626].

sources.summary: The paper finds GOODS 850-7 has two optical counterparts, but SMA data place the submillimeter emission on one redder chain-galaxy component and leave projection uncertainty.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Submillimeter Properties of the SMA Sample: 850-7 & 04 & 189.067123 & 62.253834

quotes.quote: In their Section 3.6, Magnelli et al. \ state that GOODS 850-7 (GN04) has two optical counterparts and that the IRAC photometry of the two counterparts is consistent with them being at the same redshift. They therefore assume that it is an interacting pair and derive the dust properties of GOODS 850-7 (GN04) by adding the MIR, FIR, and radio 2 fluxes of the two ``counterparts'' together. However, our SMA observations clearly show that the submillimeter emission arises from only one of the two IRAC sources (see Figure A1).

In the top panel, we show the region around GOODS 850-7 (GN04). This source appears to be a chain galaxy (Cowie et al. \ 1995), consisting of three knots with very varied colors. The spectroscopic redshift of z=2.578 from Chapman et al. \ (2005) relates to the upper bluer regions, but the knot corresponding to the submillimeter emission is much redder. The magnitudes in the table correspond to this redder component. Given the configuration, we accept the redshift as applying to the SMG, but we caution that it could still be a chance projection.

gmw_00012784.name: GOODS 850-12
gmw_00012784.overview: GOODS 850-12 is a submillimeter galaxy located near an X-ray source at a redshift of z=2.737 [arXiv:1209.1626]. This source appears to be part of a merging system with outlying debris, where extended radio emission suggests star formation is the primary power source [arXiv:1209.1626].

sources.summary: The paper associates GOODS 850-12 with submillimeter emission near a z=2.737 X-ray source in a probable merging system, while noting chance-projection uncertainty.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Submillimeter Properties of the SMA Sample: 850-12 & 15/850.2 & 189.233002 & 62.200531

quotes.quote: In the center panel, we show the region around GOODS 850-12 (GN15/HDF 850.2). The submillimeter emission arises from a location near an X-ray source that has a redshift of z=2.737 from Barger et al. \ (2008). The X-ray source, which appears to be part of a merging system with outlying debris, was tentatively identified by P06 as the counterpart to the SMG. We feel it is likely that the submillimeter emission is associated with the merging system and therefore accept the redshift as applying to the SMG, but again we caution that it could still be a chance projection.

Six of our SMA sources contain AGNs based on their X-ray luminosities, but in the case of GOODS 850-12 (GN15/HDF850.2), the radio emission is extended, suggesting the primary power source may be star formation (see Table tab5 ).

gmw_00012785.name: GOODS 850-17
gmw_00012785.overview: GOODS 850-17 is a submillimeter galaxy located near a brighter X-ray and radio source [arXiv:1209.1626]. The system may represent a galaxy merger, a chance projection, or a gravitationally lensed source [arXiv:1209.1626].

sources.summary: The paper places GOODS 850-17 off a brighter X-ray/radio galaxy, considers merger, projection, or lensing explanations, and rejects that brighter galaxy redshift.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Submillimeter Properties of the SMA Sample: 850-17 & & 189.120160 & 62.179242

quotes.quote: In the bottom panel, we show the region around GOODS 850-17. Here the submillimeter emission lies off the edge of a much brighter galaxy. The bright galaxy is an X-ray and radio source with a redshift of z=1.013 from Barger et al. \ (2008). Although it is possible that this is a merging system, in which case the redshift would also apply to the SMG, it is also possible that the SMG is a chance projection, possibly amplified by gravitational lensing due to the larger galaxy. Given its 2.3'' offset from the radio and X-ray source, we do not accept this redshift identification for the SMG. (Note that Bothwell et al. \ (2012) also did not detect GOODS 850-17 with the IRAM PdBI in CO [ 2-1 ] at this redshift.) With the above assignments for these three difficult cases, we have spectroscopic redshifts for 10 of the 16 SMGs (8 optical/IR and 2 CO) with values ranging from z=2-5.2 .

gmw_00012786.name: NGC 199
gmw_00012786.overview: NGC 199 is a lenticular central galaxy [arXiv:2006.04294]. It hosts a peaked overdensity of surrounding low surface brightness galaxies, which represents its dwarf satellite population [arXiv:2006.04294].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 199 as the lenticular central galaxy with the strongest LSBG overdensity, interpreted as a peaked dwarf-satellite population.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Characteristics of the 10 most prominent density peaks: NGC 199 & (9.8882, 3.1385) & 0.0153

quotes.quote: We find that the normalized number density of LSBGs peaks at similar amplitudes for most systems, with the most peaked overdensity found around the lenticular galaxy NGC 199. This may be expected given that this association represents the dwarf satellite population of a single central bright galaxy.

The third case where the distribution of 2MPZ and LSBG galaxies differ is around NGC 199. Again, the LSBGs are much more peaked than the 2MPZ sample, suggesting that the observed LSBG overdensity is caused by dwarf galaxies surrounding a single central host.
We find that for RXC J0340.1 1835 and Fornax, p 0.01 (thus strongly rejecting the null hypothesis), p=0.015 for NGC 199 (making the null hypothesis unlikely), while for all the other systems p>0.1 .

gmw_00012787.name: SBS0
gmw_00012787.overview: SBS0 is a tadpole galaxy with a major axis length of approximately 2.6 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2211.09292]. Its main head contains young star clusters under 10 million years old, while the main body exhibits a variety of cluster ages ranging from very young to old [arXiv:2211.09292].

sources.summary: The HST study finds SBS0 has young sources around its main head, varied cluster ages in the body, no strong age trend, and a 2.6 kpc major axis.

quotes.quote: SBS0 In SBS0 we find typically young ages <10 Myr for sources in and around the main head of the galaxy. The main body of the galaxy shows a variety of cluster ages, ranging from very young to old, with several of the youngest clusters being associated with emission in the main body. We do not observe any significant age trends throughout the main galaxy (besides the overall young ages in the head). We measured approximate major axis lengths A _ major of the tadpoles based on the visible extent of the galaxies on the HST F814W images. The major axis of SBS0 determined by this method is D _ HST 23 arcsec, corresponding to A _ major 2.6 kpc for its distance of 24.7 Mpc.

gmw_00012788.name: SBS1
gmw_00012788.overview: SBS1 is an interacting galaxy connected to its more massive companion, SBS 1129+577, by a northern H I tidal bridge [arXiv:2211.09292]. The galaxy features a single large star-forming site in the south, a smaller central site, and scattered star clusters with no clear age progression [arXiv:2211.09292].

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS1 as an interacting tadpole/BCD system with an H I bridge, a southern star-forming site, scattered clusters, and no clear age progression.

quotes.quote: SBS1 is interacting with its more massive companion galaxy SBS 1129+577 and an H i bridge joins the two galaxies. The interaction, however, seems unlikely to be the source of low metallicity material in SBS1 since the more massive and optically brighter SBS 1129+577 galaxy is expected to have higher metallicity than SBS1.

SBS1 SBS1 (D _ HST 47 arcsec; A _ major 5.5 kpc) has a single large star-forming site in the south, less dominant than in the other galaxies in this paper, plus a smaller site near the center.The smaller apparent size of the brightest star forming region in SBS1 is primarily due its location in a relatively large galaxy. There are star clusters scattered across the galaxy, including older clusters between the head and the midsection, with no clear age progression. The H i tidal bridge is to the north, on a larger spatial scale than the galaxy, and does not appear to be directly connected to the current star forming patterns.

gmw_00012789.name: UM461
gmw_00012789.overview: UM461 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy with a face-on disk inclination and an extended neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:2211.09292]. The galaxy features two luminous, compact, young star-forming regions, with its youngest star clusters located around these main clumps and along its western edge [arXiv:2211.09292].

sources.summary: The paper describes UM461 as a blue compact dwarf with a face-on disk, two luminous compact young regions, an extended H I disk, and youngest clusters around two clumps.

quotes.quote: UM461 is presumably more face-on inclination 30 10 ^ ; vanzee98 . UM461 is also unusual in having 2 luminous, compact, young stellar regions. The H i line map of UM461 by shows an extended H i disk, typical of blue compact dwarf (BCD) systems, with no indication of a tidal interaction with the nearby UM462 system.

UM461 has two large star-forming regions in the E and W portions in the northern half of the galaxy. Star clusters are evident in several regions throughout the galaxies, discussed below. The logarithmic stretches show extended emission well beyond the brightest parts of the disks. In UM461, the southwest part of the galaxy is extended away from the star-forming peaks and a ridge containing young stellar populations extends westwards from the north side of the system.

UM461 In UM461 (D _ HST 24 arcsec; A _ major 1.5 kpc), the youngest star clusters are around the two main clumps, near H regions, and along the western edge. There are intermediate ages between the clumps, and older clusters throughout the galaxy. There is a sprinkling of more young clusters in the north-western part of the galaxy, extending away from the main body of this small system, some of which appear to be associated with faint nebular emission.

gmw_00012790.name: OHM J095903.22+025356.1
gmw_00012790.overview: OHM J095903.22+025356.1 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy and starburst system that hosts an OH megamaser [arXiv:2312.04345]. The galaxy is currently undergoing a merger, as evidenced by a tidal tail, and its emission is likely gravitationally lensed [arXiv:2312.04345].

sources.summary: The paper describes OHM J095903.22+025356.1 as an OH megamaser host with high SFR, ULIRG-like colors, merger tidal-tail evidence, and likely lensing.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy has a stellar mass of M_ = 2.95 10^ 10 M _ and a star-formation rate of SFR = 371 M _ yr ^ 1 , placing it 1.5 dex above the main sequence for star-forming galaxies at this redshift, and can be classified as an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy. Alongside the optical imaging data, which exhibits evidence for a tidal tail, this suggests that the OH megamaser arises from a system that is currently undergoing a merger, which is stimulating star formation and providing the necessary conditions for pumping the OH molecule to saturation. The OHM is likely to be lensed, with a magnification factor of 2.5 , and perhaps more if the maser emitting region is compact and suitably offset relative to the centroid of its host galaxy's optical light.

Using the colour-colour plots from , who investigate where the host galaxies of masers should reside compared to those of H i galaxies, we find that the host galaxy of OHM J095903.22+025356.1 has WISE and IRAC colours fully consistent with the local OHM host galaxy population. However, we note it has a relatively red W2-W3 = 6.15 mag, meaning it lies at the extreme red end of the ULIRG population in the WISE colour-colour diagram of . The extreme W2-W3 colour is partly due to the fact that the W2 and W3 bands are sampling the rest-frame 2.7 and 7.1 m emission, and that at these rest wavelengths, the PAH emission contributes significantly to the measured photometry, particularly in the W3 filter. However, we note the W1-W2 = 0.714 colour is very much in the middle of the expected range for ULIRGs and starburst galaxies and lies within the colour range expected for OHM host galaxies see e.g.

gmw_00012791.name: G1
gmw_00012791.overview: G1 is a foreground lens candidate galaxy located 2.6 arcseconds west of an OH megamaser host galaxy [arXiv:2312.04345]. It has a photometric redshift of approximately 0.35 to 0.43 and a Seersic index of 3.74 [arXiv:2312.04345].

sources.summary: The paper models G1 as the nearby foreground lens candidate west of the OHM host, deriving photometric redshift, stellar mass, and an Einstein radius.

quotes.quote: There is a galaxy 2.6 arcsec away from the OHM host galaxy (the galaxy lying directly to the west in Figs. fig:postage , fig:ohm-contours and fig:datamodelresid , referred to as G1), which is present in the COSMOS2020 catalogue. It has a best-fit photometric redshift of z_ phot = 0.43^ +0.02 _ 0.03 and stellar mass of _ 10 ( M_ / M _ ) = 9.56 using Le Phare Ilbert2006 and z_ phot = 0.35 0.01 and _ 10 (M_ / M _ ) = 9.75 derived using the Eazy eazy . The Einstein radius for a lens of this mass and redshift (similar for both photometric redshifts) is 1.1 arcsec, assuming a halo mass of M_ halo = 3.2 10^ 11 M _ , estimated using an empirically derived stellar-mass to halo-mass ratio Behroozi2010 .

We explore two lens models: the first is the `single lens model' with the closest galaxy (G1) as the only lens, with a projected separation of 2.4 arcsec, the second model, the `two lens model', includes a more distant galaxy (G2) which is 9.1 arcsec away to the north west (and can be seen in Fig fig:ohm-contours ), and may perturb both the convergence and shear of the lensing system. G2 is also listed in the COSOMOS2020 catalogues and has a photometric redshift of z_ phot = 0.35 , with stellar mass M_ = 1.9 10^ 10 M _ ( M_ halo = 8.8 10^ 11 M _ ), which has an Einstein radius of _ E =1.8 arcsec. Both models assume Single Isothermal Ellipsoid (SIE) mass density profiles for the foreground lens(es) and lens redshift(s) of z= 0.35 . Both models also assume lens light follows mass (i.e. the lens light and mass distributions have co-located centroids and matching ellipticity and position angle). We first fit the G1 galaxy light profile without considering any lensing (i.e. _ E = 0 ), deriving a S ersic index of n_ s = 3.74 .
Using the single lens model we find a lens Einstein radius for G1 of _ E = 1.32 0.16 arcsec, while with the two lens model we find a slightly lower value of = 1.16 0.16 arcsec.

gmw_00012792.name: ID582
gmw_00012792.overview: ID582 is a blue star-forming galaxy situated at a redshift of approximately 4 [arXiv:2208.03468]. Its spectral energy distribution exhibits a flux boost in the F277W band, which is driven by a Balmer break and strong contributions from H-beta and [OIII] emission lines [arXiv:2208.03468].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID582, with ID1284, as a blue z~4 star-forming galaxy whose red-band flux boost requires strong H and [OIII] emission-line contributions.

quotes.quote: To start with ID582 and ID1284 show examples of blue z 4 star forming galaxies that are the dominant population of galaxies selected by our criteria. It can be seen that the increased flux >2 \ comes from the Balmer break together with a strong contribution from H and [ OIII ] emission lines. These SEDs can not be fit well at all without strong line flux contributions, if one removes the high equivalent width template from EAZY then the median ^2 SED residual of the faint sample increases significantly from 2.2 to 7.7.

The contribution of such emission lines can be seen in for example the F277 boost of ID582 and 1284 in Figure 1, it is even evident by eye in the F277 images. figure 1 The number of such sources was notable, so we investigated what level of emission line equivalent widths were needed to give such boosts to the photometry.

This makes sense as the NIRCAM filters are quite broad, for example to boost the F277W by a factor of 50-70 (as shown by the SED ID 582 and 1284) requires an observed frame equivalent width of 3000--5000 .

gmw_00012793.name: ID1284
gmw_00012793.overview: ID1284 is a blue star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4 [arXiv:2208.03468]. Its spectral energy distribution and F277W filter boost indicate a strong contribution from hydrogen-beta and oxygen III emission lines [arXiv:2208.03468].

sources.summary: The paper presents ID1284, with ID582, as a blue z~4 star-forming galaxy whose SED and F277 boost require strong emission-line flux contributions.

quotes.quote: To start with ID582 and ID1284 show examples of blue z 4 star forming galaxies that are the dominant population of galaxies selected by our criteria. It can be seen that the increased flux >2 \ comes from the Balmer break together with a strong contribution from H and [ OIII ] emission lines. These SEDs can not be fit well at all without strong line flux contributions, if one removes the high equivalent width template from EAZY then the median ^2 SED residual of the faint sample increases significantly from 2.2 to 7.7.

The contribution of such emission lines can be seen in for example the F277 boost of ID582 and 1284 in Figure 1, it is even evident by eye in the F277 images. figure 1 The number of such sources was notable, so we investigated what level of emission line equivalent widths were needed to give such boosts to the photometry.

This makes sense as the NIRCAM filters are quite broad, for example to boost the F277W by a factor of 50-70 (as shown by the SED ID 582 and 1284) requires an observed frame equivalent width of 3000--5000 .

gmw_00012794.name: ID5029
gmw_00012794.overview: ID5029 is a resolved quiescent galaxy at a redshift of approximately 2 [arXiv:2208.03468]. It has an extremely low stellar mass of 8 billion solar masses, low star formation, and moderate dust attenuation [arXiv:2208.03468].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID5029 as a resolved, extremely low-mass z=2 quiescent galaxy with moderate dust, low star formation, and F200W below ground-based K-band limits.

quotes.quote: ID5029 is well fit by a z=2.0 quiescent galaxy with an extremely low stellar mass of 8 10^8 M _ , star formation rate 0.3 M _ yr ^ 1 and with moderate dust attenuation ( A_V=1.3 ). We expect such low mass quiescent galaxies to be significantly rarer than their massive cousins (of which many examples can be seen at brighter magnitudes in Figure 1) because the quiescent z 2 galaxy stellar mass function of declines at low masses.

The source is resolved with an estimated size of 0.2––0.4 arcsec; this is similar to those of the lowest mass ( 10^ 10 M _ ) quiescent galaxies of . ID5029 is likely the lowest mass quiescent galaxy identified at z 2 . We note it has F200W = 27.7, considerably below the limit of ground based K band surveys S16 .

gmw_00012795.name: ID4387
gmw_00012795.overview: ID4387 is a high-confidence Lyman-break galaxy candidate at a redshift of approximately 12.6 [arXiv:2208.03468]. The galaxy was discovered using classical color selection and exhibits a pronounced Lyman dropout between the F150W and F200W filters [arXiv:2208.03468].

sources.summary: The paper recovers ID4387 as a robust high-confidence z=12.6 Lyman-break galaxy candidate with a pronounced dropout between F150W and F200W.

quotes.quote: Our general selection criteria allow us to independently identify other phenomena as diverse as the robust z 12 Lyman Break Galaxy reported in paper III, and a very cool brown dwarf reported in XIII.

ID4387 is a high confidence z=12.6 Lyman break galaxy candidate with a pronounced Lyman dropout between F150W and F200W, this was presented in detail in our companion Paper III Castellano2022 where it was discovered by classical Lyman break color selection.
We recover a robust z=12.6 Lyman break galaxy found by earlier color selection and identify two additional, weaker, candidates at z 16 .

gmw_00012796.name: ID679
gmw_00012796.overview: ID679 is a faint galaxy candidate with a possible Lyman-break redshift of 15.9 [arXiv:2208.03468]. This high-redshift solution is not robust, as the object's spectral energy distribution and redshift probability distribution show a probability of it being at a low redshift of approximately 1 [arXiv:2208.03468].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID679 as a possible z=15.9 Lyman-break galaxy candidate, but notes its faintness, unconstrained color, and significant low-redshift probability make it non-robust.

quotes.quote: ID679 is a possible z=15.9 Lyman break galaxy candidate with F444W=26.9; it has a candidate Lyman break between F200W and F277W, a blue continuum shape at longer wavelengths ( >2 ), and no flux detected in the bluer channels. It is quite faint; because of this the F115W F200W color is not constrained well enough to have put it in the color selection window for these redshifts of Paper III. This also means the z=15.9 solution is not robust; the SED and P(z) of of this object show that there is significant probability of low redshift ( z 1 ).
Given low redshift is a priori more likely we can not regard this as a strong candidate.

gmw_00012797.name: ID2060
gmw_00012797.overview: ID2060 is a faint, weak candidate for a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy at z ~ 16 [arXiv:2208.03468]. However, it is not robust due to its low-redshift alternative solutions and potential photometric contamination from a nearby bright galaxy's diffraction spike [arXiv:2208.03468].

sources.summary: The paper presents ID2060 as a weak z~16 candidate with low-redshift alternatives and possible nearby-galaxy contamination; the two faint z~16 candidates are not robust.

quotes.quote: The other z=16.4 object in Figure 1, ID2060, is similar and also has low-z solutions, furthermore it lies very close to the diffraction spike of a nearby bright galaxy that may contaminate the photometry.
Confirming the two objects in our sample would require significantly deeper imaging data, shortwards of F200W.

We recover a robust z=12.6 Lyman break galaxy found by earlier color selection and identify two additional, weaker, candidates at z 16 . However, these two are not robust due to their very faint magnitudes.

gmw_00012798.name: BCGS
gmw_00012798.overview: BCGS is one of the two brightest cluster galaxies in the Abell 1240 cluster [arXiv:0905.3011]. It is located in the southern region of the cluster, where it resides within the southern subclump [arXiv:0905.3011].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCGS as one of Abell 1240's two brightest galaxies, places it in the southern subclump, and uses it with the other BCG to trace merger geometry.

quotes.quote: In particular, the two brightest ones, ID. 75 and ID. 56, lie in the southern and northern region of A1240, respectively, and show comparable luminosity (hereafter BCGS and BCGN).
A1240S and A1240N host the brightest and the second brightest galaxies, BCGS and BCGN, respectively.

The values of relevant parameters for the two--clumps system, as deduced from the BCGN and BCGS, are: the relative LOS velocity in the rest--frame, V_ rf =390 , and the projected linear distance between the two clumps, D=1.2 .

gmw_00012799.name: BCGN
gmw_00012799.overview: BCGN is one of the two brightest cluster galaxies in the Abell 1240 galaxy cluster [arXiv:0905.3011]. It is located in the northern region of the cluster and is used to trace the geometry of the system's merger [arXiv:0905.3011].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCGN as one of Abell 1240's two brightest galaxies, places it in the northern subclump, and uses it with the other BCG to trace merger geometry.

quotes.quote: In particular, the two brightest ones, ID. 75 and ID. 56, lie in the southern and northern region of A1240, respectively, and show comparable luminosity (hereafter BCGS and BCGN).
A1240S and A1240N host the brightest and the second brightest galaxies, BCGS and BCGN, respectively.

The values of relevant parameters for the two--clumps system, as deduced from the BCGN and BCGS, are: the relative LOS velocity in the rest--frame, V_ rf =390 , and the projected linear distance between the two clumps, D=1.2 .

gmw_00012800.name: J081420G1
gmw_00012800.overview: J081420G1 is a galaxy with an inclination of approximately 35 degrees and an azimuth angle of about 80 degrees [arXiv:1110.5877]. It features a galactic wind modeled as a cone pointing away from the observer with an outflow speed of roughly 200 km/s [arXiv:1110.5877].

sources.summary: The paper models J081420G1 as a minor-axis quasar-galaxy pair whose redshifted absorption is matched by a cone pointing away from the observer with V_out about 200 km/s.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:J0814 (a) shows an example of the wind model for the galaxy J081420G1 (towards the quasar SDSSJ081420.19 + 383408.3) whose inclination is i 35^ , and azimuth angle is | | 80^ .

In this particular case, we choose the model where the cone is pointing away from the observer since the observed \ absorption is redshifted with respect to the galaxy systemic velocity (0 ). The wind speed V_ out is tuned to match the observed velocity range. For this particular case, we find that V_ out 200 25 \ produces a good match to the data.

gmw_00012801.name: J102819G1
gmw_00012801.overview: J102819G1 is a galaxy with an inclination of approximately 55 degrees [arXiv:1110.5877]. It hosts a galactic wind with an inferred radial speed of about 300 kilometers per second, which produces absorption redward of the galaxy's systemic velocity [arXiv:1110.5877].

sources.summary: The paper models J102819G1 as a less inclined galaxy where the wind gives redward absorption; extending the model radius yields V_out about 300 km/s and reproduces the profile.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:J1028 shows the wind model for J102819G1 towards the quasar SDSSJ102847.00 + 391800.4. The main difference with the previous example is that this galaxy is less inclined with i 55^ . As a result, the wind produces absorption only redward of the galaxy systemic velocity. Because of the large impact parameter ( b=89 ) of the quasar, we extended our wind model to R_ max =140 \ and the inferred radial wind speed is V_ out 300 . Note that the observed velocity profile is rather well reproduced.

gmw_00012802.name: J111850G1
gmw_00012802.overview: The galaxy J111850G1 has a low inclination of approximately 30 degrees and a star formation rate of 3.75 solar masses per year [arXiv:1110.5877]. Located at an impact parameter of 25 kpc from a background quasar, its sight-line intercepts both a galactic wind and its disk [arXiv:1110.5877].

sources.summary: The paper gives J111850G1 a high SFR, low inclination, and small quasar impact parameter; wind plus disk absorption is needed because the wind-only model misses part of the profile.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:J1118 shows the wind model for J111850G1 towards SDSSJ114518.47 + 451601.4. This galaxy has SFR =3.75 , is less inclined with i 30^ , and the quasar impact parameter is b 25 kpc. As a result, this sight-line likely intercepts the wind and the disk at the same time, resulting in significant absorption at V_ sys . As a result, our model, which does not include a disk component, cannot reproduce the entire \ profile and the wind speed is less constrained. In fact, the disk model of KacprzakG_11a can reproduce the kinematics not accounted by our wind model (blue hatched).

gmw_00012803.name: J092300G1
gmw_00012803.overview: J092300G1 is an early-type galaxy characterized by a high Sérsic index of approximately 5 [arXiv:1110.5877]. It exhibits red colors and a low star formation rate of 0.02 solar masses per year [arXiv:1110.5877].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies J092300G1 as ambiguous: its low impact parameter and high inclination let the sightline intercept other galaxy regions despite a high azimuth angle.

quotes.quote: We next discuss two pairs (J092300G1, J114518G1) whose classification based on was misleading once the global aspects of the geometry are taken into account. In particular, the sight-line towards SDSSJ092300.67 + 075108.2 has the lowest impact parameter ( b=12 kpc). Figure fig:J0923 reveals that the low impact parameter combined with the high galaxy inclination implies that the sight-line can intercept the other parts of the galaxy in spite of having a high azimuth angle | |=82^ . Hence, this galaxy-quasar pair is classified as 'ambiguous'. Furthermore, the galaxy has a low SFR of 0.02 , red colors and an early type morphology (S ersic index of n 5 ).

gmw_00012804.name: W0533 3401
gmw_00012804.overview: W0533 3401 is a dust-obscured quasar host galaxy at a redshift of z=2.9 [arXiv:1910.10155]. It is a gas-rich maximum-starburst galaxy that contains a possible rotating gas disk and is undergoing rapid central black hole growth [arXiv:1910.10155].

sources.summary: The paper presents W0533 3401 as a z=2.9 dust-obscured quasar host with detected CO and continuum, high gas fraction, maximum starburst activity, and rapid SMBH growth.

quotes.quote: Our ALMA band-3 observations detect both the dust continuum and the \ line. We derive molecular gas mass, M_ gas =8.4 10^ 10 \ M_ and its fraction f_ gas =0.7 suggest that \ is gas-rich. Based on the FWHM of the \ line, we estimate the dynamical mass M_ dyn =1.6 10^ 10 \ M_ , which is generally consistent with the sum of the molecular gas mass and stellar mass. The velocity map of the \ emission line showing a velocity gradient is possibly due to a rotating gas disk.

The star formation rate (SFR) is estimated to be 3000-7000 \ M_ yr ^ 1 by using different methods. The high values of SFR and specific SFR suggest \ is a maximum-starburst. The corresponding gas depletion timescales are very short ( t_ depl 12-28 Myr). Finally, we infer the black hole mass growth rate of \ ( M _ BH = 49 M_ yr ^ 1 ), which suggests a rapid growth of the central SMBH.

gmw_00012805.name: FOCA 195
gmw_00012805.overview: FOCA 195 is a spiral galaxy belonging to an infalling Coma group with a high radial velocity [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316]. The galaxy exhibits a blue color, a normal rotation pattern, and a regular, extended gas distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316].

sources.summary: The paper places FOCA 195 in an infalling Coma group with normal extended gas, blue color, high velocity, and a normal rotation pattern.

quotes.quote: KUG 1258+287, FOCA 195 and 098 These objects are part of a group dominated by 098 (see Biviano 1996). As all the galaxies in Coma detected in outside the X-ray emission, this group shows a normal content and a regular gas distribution (Figs. 16, 18 and 20), with the more extended than the optical disk and coinciding with the optical position. Particular features of these galaxies are their systematic blue m _ UV -b color (see Table 2 and Fig. 7 in Paper I), and their high radial velocity (between 8400 and 8880 ), indicating a fast accretion of the group towards the main cluster body. Normal rotation patterns are shown by the bluest objects KUG 1258+287 and FOCA 195 (see Figs. 17 and 19).

gmw_00012806.name: IC 842
gmw_00012806.overview: IC 842 is a spiral galaxy located in the far northern region of the Coma Cluster, situated along the northeast filament of the supercluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316]. It exhibits a normal rotation pattern and normal gas content, showing no signs of interaction with the intracluster medium [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 842 as a far-northern Coma spiral in the NE filament, with normal gas content, no evident ICM interaction, and normal rotation.

quotes.quote: IC 842 and IC 4088 These galaxies lie in the far northern region of Coma, along the supercluster NE filament, and far from the X--ray emission. As expected, they display normal content and no effects of interaction with the ICM are seen (Figs. 27 and 29). They also display normal rotation patterns (Figs. 28 and 30).

gmw_00012807.name: IC 4088
gmw_00012807.overview: IC 4088 is a spiral galaxy located in the far northern region of the Coma Cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316]. It exhibits normal gas content and a normal rotation pattern, with three nearby dwarf systems detected in its vicinity [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4088 as a far-northern Coma spiral with normal gas and rotation, plus nearby H I dwarf systems without evident optical distortions.

quotes.quote: IC 842 and IC 4088 These galaxies lie in the far northern region of Coma, along the supercluster NE filament, and far from the X--ray emission. As expected, they display normal content and no effects of interaction with the ICM are seen (Figs. 27 and 29). They also display normal rotation patterns (Figs. 28 and 30). Three dwarf systems were detected in around IC 4088, one of them ( [ GMP 83 ] 1866) lies at only 2 ( 40 kpc) north of the spiral, but no optical distortions are evident. The dwarfs show velocity dispersions between 43 and 173 , and their masses range between 0.2 and 2.0 10 ^9 .

gmw_00012808.name: UGC 3574
gmw_00012808.overview: UGC 3574 is a nearby, low-inclination late-type spiral galaxy located at a distance of 21.8 Mpc [arXiv:1509.07094]. The galaxy features two faint spiral arms, with its strongest emission originating from the nuclear region and several compact, clumpy sources [arXiv:1509.07094].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 3574 as the sample's farthest nearby late-type spiral, with faint arms and clumpy/nuclear emission; despite weak emission, measurable vertical-velocity deviations appear linked to spiral arms.

quotes.quote: UGC 3574 is a nearby late-type spiral galaxy, with low inclination; at a distance of 21.8 Mpc 2004A&A...414...23J it is the furthest galaxy of the sample. Two faint spiral arms can be distinguished, while the stronger emission comes from the nuclear region and some very compact and clumpy sources at the end of the southern spiral arm or across the northern one. No specific studies for this galaxy are found in the literature, but it is included in many surveys for different purposes, e.g. 2002AJ....123.1389B who study the nuclear star clusters in late-type spiral galaxies.

In NGC 2500 and UGC 3574 there are some regions with no \ emission or too faint to be fitted, and consequently there is no V_Z associated with these regions. This last feature is more remarkable in UGC 3574, the most distant galaxy of the sample, losing therefore a global view of the vertical velocity deviations with respect to the \ emission (Figure fig5 ).

UGC 3574 is the farthest galaxy of the sample, therefore the \ emission is fainter and more difficult to measure along the slit (Figure fig5 ). Nevertheless, the vertical velocity deviations are appreciable and these seem to be linked to the spiral arms. At PA = 27^ , between 9 to 8 kpc and 1 to 2 kpc we can see how the V_Z changes across the spiral arms.

gmw_00012809.name: LH610MHzJ105407.0+573308
gmw_00012809.overview: LH610MHzJ105407.0+573308 is a high-redshift ultra-steep spectrum radio source [arXiv:1108.4037]. Mid-infrared spectroscopy indicates the galaxy has a spectroscopic redshift of 2.56, although its photometric redshift probability distributions are broad and flat between redshifts of 1 and 3 [arXiv:1108.4037].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LH610MHzJ105407.0+573308 as a high-redshift USS radio source with z_spec=2.56, while EAZY gives a catastrophic photometric-redshift outlier with broad z~1-3 solutions.

quotes.quote: There is one ``catastrophic'' outlier ( | z|/(1+z_ spec )>5 _ nmad ) for the photometric redshift of LH610MHzJ105407.0+573308 as given by EAZY ( | z|/(1+z_ spec ) = 6.3 _ nmad ). This source has the second highest spectroscopic redshift of the USS sample, z_ spec =2.56 , a value obtained from IRS mid-infrared spectroscopy. A detailed inspection of the resulting photometric redshifts probability distributions for this source shows broad and flat distributions between z 1 and z 3 , with high probability secondary solutions at z 2.3-2.4 .

gmw_00012810.name: NGC 493
gmw_00012810.overview: NGC 493 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy [arXiv:1705.03555]. It features an apparent stellar halo with faint light extending along its minor axis, and it displays no structural truncation [arXiv:1705.03555].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 493 as a highly inclined spiral with an apparent stellar halo, no truncation, about 49 percent PSF contamination, and faint minor-axis extension.

quotes.quote: Conversely, all the highly inclined ( i > 70 deg) galaxies of our sample (NGC 493, UGC 651, UGC 866, UGC 1934, and UGC 2319), feature apparent stellar haloes yet none displays a truncation.
The PSF contamination contribution, defined as [ flux(convolved) - flux(intrinsic) ] / flux(intrinsic), are 49 \ for NGC 493, 45 \ for UGC 2428 and 47 \ for IC 1515.

But NGC 493 should serve as a good example, see Fig. fig:PPRhalo . The fainter surface brightnesses indeed extend further out along the minor axis, indicating the the faint light has a more or less spherical distribution but stretched in the production of the polar plots, as expected for a stellar halo (but also for the case of scattered light).

gmw_00012811.name: IC 1515
gmw_00012811.overview: IC 1515 is a low-inclination galaxy that has been used as a test case for surface photometry [arXiv:1705.03555]. At faint levels, the galaxy exhibits some lopsidedness, and its outer profile features include detected breaks, haloes, and truncations [arXiv:1705.03555].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 1515 as a low-inclination surface-photometry test case, noting faint lopsidedness, detected outer-profile features, and a modeled PSF contamination contribution of 47 percent.

quotes.quote: IC 1515 shows some lopsidedness at faint levels (larger extent in angles at the left) that has been rectified in the process.

IC 1515, IC 1516, and NGC 799 have the lowest inclinations of the sample. Between the three of them, breaks, haloes, and truncations have all been detected, explicitly showing the feasibility of detecting these features in face-on galaxies.
The PSF contamination contribution, defined as [ flux(convolved) - flux(intrinsic) ] / flux(intrinsic), are 49 \ for NGC 493, 45 \ for UGC 2428 and 47 \ for IC 1515.

gmw_00012812.name: The Wide-Angle-Tail radio galaxy in the cluster Abell 1033
aliases_local: The Wide-Angle-Tail radio galaxy in the cluster Abell 1033
gmw_00012812.overview: The Wide-Angle-Tail radio galaxy is located in the merging galaxy cluster Abell 1033 [arXiv:2401.05595]. Its integrated radio spectrum exhibits extreme curvature, and its northern radio tail features two distinct bends before becoming straight [arXiv:2401.05595].

sources.original_name: The Wide-Angle-Tail radio galaxy in the cluster Abell 1033
sources.summary: The paper highlights this WAT as a radio galaxy in merging cluster Abell 1033 with an extremely curved spectrum and tail bends coincident with spectral-index reversals.

quotes.quote: The Wide-Angle-Tail radio galaxy in the cluster Abell 1033: A crucial new catch The radio galaxy we now discuss is a Wide-Angle-Tail (WAT) associated with a merging galaxy cluster Abell 1033 at z = 0.126.
Its integrated radio spectrum exhibits extreme curvature, with _ 144-323 MHz 4 and _ 54-144 MHz 2 .

On the LOFAR 144 MHz image of this WAT (Fig. \ 2 of Elder et al. 2022, reproduced in Fig. \ 1 here), the brighter northern radio tail is traceable out to 700 kpc from the parent galaxy. A ridge-line drawn through this radio tail clearly shows two bends (in opposite directions). Note that these bends occur within the first half of the tail, at close to 200 kpc and 375 kpc distances from the nucleus of the WAT. Thereafter, the tail becomes straight.

gmw_00012813.name: Object 15
gmw_00012813.overview: Object 15 is a candidate type 2, heavily absorbed quasar at a high redshift [arXiv:astro-ph/0006233]. Its optical and near-infrared colors match those of a young galaxy, while its optical morphology appears fuzzy and possibly interacting [arXiv:astro-ph/0006233].

sources.summary: Object 15 is the only SSA13 hard X-ray source with an 850 m counterpart; its colors, HST morphology, luminosity, and optical/near-IR properties suggest a young high-redshift type 2 QSO.

quotes.quote: None of the 11 hard X-ray (2--10 keV) sources has a counterpart at 850 m, with the exception of a Chandra source in the SSA13 field, which is a candidate type 2, heavily absorbed QSO at high redshift.

Only one ( 15) out of these three sources has a counterpart at 850 m: it has not been spectroscopically identified, but its colours match those of a young galaxy at z 1.9, with significant probability at z 2.6--2.8 as well. This is also consistent with the HST optical image (fuzzy and possibly interacting).

At this redshift, the observed flux implies a luminosity of L_ 2-10keV 1-2 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 (or intrinsically higher if absorbed) We assume H_0=50 and q_0=0.5 . , which is not extreme, but in the QSO range. Although no information is available on its X-ray spectral slope, the optical to near-IR properties (see above) and, as we will see, the _ SX suggest that this is a type 2 QSO.

gmw_00012814.name: IRAS 11582+3020
gmw_00012814.overview: IRAS 11582+3020 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy classified as a LINER [arXiv:0709.1172]. It exhibits a nuclear starburst disk, a superwind outflow, and diffuse tidal tails likely originating from a recent merger [arXiv:0709.1172].

sources.summary: IRAS 11582+3020 is described as an ultra-luminous LINER with high SFR, superwind evidence, diffuse tidal features, CO components in a nuclear starburst disk and extended post-merger disk.

quotes.quote: We mapped with the Plateau de Bure interferometer one of the best-detected galaxies, IRAS 11582+3020. It is an ultra-luminous galaxy with L(IR)= 5.4 10 ^ 12 L _ , classified as a LINER by Kim et al. (1998). Rupke et al. (2005) find evidence of a superwind outflow in this galaxy of about 15 M_ yr^ 1 , while its SFR is estimated at 740 M_ yr^ 1 from the infrared luminosity. The red image of Kim et al. (2002) reveals some extended diffuse emission, which could be diluted tidal tails, from a recent interaction, while two galaxies of the same group are within 90 kpc in projection (Fig 3).

In summary, the CO emission in IRAS 11582+3020 suggests the presence of two components: a nuclear disk 1.5kpc in radius with a nuclear starburst (as usual in local starbursting galaxies, where disks of radii= 300-800pc are found, Downes Solomon 1998); and a more extended disk up to 20-30kpc diameter, which would correspond to the optical merger morphology. The physical properties of IRAS 11582+3020 (size, SFE) appear to be intermediate between those of local ULIRGs and high-z submillimeter galaxies (SMG) as mapped by Tacconi et al. (2006). There might be a trend, also followed by IRAS 11582+3020, for high-z galaxies to have a higher L(FIR)/M(H _2 ) ratio (Riechers et al. 2006), however this must be confirmed with larger samples.

gmw_00012815.name: Galaxy 24626
gmw_00012815.overview: Galaxy 24626 is a massive galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of z=1.31 [arXiv:1101.0843]. It features a highly concentrated light profile and a well-defined red-centered color gradient, with radial gradients of dust obscuration and stellar age that differ significantly from other sample galaxies [arXiv:1101.0843].

sources.summary: Galaxy 24626 has the clearest red-centered color gradient; its large concentrated profile, stellar mass, and unusual dust and age gradients suggest a distinct star-formation or assembly history.

quotes.quote: The last galaxy, 24626, resides just below the pBzK selection window in the (B-z) vs.(z-K) color--color plane, most likely because its spectroscopic redshift, z=1.31 , is outside of the targeted range of the pBzK criterion.

Galaxy 24626 has the most well-defined red center and the clearest color gradient. Galaxy 23555 and 24279 also have well--defined red centers, but their outskirts are observed at relatively lower S/Ns and the resulting color gradient is not as clear as that of Galaxy 24626.

Galaxy 24626 is excluded from the average, because, as Figure fig:covar shows, its gradients of dust obscuration and age are very different from those of the other galaxies. Its half--light radius and index, R_ eff 3.7 kpc and index n=7.4 see cassata10 , the largest size and most concentrated light profile in the sample, as well as its stellar mass, Log(M/M_ ) =11.1 , are typical of the bright elliptical galaxies in the local universe often observed in groups with estimated total (dark matter) mass M 10^ 13 M_ ycguo09cen . Thus, it is likely that the star--formation and/or stellar--mass assembly history of this galaxy considerably differ from those of the other five samples galaxies, a fact that might reflect in the radial gradients of its stellar population properties.

gmw_00012816.name: Galaxy 23495
gmw_00012816.overview: Galaxy 23495 is identified with an X-ray counterpart in the Chandra Deep Field South [arXiv:1101.0843]. The galaxy is not detected in VLA radio maps, but it exhibits measured luminosities in both the soft and hard X-ray bands [arXiv:1101.0843].

sources.summary: Galaxy 23495 is identified with a Chandra Deep Field South counterpart, has measured soft and hard X-ray luminosities, and is not detected in VLA radio maps.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 23495 has a counterpart in the Chandra Deep Field South 2--Megasecond catalog luo08 . It has X-ray luminosity 3.8 10^ 43 erg/s and 5.6 10^ 43 erg/s in the soft and hard band, respectively. It is not, however, detected in the VLA map by kellermann08 and miller08 .

gmw_00012817.name: AK03
gmw_00012817.overview: AK03 is a high-redshift galaxy system consisting of two main ultraviolet components, AK03-N and AK03-S, which are connected by a possible bridge [arXiv:1802.07751]. This structure suggests a galaxy merger, with the bulk of the star formation and compact dust emission concentrated in AK03-S [arXiv:1802.07751].

sources.summary: AK03 has two UV components connected by a possible bridge; AK03-S has comparable photometric redshift, compact dust emission, and likely contains the bulk star formation in a merger.

quotes.quote: AK03 : This system has two main UV components separated by 1 (AK03-N and AK03-S), with the F125W image suggesting a bridge connecting the two at an integrated S/N = 2.4 . The spectroscopic confirmation refers to AK03-N, but AK03-S has a comparable photometric redshift see Section subsec:mstar_cav smolcic15 . All these may be considered evidence for a merger. The dust continuum emission is associated with AK03-S and shows two very compact emission peaks (unresolved at the current resolution), whereas AK03-N remains undetected. Therefore, the bulk of the star formation is associated with AK03-S.

gmw_00012818.name: UDF5
gmw_00012818.overview: UDF5 is a dusty star-forming galaxy with a clumpy and disturbed rest-frame optical morphology [arXiv:2108.05596]. This morphology is attributed to gas compaction and dust-enshrouded star formation occurring within a compact central region [arXiv:2108.05596].

sources.summary: The paper describes UDF5 as a DSFG with compact radio/millimeter star-forming sizes, disturbed optical morphology attributed to dusty star formation and gas compaction, and main-sequence SED properties.

quotes.quote: UDF5 (J033236.94-274726.8) UDF5 is a DSFG detected both in the B6 ( 1.3 mm) ALMA and 6 GHz VLA maps of the HUDF by Dunlop2017:2017MNRAS.466..861D and Rujopakarn2016:2016ApJ...833...12R .

Both ALMA and VLA sizes can provide a good measure of the region where the bulk of star formation is occurring, i.e. very compact, 2 kpc _ ALMA <2.5 kpc; VLA 1.5 kpc; see Tab. tab:continuum and Rujopakarn2016:2016ApJ...833...12R . Rujopakarn2016:2016ApJ...833...12R claim that the 6 GHz VLA flux is solely compatible with the star formation activity of the galaxy, confirming the result by Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P . The HST/WFC3 H _ 160 circularized radius of UDF5 is _ H 2.3 kpc, slightly larger than the radio one and consistent with the upper limit on the ALMA B6 size.

The rest-frame optical morphology is clumpy/disturbed (Fig. fig:cont and Tab. tab:summary_resolved ). Since no clear evidence of interactions and/or AGN feedback has been observed, we ascribe the galaxy multi-band morphology to both the dust enshrouded star formation occurring at radii 2 kpc and the gas compaction towards the central region. From the analysis by Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P , UDF5 is in the vicinity of the main-sequence at the corresponding redshift.

sources.summary: The paper treats UDF0702 as one of four EROs whose classification disagrees by method: most diagnostics call it dusty/star-forming, while the color-color criterion places it near the old-galaxy side, likely because it is faint.

quotes.quote: There are 4 EROs (UDF0295, UDF0565, UDF0629 and UDF0702) which are classified as DGs by the SED fitting method, but are located at the left-hand side of the thick dot-dashed line. These galaxies are all close to the dividing line and very faint in K-band, the photometric uncertainty of those faint EROs may cause this discrepancy.

For 4 EROs (UDF0295, UDF0565, UDF0629 and UDF0702) in our sample, which are classified as DGs by the other 3 methods, but are located at the left-hand side of the discriminating line, and classified as OGs by the (i-K) versus (J-K) method. Considering these EROs are close to the dividing line and faint, the photometric uncertainty of them may cause this discrepancy. We classified them as OGs.

gmw_00012819.name: UDF10
gmw_00012819.overview: UDF10 is a dusty star-forming galaxy at a redshift of 2.086 [arXiv:2108.05596]. It is classified as a normal galaxy that lies on the redshift-two main sequence, with a compact optical radius of 2.0 kiloparsecs and an unresolved ALMA size under 2.5 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2108.05596].

sources.summary: The paper describes UDF10 as a z=2.086 DSFG with compact ALMA and optical sizes, a Chandra source classified as normal, and SED evidence placing it on the z~2 main sequence.

quotes.quote: UDF10 (J033240.73-274749.4) UDF10 is a DSFG at z _ spec =2.086 grism redshift by based on the detection of an optical rest-frame line in the 3D-HST data; Tab. tab:galaxy_properties Momcheva2016:2016ApJS..225...27M .

The source is not resolved in the ALMA map, but its size must be <2.5 kpc (Tab. tab:continuum ). After the astrometric corrections, the ALMA centroid emission appears to be shifted of 10 mas from the optical peak Fig. fig:cont ; see also Dunlop2017:2017MNRAS.466..861D . UDF10 optical radius is _ H 2.0 kpc (Tab. tab:galaxy_properties ), that is comparable with the upper limit on ALMA size. UDF10 is detected with Chandra (IDX 756) in the 7 Ms CDFS catalog by Luo2017:2017ApJS..228....2L .

The source 2-10 keV luminosity is then 6 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 . Luo2017:2017ApJS..228....2L classified the source to be a normal galaxy (Tab. tab:galaxy_properties ). Putting together these evidences with the outcomes from the galaxy SED analysis presented in Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P , UDF10 is a z 2 main-sequence galaxy, since it overlaps the corresponding relation by Speagle2014:2014ApJS..214...15S .

gmw_00012820.name: BX 389
gmw_00012820.overview: BX 389 is a high-redshift, bulge-dominated star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1010.4376]. The galaxy features a weak outer ring or disk structure and exhibits highly turbulent velocities, with the majority of its star formation concentrated within its central bulge [arXiv:1010.4376].

sources.summary: The paper models BX 389 as a high-redshift, bulge-dominated star-forming galaxy with a weak ring or disk, high turbulence, and most star formation concentrated in the bulge.

quotes.quote: BX 482 and BX 389 are selected for applications, whose dynamical structure and emission lines are presented by Genzel et al. (2008) and Lehnert et al. (2009), respectively. BX 482 is rotating star-forming outer ring/disk dominated galaxy while BX 389 is central bulge/inner disk dominated (Genzel et al. 2008). On the other hand, they are located in the star forming area in the BPT diagram (Figure 9 and 10 in Lehnert et al. 2009). This sets up additional constraints on the details of both dynamical structure and emission of the two galaxies.

As another illustration, we apply the present model to BX 389, which is a bulge-dominated star forming galaxy with a weak ring or disk at a scale of R_ ring =4.4 0.5 kpc and a turbulence velocity V_ tur =180 km s ^ 1 . This object is very different from BX 482. The total star formation rates are 150 75 , of which 100 50 in the bulge and 50 25 in the ring.

gmw_00012821.name: Sausage 5
gmw_00012821.overview: Sausage 5 is classified as a star-forming spiral galaxy [arXiv:2011.13935]. Its ionized nebular gas exhibits an asymmetric rotation pattern with detected tails and spurs, as well as an emission peak offset to the north [arXiv:2011.13935].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Sausage 5 as a star-forming spiral, finding IFU emission consistent with photoionization plus an offset, asymmetric ionized-gas pattern with detected tails and spurs.

quotes.quote: Sausage 5, 6, 7 are firmly classified as SF spiral galaxies based on line ratios in their 1D spectra Sobral2015 . Further evidence to support this scenario comes from the IFU data, where the emission is consistent with photoionization throughout the galaxies, considering the small ratios between the doublet (which in many cases is not detected) and Kewley2006 .

In Sausage 5, the peak of the emission is offset in the north direction by about 0.2^ ( 0.6 kpc). The ionized nebular gas in Sausage 5 has an asymmetric rotation pattern with higher amplitude in velocity on the approaching side ( 100 with 7--8 uncertainty per pixel) and velocities of up to 70 23 at the northern tip, an offset flux peak towards the north-west from the stellar disk. The tails and spurs of ionized gas are detected at S/N 4--10 per pixel across the features.

gmw_00012822.name: Sausage 6
gmw_00012822.overview: Sausage 6 is classified as a star-forming spiral galaxy [arXiv:2011.13935]. Its ionized emission peak is offset northwest from the stellar emission peak in a region of metal-poor gas, which is followed by a metal-rich spur [arXiv:2011.13935].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Sausage 6 as a star-forming spiral and finds its ionized emission peak offset northwest in metal-poor gas, followed by a metal-rich spur.

quotes.quote: Sausage 5, 6, 7 are firmly classified as SF spiral galaxies based on line ratios in their 1D spectra Sobral2015 . Further evidence to support this scenario comes from the IFU data, where the emission is consistent with photoionization throughout the galaxies, considering the small ratios between the doublet (which in many cases is not detected) and Kewley2006 .

For example, in Sausage 6, the emission peak, embedded in a region of metal-poor gas ( 8.5 0.05 per pixel) is offset 0.6^ ( 1.9 kpc) north-west from the peak of the stellar emission, followed by a spur of extremely metal-rich gas ( 9.0 0.05 per pixel) towards the north-west of the galaxy (offset 3.7^ west and 1.2^ north from the stellar disk center).

gmw_00012823.name: Sausage 7
gmw_00012823.overview: Sausage 7 is classified as a star-forming spiral galaxy [arXiv:2011.13935]. Its clumpy, bow-like ionized gas distribution is offset from the stellar disk, which features a bright nucleus and a well-defined spiral structure [arXiv:2011.13935].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Sausage 7 as a star-forming spiral whose clumpy bow-like ionized gas is offset from the stellar disk and has a strong metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: Sausage 5, 6, 7 are firmly classified as SF spiral galaxies based on line ratios in their 1D spectra Sobral2015 . Further evidence to support this scenario comes from the IFU data, where the emission is consistent with photoionization throughout the galaxies, considering the small ratios between the doublet (which in many cases is not detected) and Kewley2006 .

In Sausage 7, the ionized gas maps look remarkably different from the stellar distribution. The bright nucleus surrounded by a well-defined spiral structure is not reflected in the complex, clumpy gas, whose bow-like distribution is offset north from the stellar disk. The peak of the emission is also offset 0.5^ ( 1.6 kpc) from the peak of the stellar light. The metallicity has a strong 0.2 dex gradient along the disk of the galaxy, with elevated values on the side closest to the northern cluster-scale shock front.

gmw_00012824.name: Sausage 9
gmw_00012824.overview: Sausage 9 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy featuring a bright nucleus dominated by active galactic nucleus emission [arXiv:2011.13935]. The galaxy exhibits fast rotational velocities, large gas dispersions, and a spiral arm pattern powered by star formation [arXiv:2011.13935].

sources.summary: The paper describes Sausage 9 as a Seyfert 1 with AGN-dominated broad emission, fast rotation, large gas dispersion, spiral-arm star formation, and an offset emission peak.

quotes.quote: While the emission line budget in both Sausage 8 and 9 is dominated by AGN contribution Sobral2015 , the resolved IFU observations reveal a more complex picture. With the fastest rotational velocities but also the largest gas dispersions of the sample (over 400 ), Sausage 9 is a classical Seyfert 1 type source, with a bright nucleus dominated by AGN emission, broad emission lines and a pronounced spiral arm pattern powered by SF, recovered in both the i band and the ionized gas maps. The peak of emission is offset south in Sausage 9, by 0.9 kpc with respect to the optical nucleus.

gmw_00012825.name: ID 3
gmw_00012825.overview: ID 3 is a radio-loud quasar characterized by a large reservoir of molecular gas [arXiv:2201.11660]. It is surrounded by a low-surface-brightness Lyman-alpha nebula that exhibits highly active kinematics, likely indicating that small-scale radio feedback is affecting the surrounding gas [arXiv:2201.11660].

sources.summary: The paper detects [C I] emission in ID 3/J 0525-233 and describes it as radio-loud, molecule-rich, and surrounded by a low-surface-brightness Ly nebula with active kinematics.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of the quasar with ID 3 (or J 0525-233) shows the [ C i ] (2-1) emission line (at 196.8 GHz) and a S/N = 1.4 bump (binning of 300 km s ^ 1 ) at the expected location of CO(7-6) (196.2 GHz), which we estimate would contribute 20 of the [ C i ] (2-1) flux when using the 300 km s ^ 1 binning.

The quasar with ID 3 is radio-loud, characterized by a large reservoir of molecular gas and has one of the Ly nebulae with the lowest surface brightness, extending out to a maximum of 70 projected kpc ( 2019arrigoni ). Its integrated Ly emission shows the most active kinematics of the sample ( FWHM_ Ly = 1542 196 \ km \ s^ 1 ), which together with the observed large Ly shift likely indicates that small-scale radio feedback is affecting the surrounding gas distribution in this system.

gmw_00012826.name: ID 8
gmw_00012826.overview: ID 8 is a quasar host galaxy surrounded by a Lyman-alpha nebula that extends out to 145 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2201.11660]. The system exhibits CO(6-5) emission, an average molecular reservoir, and a blueshifted velocity that may indicate cool-gas inflow [arXiv:2201.11660].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(6-5) in ID 8/UM683, notes a possibly spurious [C I] feature, and links its Ly blueshift and intermediate nebula to possible cool-gas inflow.

quotes.quote: From the spectra shown in Fig. fig:co65spec , we found CO(6-5) emission line detections (represented by a green shaded area) for three sources: quasars with IDs 8, 43, and 50. Two of these detections are closer to the redshift of the Ly nebula (IDs 43 and 50; purple vertical line) than the uncertain systemic redshift computed from C iv (orange vertical line). They present fluxes of 3.4 I_ CO(6-5) <5.1 Jy km s ^ 1 .

The quasar with ID 8 (or UM683) has a feature at 196.1 GHz (or -328 km s ^ 1 , integrated S/N = 3.1, I_ line 3 Jy km s ^ 1 , which could be a spurious line given the results of the jack-knife test for this spectrum (see Appendix app:spurious ).

The observed blueshift for the quasars with ID 8 (or UM683) and ID 50 (or SDSS J0819+0823) is comparable with the inflow velocities expected for the cool gas in quasar host halos. The quasar with ID 8 has an average molecular reservoir and a Ly nebula with intermediate surface brightness, extending out to 145 kpc ( 2019arrigoni .)

sources.summary: Q0334 204 is treated with two other AAL quasars; their absorbers show 1900-3000 km/s blueshifted outflows, no partial covering, large inferred distances, and uncertain feedback relevance.

quotes.quote: The AALs in the other three quasars in our sample, Q0105+061, Q0334 204, and Q2044 168, have velocity blueshifts consistent with outflows at speeds of 1900 to 3000 . These systems do not exhibit partial covering of the background light source, and the upper limits we derive on their densities, 150 to 15 , place the absorbers at large distances from the quasars, namely, 40 to 330 kpc. Thus the relationship of the AAL systems to the quasars is not known. It is possible that they form in intervening gas that is not physically related to the quasars, such as galactic halos in the same galaxy cluster or group as the quasar host galaxies.

We conclude that the AAL systems in these three quasars are likely to form in quasar-driven outflows, but their energies and potential for feedback to the host galaxies cannot be determined from existing data.

gmw_00012827.name: ID 29
gmw_00012827.overview: ID 29 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits the brightest carbon-one line detection in its sample [arXiv:2201.11660]. This emission features a unique double-peaked line split into two components, which may indicate a rotating disk or a merger between two molecular gas reservoirs [arXiv:2201.11660].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 29/Q-0115-30 as the brightest [C I] detection, with a unique double-peaked line split into two Gaussian components that may trace a rotating disk or merger.

quotes.quote: Quasar ID 29, with a total I_ C i (2-1) =15.7 3.7 Jy km s ^ 1 , is therefore the brightest [ C i ] (2-1) detection in this sample. Furthermore, it is 3 times brighter than the lensed Cloverleaf quasar ( I_ C i (2-1) 5.2 Jy km s ^ 1 , 2003weiss ), which is as far as we know the brightest [ C i ] (2-1) z 3 quasar detected in the literature. We further discuss the nature of this double-peak emission of quasar with ID 29 (or Q-0115-30) in Section dis:q0115 .

In Section sec:detections , we showed that the quasar with ID 29 (or Q-0115-30) has a [ C i ] (2-1) line emission consisting of two Gaussian components separated by 800 km s ^ 1 . While this line identification needs further confirmation, in this section we discuss their possible origins. This double-peaked feature is unique within our sample and could be produced by a rotating disk or a merger between two molecular gas reservoirs e.g., 2003neri,2006narayanan,2008greve,2011polletta .

gmw_00012828.name: ID 39
gmw_00012828.overview: ID 39 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits [C I] (2-1) emission line detections [arXiv:2201.11660]. The quasar is surrounded by a Lyman-alpha nebula extending out to 50 projected kiloparsecs, which displays a modest blueshift that may be caused by projection effects or turbulent motions [arXiv:2201.11660].

sources.summary: The paper detects [C I] in ID 39/SDSS J0100+2105 and describes a less extended Ly nebula whose modest blueshift may reflect projection or turbulent motions.

quotes.quote: From the spectra in Fig. fig:co76spec , we found [ C i ] (2-1) emission line detections in quasars with IDs 3, 29, and 39. They have fluxes of 2.3 I_ C i (2-1) 15.7 Jy km s ^ 1 .

The quasar with ID 39 (or SDSS J0100+2105) is surrounded by one of the less extended Ly nebulae in our sample (see Fig. fig:masseslya ; Ly extends out to 50 projected kpc). The observed blueshift for this source is lower than the inflow velocities ( v_ in 0.9 v_ vir 320-460 km s ^ 1 ; 2015goerdt ) expected for the cool gas in quasar host halos ( 10^ 12 10^ 13 M _ , e.g., 2012white,2018timlin and references therein). This could be due to projection effects or to the presence of other violent motions (e.g. winds, turbulences) that could wash out the inflow signature.

gmw_00012829.name: ID 43
gmw_00012829.overview: ID 43 is a high-redshift quasar that hosts an average molecular reservoir, as evidenced by the detection of CO(6-5) emission [arXiv:2201.11660]. The system is surrounded by an asymmetric Lyman-alpha nebula extending 118 kpc, with kinematics and line shifts that suggest the presence of outflowing gas [arXiv:2201.11660].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(6-5) in ID 43/CTSH22.05 and interprets its active, asymmetric Ly nebula and large positive Ly shift as possible outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: From the spectra shown in Fig. fig:co65spec , we found CO(6-5) emission line detections (represented by a green shaded area) for three sources: quasars with IDs 8, 43, and 50. Two of these detections are closer to the redshift of the Ly nebula (IDs 43 and 50; purple vertical line) than the uncertain systemic redshift computed from C iv (orange vertical line). They present fluxes of 3.4 I_ CO(6-5) <5.1 Jy km s ^ 1 .

The quasar with ID 43 has an average molecular reservoir and a Ly nebula with intermediate surface brightness, extending out to 118 kpc ( 2019arrigoni ). Its radio nature is not clear because there are no radio observations of this target in the literature. Its relatively active Ly kinematics ( FWHM_ Ly = 1068 142 km \ s^ 1 ), together with the large observed positive shift of the Ly line with respect to CO(6-5) and the asymmetric morphology of the nebula with a northern bright region, might all be hints of outflowing gas in this system.

gmw_00012830.name: ID 50
gmw_00012830.overview: ID 50 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits CO(6-5) molecular emission [arXiv:2201.11660]. It is surrounded by a bright, asymmetric, and extended Lyman-alpha nebula that spans 130 projected kiloparsecs [arXiv:2201.11660].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(6-5) in ID 50/SDSS J0819+0823 and finds a blueshifted, bright, asymmetric, extended Ly nebula consistent with massive-halo motions and turbulence.

quotes.quote: From the spectra shown in Fig. fig:co65spec , we found CO(6-5) emission line detections (represented by a green shaded area) for three sources: quasars with IDs 8, 43, and 50. Two of these detections are closer to the redshift of the Ly nebula (IDs 43 and 50; purple vertical line) than the uncertain systemic redshift computed from C iv (orange vertical line). They present fluxes of 3.4 I_ CO(6-5) <5.1 Jy km s ^ 1 .

The observed blueshift for the quasars with ID 8 (or UM683) and ID 50 (or SDSS J0819+0823) is comparable with the inflow velocities expected for the cool gas in quasar host halos. The quasar with ID 8 has an average molecular reservoir and a Ly nebula with intermediate surface brightness, extending out to 145 kpc ( 2019arrigoni .) Interestingly, ID 50 is surrounded by one of the brightest and more extended (in area) Ly nebulae in the QSO museum sample (see Fig. fig:masseslya ; Ly extends out to 130 projected kpc), with a clearly asymmetric morphology. The widths of their integrated Ly lines, FWHM _ Ly = 915 46 km s ^ 1 and FWHM _ Ly = 967 38 km s ^ 1 (for IDs 8 and 50, respectively), may therefore be explained by gravitational motions in such massive halos ( FWHM _ 1D ^ DM 580-870 km s ^ 1 ; 2019arrigoni ) and by the presence of large turbulence within the cool gas reservoir.

gmw_00012831.name: B2 1059+29
gmw_00012831.overview: B2 1059+29 is a radio galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.106 that exhibits double-peaked [O III] lines [arXiv:2402.12521]. Its host galaxy displays tidal features indicative of a past merger event, while its compact radio structure closely resembles a compact symmetric object [arXiv:2402.12521].

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 1059+29 as a double-peaked, merger-marked radio galaxy with changing jet direction and VLBA compact components that may be jet-related or a CSO.

quotes.quote: B2 1059+29 is at a redshift of z = 0.106. The SDSS spectrum of the source shows double-peaked [ O III ] lines. The Legacy Survey image of B2 1059+29 shows tidal features suggesting a past merger event. The source appears to possess a rotational symmetry in the low-resolution images. The detailed structure of the source is conspicuous in our higher-resolution images.
