This source shows two close, faint, compact components in the VLBA image (see Figure vlba ). The central point-source spectrum estimated using our uGMRT images at 400 MHz and 1250 MHz is steep ( 0.8 ), diminishing the likelihood of the BBH scenario. Since the central point-source spectrum is steep, the extended structure seen in the VLBA image might likely be jet-related, and the source morphology closely resembles a compact symmetric object (CSO).

gmw_00012832.name: J1430+5217
gmw_00012832.overview: J1430+5217 is a quasar characterized by double-peaked emission lines [arXiv:2402.12521]. Its radio structure features east and west lobes, north and south wings, and precessing jets that reproduce its parsec-scale jet angle [arXiv:2402.12521].

sources.summary: The paper models J1430+5217 emission lines as double-peaked and fits its quasar radio structure with precessing jets that reproduce the E/W lobes and parsec-scale jet angle.

quotes.quote: BADASS3 treats multiple line components as outflows, and hence, we model the emission lines as a combination of narrow-line and outflow components. We assume Gaussian profiles for both these components. Figure fig:J1430+5217 shows the raw spectra, the model, and individual components, including the stellar continuum and the multiple Gaussian narrow emission lines. Even though we modeled the emission lines using a narrow + outflowing component, neither of these components seems to be centered around the systemic velocity of the host galaxy, pointing to the existence of double-peaked emission lines. We have tabulated the results of this emission line fitting required for the analysis in this paper in Table tab:BH-mass-estimates-nelson .

The upper-left panel of fig:precmodbest shows the brightness distribution at 1.4 GHz of the quasar J1430+5217. The brighter regions were labeled as east (E) and west (W) lobes, while more diffuse and extended regions were labeled as north (N) and south (S) wings Lal2019 . Moreover, the northeast (NE) jet of J1430+5217 is shorter in length than the southwest (SW) jet, even though the former is brighter, in contradiction with the usual behavior of the jet being more extended and brighter than the counter-jet. Precession helices generated from the set of parameters listed in tab:PrecParam are superposed on the 1.4 GHz map of J1430+5217, with the jet pointing towards the NE-direction. This precession model recovers quite well the position of the E and W lobes, as well as the nowadays position angle of its parsec-scale jet seen in vlba ( 30 , measured from N to E direction).

gmw_00012833.name: GAMA J224500.05-343031.7
gmw_00012833.overview: GAMA J224500.05-343031.7 is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.3562 [arXiv:2004.00847]. It exhibits characteristic absorption lines with no detected emission lines, and it may host an extended neutral hydrogen disc [arXiv:2004.00847].

sources.summary: The paper detects H I absorption in GAMA J224500.05-343031.7, identifies it as an early-type galaxy at z=0.3562, and suggests the absorption may trace an extended H I disc.

quotes.quote: Using ASKAP we have detected H i absorption at z = 0.3562 in the galaxy GAMA J224500.05 343031.7. In figure:J224500-343030_rgb_image we show a three-colour optical image of this galaxy using gri band images from Data Release 4 of the VST Kilo-Degree Survey (KiDS; Kuijken:2019 ). The catalogued position of the radio source NVSS J224500 343030 is offset from the centre of the galaxy by 2.6 arcsec, which is significant when compared with the uncertainty due to the noise ( 26 mas) and calibration in NVSS ( 0.5 arcsec; Condon:1998 ), and the known astrometric error of the VLA with respect to optical surveys ( 20 mas; e.g. Helfand:2015 ).

This re-reduced spectrum was then independently redshifted using the AutoZ code ( Baldry:2014 ), and a high confidence redshift confirmed at z = 0.3562 . It is characteristic of an early-type galaxy, exhibiting a 4000 break, Na, Mg, Ca ii H and K absorption lines, and no detected emission lines.

If the absorber detected in GAMA J224500.05-343031.7 does form part of a larger H i disc, then the H i size-mass relation (see e.g. Wang:2016 and references therein) would imply a total H i mass of M_ HI 3 10^ 9 M _ . Massive and extended H i discs are rare in early type galaxies, but have been seen in the nearby Universe ( Serra:2011 ). We may therefore be seeing 21-cm absorption through such an H i disc in GAMA J224500.05 343031.7.

gmw_00012834.name: SPT0155-62
gmw_00012834.overview: SPT0155-62 is an astronomical source characterized by blended CO(7-6) and [CI](2-1) emission lines [arXiv:2306.03153]. These blended lines can be resolved using a multiple-Gaussian fit to estimate their individual flux fractions [arXiv:2306.03153].

sources.summary: The paper treats SPT0155-62 as a blended CO(7-6)/[CI](2-1) source, deblending it with four Gaussian components and finding line fluxes consistent with moment-map estimates.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:ACA CI21 spectra , the spectra of sources SPT0155-62 and SPT2037-65 have blended CO(7-6) and [ CI ] (2-1) emission. We use a multiple-Gaussian fit in-order to estimate the fraction of flux in the blended region. For SPT0155-62, we fit 4 Gaussians with 6 free parameters. We use for amplitudes, 2 central velocities and 2 line widths, thereby forcing the same width for each of these lines. We thus use the combined width of these two components for each of these lines as the integration window for the moment-0 maps.
The line fluxes of CO(7-6) and [ CI ] (2-1) estimated by this method are 12.1 1.9 and 8.9 1.6 Jy km/s. The difference between these fluxes and that estimated by the moment-0 maps is < 1 .

gmw_00012835.name: SPT2037-65
gmw_00012835.overview: The galaxy SPT2037-65 exhibits blended CO(7-6) and [CI](2-1) emission lines [arXiv:2306.03153]. Multi-Gaussian modeling of this blended region estimates a CO(7-6) flux of 9.1 Jy km/s and a [CI](2-1) flux of 7.1 Jy km/s [arXiv:2306.03153].

sources.summary: The paper identifies blended CO(7-6) and [CI](2-1) emission in SPT2037-65, applies the same multi-Gaussian deblending model, and finds fluxes consistent with moment-map estimates.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:ACA CI21 spectra , the spectra of sources SPT0155-62 and SPT2037-65 have blended CO(7-6) and [ CI ] (2-1) emission. We use a multiple-Gaussian fit in-order to estimate the fraction of flux in the blended region.

We use the same model for SPT2037-65 and estimate CO(7-6) flux as 9.1 1.7 Jy km/s and [ CI ] (2-1) as 7.1 1.4 Jy km/s. The difference between the moment map estimated flux is < 1.2 for this source. Hence we proceed to use the fluxes estimated by the moment-0 map for uniformity.

gmw_00012836.name: WISE 0145-1327
gmw_00012836.overview: WISE 0145-1327 is a chance projection of two distinct quasars [arXiv:1607.07476]. The brighter quasar in the pair is located at a redshift of z=1.0902, while the fainter companion lies at a higher redshift of z=1.9749 [arXiv:1607.07476].

sources.summary: The paper classifies WISE 0145-1327 as a chance projection of two quasars, with the brighter MgII source at z=1.0902 and the fainter CIV source at z=1.9749.

quotes.quote: WISE 0145-1327 is a chance projection of two quasars. The brighter of the pair is at a redshift of z=1.0902 0.0005 from a Gaussian fit to the MgII emission line. The fainter object is at a higher redshift of z = 1.9749 0.0005 based on its CIV broad emission line. The spectra are shown in Figure 5.

gmw_00012837.name: WISE 0326-3122
gmw_00012837.overview: WISE 0326-3122 consists of two quasar images with nearly identical redshifts of approximately z = 1.33 [arXiv:1607.07476]. These lensless twins exhibit a constant spectral flux ratio, and the brighter component's spectrum displays a magnesium II absorption doublet [arXiv:1607.07476].

sources.summary: The paper calls WISE 0326-3122 lensless twins: two quasar images with nearly identical redshifts, constant spectral flux ratio, an absorption doublet, and no detected lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: The two quasar images of WISE 0326-3122 have nearly identical redshifts and spectral flux ratios. Both show a CIII ] broad emission line at around 4460 . Gaussian fits to the CIII ] profiles yield identical source redshifts of z = 1.3342 0.0016 for the brighter object and z=1.3336 0.0026 for the fainter object. This redshift places the MgII broad emission line inside the IMACS chip gap with only a hint of the feature's wing visible for the brighter component.

There is also a MgII 2796,2803 absorption doublet at z = 0.5080 0.0001 present in the brighter component's spectrum. The flux ratio between components is also remarkably constant at about 3.2:1 (0.5 dex in Figure 5) over the entire IMACS spectral range. This makes it all the more surprising that no lensing galaxy is observed in the PSF subtracted image (Figure 3). We take the two sources to be lensless twins.

gmw_00012838.name: WISE 1051-1142
gmw_00012838.overview: WISE 1051-1142 consists of a pair of lensless twin quasars [arXiv:1607.07476]. The two components have nearly identical redshifts of z = 0.8839 and z = 0.8811, showing MgII emission with no obvious signs of intervening absorption [arXiv:1607.07476].

sources.summary: The paper treats WISE 1051-1142 as lensless twins, with nearly identical redshifts, MgII emission, no obvious intervening absorption, and a mildly wavelength-dependent flux ratio.

quotes.quote: The two quasar images of WISE 1051-1142 have nearly identical redshifts. The single prominent emission line just blueward of the IMACS chip gap is likely MgII due to the absence of other emission features in the observed wavelength range. Gaussian fits to the emission profiles yield source redshifts of z = 0.8839 0.0002 and z = 0.8811 0.0008 for the brighter and fainter components, respectively, which overlap at the 3-4 level. There are no obvious signs of intervening absorption.

The flux ratio between components varies from 6:1 at the blue end to 7:1 at the red end, not inconsistent with wavelength-dependent continuum microlensing seen in other lensed quasars. We take the two sources to be lensless twins.

gmw_00012839.name: WISE 1427-0715
gmw_00012839.overview: WISE 1427-0715 is a projected pair of two quasars aligned along the line of sight [arXiv:1607.07476]. The brighter component of the pair is located at a redshift of z = 1.2258, while the fainter component lies at a redshift of z = 0.7218 [arXiv:1607.07476].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISE 1427-0715 as a projected quasar pair: the brighter component is at z=1.2258, while the fainter MgII source is at z=0.7218.

quotes.quote: WISE 1427-0715 is a chance projection of two quasars. The brighter component shows the MgII broad emission line at about 6230 yielding a source redshift of z = 1.2258 0.0004 . The only prominent emission line at 4820 \ in the fainter component is likely MgII due to the absence of other features in the observed wavelength range, yielding a source redshift of z = 0.7218 0.0004 . A MgII 2796,2803 absorption doublet is also seen in the brighter component's spectrum at a fitted redshift of z = 0.7205 0.0004 and thus associated with the host galaxy of the fainter quasar. The spectra are shown in Figure 6.

gmw_00012840.name: WISE 2215-3056
gmw_00012840.overview: WISE 2215-3056 is a binary quasar consisting of two components with similar redshifts [arXiv:1607.07476]. Despite these similar redshifts, the two components exhibit spectral differences, including distinct absorption features and FeIII/UV emission lines [arXiv:1607.07476].

sources.summary: The paper classifies WISE 2215-3056 as a binary quasar whose components have similar redshifts but distinct absorption and FeIII/UV spectral features.

quotes.quote: WISE 2215-3056 is a binary quasar. Its components have similar redshifts but have notable spectral differences. The brighter component is at z = 1.3474 0.0003 based on its MgII broad emission line profile and shows strong absorption features blueward of its CIII ] and MgII emission lines.

The fainter component has a source redshift of z = 1.3508 0.0004 based on its MgII profile, a > 5 difference from the brighter component, but shows neither of the two absorption features seen in the brighter component. There is also a prominent FeIII/UV 48 broad emission line of comparable intensity to CIII ] in the brighter component that is lacking in the fainter companion. Despite the similar redshifts, the spectral differences argue for two separate quasars. The spectra are shown in Figure 6.

gmw_00012841.name: WISE 2304-2214
gmw_00012841.overview: WISE 2304-2214 is a doubly lensed quasar located at a redshift of z=1.423 [arXiv:1607.07476]. The system features a lensing galaxy at z=0.4455, and its emission line equivalent widths suggest the presence of microlensing [arXiv:1607.07476].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISE 2304-2214 as a doubly lensed quasar at z=1.423, with matched component redshifts, a detected lensing galaxy at z=0.4455, and possible microlensing.

quotes.quote: WISE 2304 -2214 is a doubly lensed quasar at z=1.423. The quasar spectra shown in Figure 6 have emission at CIII ] , CII ] and MgII. Gaussian fits to the MgII emission profiles yield source redshifts of z = 1.4222 0.0004 and z = 1.4218 0.0003 for the brighter and fainter components, respectively. The brighter component has an MgII absorption doublet at z=0.6362 0.0001 that is much weaker, if not totally absent, in the fainter component.

Fitting a third Gaussian component of the same width between the two quasar components yields a spectrum of what appears to be the lensing galaxy with Ca H K absorption near 5700 giving z_ gal = 0.4455 0.0004 . The emission line equivalent widths are substantially smaller in the brighter component, suggesting that one or both components are micro-lensed.

gmw_00012842.name: WISE 2344-3056
gmw_00012842.overview: WISE 2344-3056 is a strongly lensed quad system with four overlapping images [arXiv:1607.07476]. The quasar has a redshift of 1.2978 and contains an intervening absorption system at a redshift of 0.9472 [arXiv:1607.07476].

sources.summary: The paper describes WISE 2344-3056 as a quad system with four overlapping images; its combined spectrum gives a quasar redshift of 1.2978 and an intervening absorber at z=0.9472.

quotes.quote: The slit used to obtain a spectrum for WISE 2344-3056 ran along the line connecting the ellipses in Figure 1, with light from all four images overlapping on roughly six spatial pixels. We present only the combined spectrum in Figure 7. The quasar redshift is z = 1.2978 0.0003 based on a Gaussian fit to the MgII broad emission line. The CIII ] broad emission line is also present at a much lower signal to noise. There is at least one intervening absorption system at z = 0.9472 0.0012 anchored by the MgII 2796,2803 absorption doublet, several FeII lines ( 2344,2374,2382,2399,2586,2600), FeIII 2419, and MgI 2852.

gmw_00012843.name: KK246
gmw_00012843.overview: KK246 is an exceptionally isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located within the Local Void [arXiv:1311.4152]. It features an old stellar population embedded in a large surrounding cloud and contains only a single, low-luminosity star-forming region [arXiv:1311.4152].

sources.summary: The paper presents KK246 as an exceptionally isolated Local Void dwarf galaxy with one small star-forming region, an old stellar component in extended H I, and normal nebular metallicity for its size.

quotes.quote: We have identified two isolated gas-rich dwarf galaxies in the SIGRID sample located within the Local Void for investigation, [ KK98 ] 246 (ESO 461-G036), hereafter ``KK246'' and MCG-01-41-006 (HIPASS J1609-04), hereafter ``J1609''. KK246 is relatively nearby 6.4 Mpc, Tully08 . J1609-04 is somewhat more massive and luminous, but is over twice as distant, at 14.82 Mpc Nicholls11 .

Description KK246 is a particularly interesting object in the SIGRID sample as it has been identified as the most isolated dwarf galaxy known in the Local Volume (D 10 Mpc, Karachentsev et al. 2013). It is situated a few Mpc within the Local Void Tully08, Kreckel11, Nasonova11 . This void is large, at least 23Mpc in radius Tully08 , and KK246 has no apparent neighbours within 3 Mpc Karachentsev04 .

Previous observations of KK246 have shown that the stellar component is embedded in a large surrounding cloud (Kreckel et al. 2011) and contains an old stellar population Karachentsev06 . Although it currently has only one small, low-luminosity star-forming region, we have been able to measure the nebular metallicity of that region using the WiFeS IFU spectrograph on the ANU 2.3m telescope at Siding Spring Observatory.

gmw_00012844.name: NGC1052-DF2
gmw_00012844.overview: NGC1052-DF2 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy that is extremely gas-poor [arXiv:1810.04938]. Deep radio observations failed to detect atomic hydrogen, indicating the galaxy may have undergone a tidal stripping event [arXiv:1810.04938].

sources.summary: The paper reports a GMRT non-detection of atomic gas in NGC1052-DF2, setting a stringent H I mass limit and interpreting the galaxy as extremely gas-poor and possibly tidally stripped.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we present a deep Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope search for atomic hydrogen in this ultra-diffuse galaxy, NGC1052-DF2. We report a non-detection of the \ transition from the galaxy and place a stringent upper limit on the mass of the galaxy - M _ HI < 3.15 10^6 \ ( V /20 \ km/s )^ 1/2 \ \ (3 ) . This makes NGC1052-DF2 an extremely gas-poor galaxy with an atomic gas to stellar mass fraction of M _ HI / M _* \ < \ 0.016 \ ( V /20 \ km/s )^ 1/2 \ (3 ) . Such low gas fractions are typical of dwarf ellipticals in dense environments and would be consistent with NGC1052-DF2 having undergone a tidal stripping event which can also explain its apparent lack of dark matter.

We searched for \ emission from NGC1052-DF2 in the spectral cube at a wide range of velocity resolutions from 1.25 km/s to 30 km/s. These resolutions cover the wide range of reported uncertainties in the velocity dispersion of NGC1052-DF2( NatPaper , Martin2018 ). We did not detect the \ line from the galaxy in any of the velocity resolutions.

gmw_00012845.name: SDSSJ082358.30+213545.2
gmw_00012845.overview: SDSSJ082358.30+213545.2 is a quasar [arXiv:1804.09954]. Its spectrum is rich in Fe II emission, with a relative Fe II strength estimated at 4.045 [arXiv:1804.09954].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ082358.30+213545.2 as a test quasar, fitting its multiwavelength SED with CIGALE/CLOUDY and modeling its Fe II-rich spectrum, deriving R_FeII=4.045 versus the catalog value 6.56.

quotes.quote: The test case: SDSSJ082358.30+213545.2 A FWHM(H ) R_ FeII diagram for 20,000 SDSS quasars was presented in using the QSO catalog , where the relative Fe _ II strength (R _ Fe_ II = EW _ Fe_ II /EW _ H ) is considered for the FeII emission within 4434-4684 .

Using multi-wavelength photometric data, we produce the best fit for this source using CIGALE (Fig. fig2 a). To construct the SED, we used http://vizier.u-strasbg.fr/vizier/sed/ Vizier photometric viewer and available literature to procure the data points. The SED is shown in Fig. fig2 b along with a CLOUDY interpolation on the overplotted data with respective errorbars.

A semi-automatic spectral fitting procedure Using the approach from , we show a detailed modeling of the quasar spectrum which includes the Fe II pseudo-continuum (Fig. fig4 a). Owing to our fitting procedure that takes into account the Balmer continuum, starlight contamination and several FeII templates, the value of R _ FeII thus estimated, is 4.045. This value quoted in the QSO catalog is 6.56.

gmw_00012846.name: J082313
gmw_00012846.overview: J082313 is classified as a type-II quasar [arXiv:2104.06223]. Despite this classification, the galaxy exhibits signs of a weak broad-line-region emission component [arXiv:2104.06223].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J082313 as a type-II QSO with weak broad-line-region emission, fits NLR and BLR Gaussian components, and maps the modeled BLR flux distribution.

quotes.quote: Even though our sample consists of QSOs classified as type II rey08 , one of them -- J082313 -- showed signs of a weak BLR contribution (only visible in the profile). To model it, we fitted the spectra -- obtained from a PSF-size aperture -- modeling it with one and one components for the NLR gas, along with a broader Gaussian representing the BLR. The other emission emission were also fitted, which helped the NLR model, since we fixed the kinematics of the components between different lines. Fig. fig:BLR (below) shows the resulting fit without (left) and with (right) the BLR component, where v_ =-140 km s^ 1 and _ =1560 km s^ 1 .

With the the BLR component modelled, we repeated the fitting process, now, for all spectra of the data cube. However, in order to account for the smearing effect of the atmospheric seeing, we fixed v_ and _ at the above values, with the amplitude being the only free parameter of the BLR component. Fig. fig:BLR (top) shows the resulting BLR flux distribution, along with white contours from the 2D-Gaussian best fit model. The resulting FWHM of 0.68 arcsec is in agreement with the seeing obtained from the field in the acquisition image (Table 2 in the paper).

gmw_00012847.name: GRB 030328 host galaxy
gmw_00012847.overview: The GRB 030328 host galaxy is a starburst galaxy with a low-extinction spectral energy distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0504059]. Photometric observations in four optical bands show that the host galaxy has a rest-frame B-band magnitude of approximately -20.4 [arXiv:astro-ph/0504059].

sources.summary: The paper reports first UBRI detection of the GRB 030328 host galaxy, gives averaged host coordinates, and finds a low-extinction starburst SED with rest-frame B magnitude M_ B 20.4.

quotes.quote: We present for the first time the detection of the GRB 030328 host galaxy in four optical bands equivalent to UBRI . The host galaxy spectral energy distribution is consistent with a low extinction ( E(B-V) < 0.21 ) starburst galaxy. The restframe B band magnitude of the host is M_ B 20.4 .

The final host coordinates, obtained averaging the four channels, are; _ J2000 =12^ h 10^ m 48.37^ , _ J2000 =-09^ 20^ 51.0^ . The astrometric error for each coordinate is 0.7^ , not accounting for the systematic error of the USNO-A2.0 catalogue ( 0.25^ , see ).

gmw_00012848.name: IGR J22292+6647
gmw_00012848.overview: IGR J22292+6647 is a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of approximately 0.113 [arXiv:0903.1302]. The galaxy's position coincides with an asymmetric-double radio source [arXiv:0903.1302].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGR J22292+6647 as a Seyfert 1 AGN: Swift localized the counterpart, a coincident asymmetric-double radio source, and optical spectroscopy gives z=0.113.

quotes.quote: IGR J22292+6647 was observed by Swift , which found a source at (J2000)=22^h29^m13^s.5 , (J2000)=+66^ 46^ 51  8^ , coinciding with USNO-B1.0 1567--0242133. There is a radio source at this location (87GB 222741.2+663124), described as an asymmetric double gc91 , which suggests this may be an AGN landi07_atel, landi09 . This is congruous with its position out of the Galactic plane, with Galactic coordinates l=109.56^ , b=+7.69^ .

The optical spectrum has a flat continuum dominated by one strong and broad emission line (Figure  fig:spectra e). If we assume that the strong feature is H , we find a redshift of z=0.113 0.001 . The broad hydrogen line indicates this is a Seyfert 1 AGN.

gmw_00012849.name: ADF22-LineA
gmw_00012849.overview: ADF22-LineA was initially identified as a candidate emission-line galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6 [arXiv:1907.05742]. However, deeper follow-up observations failed to reproduce the emission-line features, indicating that the original detection was likely spurious [arXiv:1907.05742].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ADF22-LineA as one of two candidate z 6 emitters from a Cycle-2 ALMA blind search, then reports Cycle-5 follow-up did not reproduce it and concludes the detections were spurious.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The blind line search described in hayatsu2017 resulted in the detection of two emission lines, at 253.79 and 269.92 GHz, with J2000 positions: (RA, Dec) = ( 22^ h 17^ m 37.43^ s , +00^ 17' 10''.7 ) and ( 22^ h 17^ m 31.95^ s , +00^ 18' 20''.3 ), respectively.

quotes.quote: We report Atacama Large Millimetre/submillimeter Array (ALMA) Cycle-5 follow-up observations of two candidate \ emitters at z 6 in the ALMA deep field in SSA 22 (ADF22). The candidates were detected blindly in a Cycle-2 ALMA survey covering 5 arcmin ^2 , with a single tuning, along with two CO lines associated with galaxies at lower redshifts. Various tests suggested at least one of the two 6 \ candidates should be robust hayatsu2017 . Nevertheless, our new, deeper observations recover neither candidate, demonstrating a higher contamination rate than expected.

Single fields centred on the targets were observed at frequencies of 251.284 and 261.183 GHz for ADF22-LineA and ADF22-LineB, respectively. The median noise value is 0.77 mJy beam ^ 1 at 36 km s ^ 1 spectral resolution for both datasets, similar to the Cycle-2 data; the angular resolution was around 1''.0 0''.6 .

Within the 200 km s ^ 1 velocity range around the line centres, the emission-line features seen in Cycle-2 are not reproduced in our Cycle-5 observations. From our Cycle-5 data we conclude that the emission-line candidates reported by hayatsu2017 were spurious; we cannot fully rule out the possibility of a transient line emitter, although the consistent detections in subsets of the data from 2014 and 2015 make a transient line emitter unlikely.

gmw_00012850.name: ADF22-LineB
gmw_00012850.overview: ADF22-LineB was initially identified as a candidate emission-line galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6 [arXiv:1907.05742]. However, deeper follow-up observations failed to reproduce the emission line, indicating that the original detection was likely spurious [arXiv:1907.05742].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ADF22-LineB as one of two candidate z 6 emitters from a Cycle-2 ALMA blind search, then reports Cycle-5 follow-up did not reproduce it and concludes the detections were spurious.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The blind line search described in hayatsu2017 resulted in the detection of two emission lines, at 253.79 and 269.92 GHz, with J2000 positions: (RA, Dec) = ( 22^ h 17^ m 37.43^ s , +00^ 17' 10''.7 ) and ( 22^ h 17^ m 31.95^ s , +00^ 18' 20''.3 ), respectively.

quotes.quote: We report Atacama Large Millimetre/submillimeter Array (ALMA) Cycle-5 follow-up observations of two candidate \ emitters at z 6 in the ALMA deep field in SSA 22 (ADF22). The candidates were detected blindly in a Cycle-2 ALMA survey covering 5 arcmin ^2 , with a single tuning, along with two CO lines associated with galaxies at lower redshifts. Various tests suggested at least one of the two 6 \ candidates should be robust hayatsu2017 . Nevertheless, our new, deeper observations recover neither candidate, demonstrating a higher contamination rate than expected.

Single fields centred on the targets were observed at frequencies of 251.284 and 261.183 GHz for ADF22-LineA and ADF22-LineB, respectively. The median noise value is 0.77 mJy beam ^ 1 at 36 km s ^ 1 spectral resolution for both datasets, similar to the Cycle-2 data; the angular resolution was around 1''.0 0''.6 .

Within the 200 km s ^ 1 velocity range around the line centres, the emission-line features seen in Cycle-2 are not reproduced in our Cycle-5 observations. From our Cycle-5 data we conclude that the emission-line candidates reported by hayatsu2017 were spurious; we cannot fully rule out the possibility of a transient line emitter, although the consistent detections in subsets of the data from 2014 and 2015 make a transient line emitter unlikely.

gmw_00012851.name: HE 1122 1628
gmw_00012851.overview: HE 1122 1628 is a quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.40 [arXiv:astro-ph/0106198]. High-resolution spectroscopy of this background source has been utilized to study Lyman-alpha forest absorption lines and correlate them with foreground galaxies [arXiv:0911.3400, arXiv:astro-ph/0106198].

sources.summary: HE 1122-1648 is used as a background quasar sightline; its field supplied 37 survey galaxies, and its STIS/UVES spectra yielded a large absorption-line list for absorber-galaxy correlation.

quotes.quote: This included 37 in the field of HE 1122-1648 that were at a redshift of z 0.68 and had a redshift confidence 4 , and so could be correlated with the \ absorbers.

The \ absorbers used in the cross-correlation function were detected in the absorption spectra of the background quasars HE 1122-1648 and PKS 1127-145. These quasars were selected because high resolution (R =30000 ), STIS echelle spectroscopy obtained using the E230M grating exists within the HST archive. These archived spectra cover the wavelength range 2050-3111 , the range of wavelengths needed to locate \ lines at a redshift of z 1 . With such a high dispersion, it was hoped that low column density ( 13 ) HI absorption lines could be resolved and Voigt profiles fitted in order that their column density and b-parameter could be determined.

The Final line-lists The final line-list for HE 1122-1648 contained 740 absorption lines that were detected in the STIS E230M and UVES echelle data that had an equivalent width significance greater than 3. This included 586 \ lines each with a b-parameter 10 \ and 154 metals. Of these \ lines 97 came from the two STIS E230M spectra.

sources.summary: The paper uses the high-resolution UVES spectrum of the z=2.40 quasar HE 1122 1628 to invert Ly-forest absorption, infer IGM temperature, and compare H I absorption structure.

quotes.quote: We study the physical properties of the Lyman- forest by applying the inversion method described by Pichon et al. (2001) to the high resolution and high S/N ratio spectrum of the z_ em  = 2.40 quasar HE 1122 1628 obtained during Science Verification of UVES at the VLT.

The m_ V  = 15.5 and z_ em  = 2.40 quasar HE 1122 1628 was observed during Science Verification of the Ultra-violet and Visible Echelle Spectrograph mounted on the 8.2 m Kuyen telescope of the European Southern Observatory, mount Paranal (Chile).

We have applied the Bayesian inversion procedure developped by Pichon et al. (2001) to the high resolution and high S/N ratio spectrum of HE 1122 1628 obtained during Science Verification of UVES at the VLT and made publicly available by ESO.

gmw_00012852.overview: The radio galaxy 081040 + 481230 exhibits a steep radio spectrum at frequencies below 10 GHz [arXiv:0807.4102]. At higher frequencies, a flat-spectrum component, which may represent a radio core, becomes significant [arXiv:0807.4102].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 081040 + 481230 has a steep spectrum below 10 GHz, with a possible high-frequency flat/core component requiring follow-up observations.

quotes.quote: 081040 + 481230 behaves similarly below 10 GHz (with S ^ , 0.42 ). At higher frequencies however, it seems that a flat-spectrum component (possibly a core component) becomes significant. Further observations are necessary to test this idea.

gmw_00012853.overview: The radio source 090624 + 005758 exhibits a monotonically rising spectrum, which may indicate a self-absorbed GHz-peaked spectrum or a flaring flat-spectrum core [arXiv:0807.4102]. Optical spectral characteristics suggest the source could be classified as a narrow-line radio galaxy [arXiv:0807.4102].

sources.summary: The paper describes 090624 + 005758 as an unusual source with a rising, possibly self-absorbed GHz-peaked spectrum or flaring flat-spectrum core, and notes optical signs that may reclassify it as an NLRG.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, 023140 011005 and 090624 + 005758 appear to be less common.

The latter shows a monotonically increasing spectrum (indicative of a GHz-peaked spectrum source with 0.62 ) suggesting a self absorbed system in which we observe only the optically thick part of the spectrum. Alternatively, such a spectral shape could be attributed to a flat spectrum source (core-dominated) that undergoes a flaring event. This could only be clarified with additional observations.

However, our recent re-analysis of the optical spectra showed that two of the sources discussed earlier, namely 090624 + 005758 and 125027 + 001345, display optical spectral characteristics that could classify them as NLRGs rather than as BLRGs.

gmw_00012854.name: spSpec-53491-2097-516
gmw_00012854.overview: The galaxy spSpec-53491-2097-516 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1009.5113]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus and exhibits emission lines where the H-alpha and [N II] 6583 intensities are similar [arXiv:1009.5113].

sources.summary: The paper uses spSpec-53491-2097-516 as an SDSS-DR7 AGN test galaxy, noting similar H and [N II] intensities and a spiral morphology.

quotes.quote: At each redshift we selected a star--forming galaxy (spSpec-52368-0580-499, spSpec-53816-2231-307), and an AGN (spSpec-53491-2097-516, spSpec-53473-2108-507). As observed in Fig. 9 and Tables 2 to 5, we selected ELGs of different H and [ N ii ] 6583 intensities, in some cases both lines have similar intensities (e.g. spSpec-53491-2097-516), and in other the [ N ii ] 6583 line is weak (e.g. spSpec-53816-2231-307). It can be also appreciated the different morphologies of the SDSS galaxies, including a spiral (spSpec-53491-2097-516), a SO/Sa spiral (spSpec-53473-2108-507), and compact galaxies (spSpec-52368-0580-499, spSpec-53816-2231-307).

gmw_00012855.name: spSpec-53816-2231-307
gmw_00012855.overview: The object spSpec-53816-2231-307 is classified as a compact galaxy [arXiv:1009.5113]. It is a star-forming system characterized by weak [N II] 6583 emission line intensity relative to its H-alpha flux [arXiv:1009.5113].

sources.summary: The paper treats spSpec-53816-2231-307 as a compact star-forming SDSS-DR7 test galaxy with weak [N II] emission, yet recovers its [N II] flux to 10% error.

quotes.quote: At each redshift we selected a star--forming galaxy (spSpec-52368-0580-499, spSpec-53816-2231-307), and an AGN (spSpec-53491-2097-516, spSpec-53473-2108-507). As observed in Fig. 9 and Tables 2 to 5, we selected ELGs of different H and [ N ii ] 6583 intensities, in some cases both lines have similar intensities (e.g. spSpec-53491-2097-516), and in other the [ N ii ] 6583 line is weak (e.g. spSpec-53816-2231-307). It can be also appreciated the different morphologies of the SDSS galaxies, including a spiral (spSpec-53491-2097-516), a SO/Sa spiral (spSpec-53473-2108-507), and compact galaxies (spSpec-52368-0580-499, spSpec-53816-2231-307).
Also, for the spSpec-53816-2231-307 galaxy it was possible to estimate their [ N ii ] 6583 line flux with an error of 10 although its flux is only 16 the H flux.

gmw_00012856.name: KIC 6423919
gmw_00012856.overview: KIC 6423919 is a nearby spiral and disk galaxy [arXiv:1511.06471]. It has a dereddened g-r color of 0.69, which is typical of normal galaxies [arXiv:1511.06471].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KIC 6423919 as a nearby spiral/disk galaxy, estimates its optical and WISE properties, and argues it contributes negligibly to KOI 6705 emission and cannot plausibly produce the transit signal.

quotes.quote: The nearest (7") source in the DSS images and 2MASS Point Source Catalog is a spiral galaxy the (see Section sec.proximal ). The galaxy is not detected as a separate WISE source, is too faint for 2MASS and it falls outside the SDSS footprint, but we associated it with KIC 6423919, which has = 17.44 and g- = 0.77 . To correct for interstellar reddening/extinction we use the mean reddening value E_ B-V = 0.079 at this location from Schlafly2011 and adopting R=A/E(B-V) of 3.31 for g and 2.32 for g from Yuan2013 , yielding a dereddened g- = 0.69 and an un-extincted = 17.26 . This g- color is typical of "normal" galaxies. To estimate the WISE magnitudes of this galaxy we identified analogs from the atlas of galaxies with well-characterized spectral energy distributions and integrated WISE fluxes of Brown2014 .

A disk galaxy (discussed in Sec. sec.star ) is centered 10 " from \ and may partially intrude into the photometric aperture. Based on a g = 18 and a typical disk galaxy M_g 21 Brown2014 , the distance modulus must be 39 and the most massive, luminous stars must be fainter than m=27 . Even the total occulting of one of these stars would produce a signal smaller than 10 ppm, thus we do not consider this object a plausible source for the signal of 6705.01.

gmw_00012857.name: ICRF3 QSO 1337+637
gmw_00012857.overview: ICRF3 QSO 1337+637 is a high-redshift quasar at a redshift of z=2.56 [arXiv:1811.10117]. Its spectrum is dominated by a Lyman-alpha line and features strong carbon IV and carbon III emission lines [arXiv:1811.10117].

sources.summary: The paper uses ICRF3 QSO 1337+637 as a high-redshift quasar example: its spectrum is Ly-alpha dominated with strong C IV/C III lines in Gaia's G band, and a large Gaia-ICRF3 offset excludes it from the precious set.

quotes.quote: For example, the spectrum of the ICRF3 QSO 1337+637 ( z=2.56 ) is dominated by a Ly _ line at approximately 425 nm. There are also strong C IV and C III lines present within the G band. This object has a large Gaia--ICRF3 offset, which left it out of the precious set.

gmw_00012858.name: J000905 205630
gmw_00012858.overview: J000905 205630 is classified as a compact symmetric object candidate radio source [arXiv:2110.07802]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry imaging reveals that the source possesses a compact core and diffuse emission, with a high turnover frequency of 900 MHz and an inverted spectrum [arXiv:2110.07802].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J000905 205630 as a CE radio source with a compact VLBI core, diffuse emission, an inverted spectrum, high compactness, and high turnover frequency.

quotes.quote: Three sources (J000905 205630, J002125 005540 and J011857 071855) are classified as CE sources.

Three sources, J000905 205630 (J0009), J002125 005540 (J0021), and J011857 071855 (J0118) show a compact core and diffuse emission in VLBI images. Both J0009 and J0118 show inverted spectra, while J0021 show a broken power law spectrum. J0009 has a high R ( =0.62 ) and a higher turnover frequency ( 900 MHz), suggesting that its overall radio structure is compact ( <0.3 ) and that the VLBI core dominates the total emission.

gmw_00012859.name: J002125 005540
gmw_00012859.overview: J002125 005540 is classified as a compact symmetric object candidate radio source [arXiv:2110.07802]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry imaging of the source reveals a compact core accompanied by diffuse emission, and it exhibits a broken power-law spectrum [arXiv:2110.07802].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J002125 005540 as a CE radio source whose VLBI image shows a compact core with diffuse emission and a broken power-law spectrum.

quotes.quote: Three sources (J000905 205630, J002125 005540 and J011857 071855) are classified as CE sources.

Three sources, J000905 205630 (J0009), J002125 005540 (J0021), and J011857 071855 (J0118) show a compact core and diffuse emission in VLBI images. Both J0009 and J0118 show inverted spectra, while J0021 show a broken power law spectrum.

gmw_00012860.name: J011857 071855
gmw_00012860.overview: J011857 071855 is a radio source classified as a compact symmetric object candidate [arXiv:2110.07802]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry observations reveal that it possesses a compact core and diffuse emission, with its overall radio emission dominated by sub-arcsecond structures [arXiv:2110.07802].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J011857 071855 as a CE radio source with compact-core plus diffuse VLBI structure, an inverted spectrum, high scintillation index, and sub-arcsecond-dominated emission.

quotes.quote: Three sources (J000905 205630, J002125 005540 and J011857 071855) are classified as CE sources.

Three sources, J000905 205630 (J0009), J002125 005540 (J0021), and J011857 071855 (J0118) show a compact core and diffuse emission in VLBI images. Both J0009 and J0118 show inverted spectra, while J0021 show a broken power law spectrum. J0009 has a high R ( =0.62 ) and a higher turnover frequency ( 900 MHz), suggesting that its overall radio structure is compact ( <0.3 ) and that the VLBI core dominates the total emission. In contrast, J0118 has a high NSI = 1.6 , but a lower R = 0.22 and a lower turnover frequency (around 300 MHz), indicating that the source structure is compact but the emission is dominated by sub-arcsec structures. These implications are consistent with their VLBI morphologies shown in Figure fig:morph .

gmw_00012861.name: J002549 260211
gmw_00012861.overview: J002549 260211 is a compact symmetric radio source with an inverted, peaked-spectrum profile [arXiv:2110.07802]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry observations reveal two jet components, classifying the object as a young radio source in an early transitional stage [arXiv:2110.07802].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J002549 260211 as a CdJ compact symmetric radio source with two jet components, an inverted spectrum, and a peaked-spectrum CSO morphology.

quotes.quote: Four sources are identified in the category of CdJ (J002549 260211, J003829 211957, J011651 205202 and J014237 074232). The VLBI X band image of the source J002549 260211 denotes one of the jet components of this compact symmetric object (CSO) whose other jet component, as identified in the S band VLBI image, is located at 0.01^ away to the southeast.

The spectra of four sources (J002549 260211, J003829 211957, J011651 205202 and J014237 074232), morphologically classified as CdJ, are all of inverted shape. From their triple VLBI morphology and overall peaked-spectrum type, we consider them as CSOs, belonging to a class of young radio sources in the earliest transitional stage toward large-size symmetric objects ( i.e. , Fanaroff-Riley type 2 galaxies) e.g., 1996ApJ...460..634R,2012ApJ...760...77A .

gmw_00012862.name: J003829 211957
gmw_00012862.overview: J003829 211957 is a young, compact symmetric radio source that exhibits a triple VLBI morphology [arXiv:2110.07802]. It is characterized by a gigahertz-peaked, inverted spectrum and a compact symmetric object-type morphology [arXiv:2110.07802].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J003829 211957 as a CdJ source with CSO-type morphology, GPS-type and inverted spectra, and young compact-symmetric radio-source characteristics.

quotes.quote: Four sources are identified in the category of CdJ (J002549 260211, J003829 211957, J011651 205202 and J014237 074232).

Except for J003829 211957 (with a CSO-type morphology and a GPS-type spectrum), the other 18 sources with available multi-epoch X-band VLBI data have a variability index 0.1 , with 12 of them having variability indices 0.2 .

The spectra of four sources (J002549 260211, J003829 211957, J011651 205202 and J014237 074232), morphologically classified as CdJ, are all of inverted shape. From their triple VLBI morphology and overall peaked-spectrum type, we consider them as CSOs, belonging to a class of young radio sources in the earliest transitional stage toward large-size symmetric objects ( i.e. , Fanaroff-Riley type 2 galaxies) e.g., 1996ApJ...460..634R,2012ApJ...760...77A .

gmw_00012863.name: J011651 205202
gmw_00012863.overview: J011651 205202 is a young radio galaxy classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:2110.07802]. It exhibits a triple VLBI morphology, an inverted radio spectrum, and properties of a candidate double-jet source [arXiv:2110.07802].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J011651 205202 as a CdJ source with triple VLBI morphology, an inverted spectrum, and compact-symmetric radio-source properties associated with young radio galaxies.

quotes.quote: Four sources are identified in the category of CdJ (J002549 260211, J003829 211957, J011651 205202 and J014237 074232).

The spectra of four sources (J002549 260211, J003829 211957, J011651 205202 and J014237 074232), morphologically classified as CdJ, are all of inverted shape. From their triple VLBI morphology and overall peaked-spectrum type, we consider them as CSOs, belonging to a class of young radio sources in the earliest transitional stage toward large-size symmetric objects ( i.e. , Fanaroff-Riley type 2 galaxies) e.g., 1996ApJ...460..634R,2012ApJ...760...77A .

gmw_00012864.name: J014237 074232
gmw_00012864.overview: J014237 074232 is a young radio galaxy classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:2110.07802]. It exhibits a triple VLBI morphology and an inverted radio spectrum [arXiv:2110.07802].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J014237 074232 as a CdJ source with triple VLBI morphology, an inverted spectrum, and compact-symmetric radio-source properties associated with young radio galaxies.

quotes.quote: Four sources are identified in the category of CdJ (J002549 260211, J003829 211957, J011651 205202 and J014237 074232).

The spectra of four sources (J002549 260211, J003829 211957, J011651 205202 and J014237 074232), morphologically classified as CdJ, are all of inverted shape. From their triple VLBI morphology and overall peaked-spectrum type, we consider them as CSOs, belonging to a class of young radio sources in the earliest transitional stage toward large-size symmetric objects ( i.e. , Fanaroff-Riley type 2 galaxies) e.g., 1996ApJ...460..634R,2012ApJ...760...77A .

gmw_00012865.name: 4FGL J407.5+0741
gmw_00012865.overview: 4FGL J407.5+0741 is a gamma-ray emitting blazar with a disputed classification [arXiv:2111.02082]. While it exhibits a BL Lacertae optical spectrum, its broad spectral energy distribution is typical of a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2111.02082].

sources.summary: The paper treats 4FGL J407.5+0741/TXS 0404+075 as a BL Lac gamma-ray source with disputed classification, citing FSRQ classifications, FSRQ-like SED modeling, and a BL Lac optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J407.5+0741, known as TXS 0404+075, is a BL Lac class ray emission object. However, this source is classified as FSRQs in other studies. Tan2020 suggested an external Compton model, which is usually applied to FSRQs due the existence of a BLR or a dust torus, to fit its broad band SED and studied the physical properties of FSRQs.

Xiong2014 classified this source as a LSP, which is mostly consist of FSRQs, during their study of intrinsic ray luminosity and jet power. This source shows typical a broad SED of FSRQ type, meanwhile, it shows the BL Lac optical spectrum. Therefore, the exact classification of this source is in debate, it is better to exclude this source when investigate the possible physical property difference between FSRQs and BL Lacs.

gmw_00012866.name: S Ori J053840.8 024022
gmw_00012866.overview: S Ori J053840.8 024022 is identified as a probable galaxy contaminant [arXiv:0909.0802]. It exhibits a slightly extended structure and has a full width at half maximum systematically larger than nearby point-like sources [arXiv:0909.0802].

sources.summary: The paper identifies S Ori J053840.8 024022 as a probable galaxy contaminant: its red colors, brighter long-wavelength detections, slight extension, and enlarged FWHM make it likely to be a galaxy.

quotes.quote: With J-H 1.2 mag and J-K_ s 2.2 mag, S Ori J053840.8 024022 could be a late L-type object or a galaxy. It is detected with brighter magnitudes at longer wavelengths, from the Omega2000 JHK_ s band images (18j--k, Table HKother ) to the [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] band images, but is then undetected in the [ 5.8 ] [ 8.0 ] band images. It is undetected in the WFC and LRIS I band images (2000i3, Table IZlris ).

In the ISAAC J band image, it is found to be slightly extended and fainter than in the lower-resolution Omega2000 J band image. The FWHMs of the object in the Omega2000 JHK_ s band images are systematically larger by a factor 1.4 than those of nearby faint point-like sources, suggesting that it is a galaxy.

gmw_00012867.name: S Ori J053811.0 023601
gmw_00012867.overview: S Ori J053811.0 023601 is a red source with optical and near-infrared colors that initially suggested it could be an early T-type object or a galaxy [arXiv:0909.0802]. However, its mid-infrared colors make an active galactic nucleus interpretation more probable [arXiv:0909.0802].

sources.summary: The paper treats S Ori J053811.0 023601 as an ambiguous red source; its photometry could indicate an early T object or galaxy, but mid-infrared colors make an AGN interpretation more probable.

quotes.quote: S Ori J053811.0 023601 , with J-H 0.5 mag and J-K_ s 1.5 mag, could be an early T-type object or a galaxy. It is detected with brighter magnitudes at longer wavelengths, from the Omega2000 JHK_ s band images (18j--k, Table HKother ) to the [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] [ 5.6 ] [ 8.0 ] band images. It is undetected in the WFC I band image and the LRIS IZ band images (2000i1 and 1998iz1, Table IZlris ). In Fig. seds , we show its spectral energy distribution, together with average ones of field dwarfs of T2 and T3 spectral types.

S Ori J053811.0 023601 appears as an L/T transition object, but from the J versus J- [ 3.6 ] and J- [ 4.5 ] diagrams (Figs. 3p6 and 4p5 ) and as for S Ori J053840.8 024022 , it is redder than the expected cluster sequence and could be a galaxy (Fig. j3p64p5goods ). S Ori J053811.0 023601 is particularly bright in the [ 5.8 ] and [ 8.0 ] bands and displays a colour [ 3.6 ] [ 8.0 ] =2.5 0.1 , redder than most Orionis low-mass member candidates zapateroosorio2007,scholz2008,luhman2008 .

In Figs. ijhgoods , ijksgoods , and nIRmidIRgoods , its optical and near-infrared colours differ from those of AGN and galaxies, but in Fig. midIRgoods , its other colours are consistent with the AGN hypothesis (see also Fig. 1 in stern2005 , representing spectroscopically identified stars, AGN, and galaxies). Hence, although we cannot exclude completely this source beeing a peculiar cluster member with extreme infrared excesses, our data seem to indicate that it is more probably an AGN.

gmw_00012868.name: GRB080319B
gmw_00012868.overview: The host galaxy of GRB080319B contains a clumpy absorption system consisting of six distinct components [arXiv:0906.3203]. High-resolution spectroscopy reveals that gas clouds within this system are located 2 to 6 kiloparsecs from the burst, suggesting a possible extragalactic origin [arXiv:0906.3203].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB080319B host absorption system as clumpy, with six components; UV-pumped Fe II features place gas 2-6 kpc from the burst and possibly extragalactic in origin.

quotes.quote: The absorption spectra of GRB 080319B and 080330 confirm that the GRB afterglows are extremely complex, featuring several systems at different redshifts. The host galaxy systems are clumpy, and are constituted by 6 and 4 components, respectively. Such components can be separated only with high resolution spectroscopy, which is thus the only effective tool to allow for a detailed study of the GRB surrounding medium. Among the host galaxy absorption features, fine structure and other excited lines play a fundamental role.

Once the lightcurve of GRB080319B has been used as input for this code, we can estimate the distance of the absorbers from GRB080319B (Fig. 5).We find that the gas of component I is 2 kpc away from the source, while that of component III is at 6 kpc. This is a surprising result, since it reveals a possible extragalactic origin of the gas clouds at the host galaxy redshift, and tells us that the powerful GRB/afterglow emission can fully ionize the intervening gas up to the kpc scale.

gmw_00012869.name: GRB080330
gmw_00012869.overview: The host galaxy of GRB080330 features a clumpy absorption system composed of four distinct components [arXiv:0906.3203]. The closest absorbing gas component is located at a distance of approximately 280 parsecs from the burst [arXiv:0906.3203].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB080330 host absorption system as clumpy, with four components; assuming UV pumping, the closest absorber is 280 50 pc away, likely smaller because this burst was weaker.

quotes.quote: The absorption spectra of GRB 080319B and 080330 confirm that the GRB afterglows are extremely complex, featuring several systems at different redshifts. The host galaxy systems are clumpy, and are constituted by 6 and 4 components, respectively. Such components can be separated only with high resolution spectroscopy, which is thus the only effective tool to allow for a detailed study of the GRB surrounding medium. Among the host galaxy absorption features, fine structure and other excited lines play a fundamental role.

Assuming also for GRB080330 that UV pumping is at work, we derive a distance of 280 50 pc of the absorbing gas from this GRB. This is a considerably smaller distance, possibly due to the lower flux level of GRB080330. Nevertheless, it confirms that GRBs influence their environment up to distances of several pc.

gmw_00012870.name: 2XMMpJ115121.8 283605
gmw_00012870.overview: 2XMMpJ115121.8 283605 is an X-ray absorbed active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0701675]. Its intrinsic X-ray luminosity and hydrogen column density are typical of a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0701675].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2XMMpJ115121.8 283605 as an X-ray absorbed source whose XMM spectrum favors a leaky absorbed power law; its inferred column density and X-ray luminosity are Seyfert-like.

quotes.quote: The source 2XMMpJ115121.8 283605 (see Fig. X-1151 ) was observed by XMM- Newton on January 3, 2002 for about 40 ksec of good exposure time. The pn data are not available because the source is outside the pn field of view. Fitting the data with a single absorbed power-law model leads to obvious residuals in the low-energy range. Our best-fit model ( ^ 2 / dof =27.41/25 ) is therefore based on a leaky-absorbed power-law continuum (see Table xmmtable ), where two power-laws are invoked to explain both the soft (0.3 1.0 keV - unabsorbed power-law) and the hard (up to 10 keV - absorbed power-law) part of the X-ray spectrum.

The absorbed power-law reveals an intrinsic hydrogen column density, N _ H , of about 2.6 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , which clearly points to an X-ray absorbed source (even with a single-absorbed power-law model the N _ H was about 2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ). The intrinsic X-ray luminosity of this source (L _ x 1.8 10^ 43 erg sec ^ 1 ) is typical of a Seyfert galaxy.

gmw_00012871.name: 2XMMpJ145442.2+182937
gmw_00012871.overview: The source 2XMMpJ145442.2+182937 is an X-ray absorbed active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0701675]. Its X-ray luminosity is typical of a Seyfert galaxy, and its spectrum shows a stable column density along with a possible blending of two narrow iron lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0701675].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2XMMpJ145442.2+182937 as an X-ray absorbed Seyfert-like source, fit by an absorbed power law plus Gaussian line, with stable column density estimates and possible blended narrow iron lines.

quotes.quote: The source 2XMMpJ145442.2+182937 (see Fig. X-1454 ) was observed by XMM- Newton on February 16, 2003 for about 37 ksec of good exposure time by the MOS cameras and 25 ksec by the pn camera. The best-fit model ( ^ 2 / dof =30.73/33 ) based on both MOS and pn data is a single absorbed power-law along with a Gaussian line detection at 90 confidence level (see Table xmmtable ). The power-law shows a photon index 1.4 , together with N _ H 1.5 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , which points to an X-ray absorbed source. If we freeze the photon index to 1.9 , the N _ H value remains quite stable around 2.4 0.4 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

In both cases the iron line equivalent width is about 700 eV with associate errors of about 60 . This equivalent width value, due to the large error associated to it, is consistent with the value of N _ H measured from the cut-off. Moreover, a detailed analysis of the line properties is not warranted by the quality of the data, therefore we also can not exclude a possible blending of two narrow iron lines at 6.4 and 6.7 keV. The X-ray luminosity (L _ x 0.7 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) is typical of a Seyfert galaxy.

gmw_00012872.name: 2XMMpJ151612.2+070341
gmw_00012872.overview: 2XMMpJ151612.2+070341 is an active galactic nucleus with an X-ray luminosity typical of a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0701675]. Its spectrum is best fit by a leaky absorbed power-law model, indicating a heavily X-ray absorbed source [arXiv:astro-ph/0701675].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2XMMpJ151612.2+070341 as a heavily X-ray absorbed source whose spectrum is best fit with a leaky absorbed power law and whose luminosity is typical of a Seyfert galaxy.

quotes.quote: The source 2XMMpJ151612.2+070341 (see Fig. X-1516 ) was observed by XMM- Newton on August 21, 2000 for about 30 ksec of good exposure time with the MOS cameras and 23 ksec with the pn camera. A single-absorbed power-law model ( ^ 2 / dof =18.98/12 ) provided a best-fit value of the photon index that was very low ( 0.5 ), whereas the value of N _ H was already high and about 4 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . Such a flat value would suggest Compton thickness, a thesis otherwise not supported by the optical data. After freezing the value of to 1.9, more typical of AGN, there were residuals at 1--2 keV. The best-fit model ( ^ 2 / dof =16.55/12 ) is a leaky-absorbed power-law continuum with a frozen photon index of 1.9 (see Table xmmtable ).
The hydrogen column density is N _ H 1.4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , which points to an X-ray absorbed object. The X-ray luminosity (L _ x 1.6 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) is typical of a Seyfert galaxy.

gmw_00012873.name: J0216-5226
gmw_00012873.overview: J0216-5226 is a high-redshift quasar with a derived redshift of z = 6.3283 [arXiv:2402.11688]. This measurement is significantly blueshifted compared to previous literature estimates, and the object's spectra lack emission lines [arXiv:2402.11688].

sources.summary: The paper measures J0216-5226's \ derived redshift at z_ =6.3283, finds it unusually blueshifted relative to the literature estimate, and omits it from continuum analysis because its emission lines are lacking.

quotes.quote: We note that we measure the \ derived redshift of J0216-5226 to be z_ =6.3283 0.0094 , which is blueshifted from the best available literature estimate of z=6.41 0.05 Yang2019 by 3305 kms ^ 1 . Yang2019 attained the measurement by matching quasar template spectra to the and \ emission lines, noting that the offset of the line with respect to the systematic redshift is typically v( Ly \ \ ) 500 kms ^ 1 . We therefore find that the estimated z_ for this object exhibits a blueshift in significant excess to the typical systematic velocity offset.
We note that due to the lack of emission lines in the spectra of J0216–5226 we cannot securely determine its continuum, and therefore omit J0216–5226 from the remainder of the analysis.

gmw_00012874.name: J1535+1943
gmw_00012874.overview: J1535+1943 is a heavily dust-obscured quasar [arXiv:2402.11688]. This heavy obscuration affects the object's continuum slope, which may lead to a biased continuum estimate [arXiv:2402.11688].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1535+1943 as heavily dust-obscured with an unusual continuum slope, cautions that its continuum estimate may be biased, and excludes it from proximity-zone analysis pending future study.

quotes.quote: We note that J1535+1943 is heavily dust obscured, leading to an unusual continuum slope. Hence, we caution that the continuum estimate might be biased due to the lack of similar objects in the PCA training set. It would be interesting to measure the proximity zone size and infer the quasar lifetime for this obscured object, however simultaneous modelling of the quasar continuum and the obscuration rate would be required. We therefore do not take this object into account in the proximity zone analysis of the following section and leave a full study of it for future work.

gmw_00012875.name: MKT J182015.5+071455
gmw_00012875.overview: MKT J182015.5+071455 is a faint, persistent radio variable source [arXiv:2203.16918]. It lacks an optical counterpart in the PanSTARRS archive and is most likely a faint active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2203.16918].

sources.summary: The paper describes MKT J182015.5+071455 as a faint persistent radio variable with no PanSTARRS counterpart, most consistent with a faint AGN population beyond standard SDSS samples.

quotes.quote: This newly identified source is faint, with a maximum flux density of 0.2 0.1 mJy (at 1.39 GHz). It is visible in the deep images of this field, with a flux density of 0.089 0.013 mJy (at 1.39 GHz), showing that the source is likely persistent and has increased in brightness by a factor of 2 at its maximum. At this flux density, it is nearly an order of magnitude fainter than typically assumed limits for radio surveys like NVSS. There are no optical sources associated with this source in the PanSTARRS archive flewelling2020 , with a lower limit of 22.7 mag in the i band.
The most likely conclusion is that this source is consistent with a fainter AGN population than was sampled by SDSS.

gmw_00012876.name: MKT J182001.4+072945
gmw_00012876.overview: MKT J182001.4+072945 is a faint, persistent radio variable source associated with the optical counterpart PSO J275.0057+07.4958 [arXiv:2203.16918]. Although Galactic alternatives exist, the source is most likely an active galactic nucleus located beyond 100 Mpc [arXiv:2203.16918].

sources.summary: The paper presents MKT J182001.4+072945 as a faint persistent radio variable associated with a PanSTARRS source, with AGN the most likely interpretation despite possible Galactic alternatives.

quotes.quote: As with the previous source, this newly detected source is faint with a flux density of 0.3 0.1 mJy at 1.39 GHz. This source is detected in the deep image with a flux density of 0.13 0.02 mJy (1.39 GHz), showing that the source is likely persistent and has increased in brightness by a factor of 3 at its maximum. We find this source is associated with the PanSTARRS source PSO J275.0057+07.4958, which is detected in all observing filters except the g band.

Although we cannot confidently conclude which type of astrophysical variable this source is, the leading contenders are a pulsar, an X-ray binary, or an AGN at a distance beyond 100 Mpc with a faster than average variability timescale. Again, an AGN is the most likely of these conclusions given the typical source densities of such sources.

gmw_00012877.name: NVSS J181849+062843
gmw_00012877.overview: NVSS J181849+062843 is associated with a faint PanSTARRS optical counterpart [arXiv:2203.16918]. The source is most likely an active galactic nucleus or quasar that exhibits radio variability on timescales of one to three months [arXiv:2203.16918].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NVSS J181849+062843 as a long-timescale radio variable with a faint PanSTARRS counterpart, concluding it is most likely an AGN or quasar.

quotes.quote: We find that NVSS J181849+062843 is associated with the PanSTARRS source PSO J274.7073+06.4791, which was detected in just two of the PanSTARRS observing filters: i =21.80 0.20 mag and z =20.88 0.02 mag. In Figure fig:274.707 , we show the MeerKAT and PanSTARRS images of this source. With an optical brightness of 21 mag and an average radio flux density of 10 mJy at 1.39 GHz, this source is most likely an AGN or quasar, as shown in Figure fig:StewartFig . With a variability timescale of 1--3 months (determined by visual inspection of peaks in the light curve), this is typically very short for observed AGN intrinsic variability on timescales > days to years; e.g., hovatta2008 but not inconsistent as shown in Figure fig:PietkaFig . Thus, we conclude that this source is most likely an AGN.

gmw_00012878.name: NVSS J181752+064638
gmw_00012878.overview: NVSS J181752+064638 is a flat-spectrum blazar associated with the optical counterpart PSO J274.4693+06.7773 [arXiv:2203.16918]. The source is classified as an active galactic nucleus that exhibits a relatively short variability timescale of three to six months [arXiv:2203.16918].

sources.summary: The paper describes NVSS J181752+064638 as a flat-spectrum blazar with a PanSTARRS counterpart, high average radio flux density, and AGN-like month-scale variability.

quotes.quote: We searched the existing multi-wavelength catalogues by querying the location in Simbad wenger2000 , and NVSS J181752+064638 has a flat spectrum and has been identified as a blazar healey2007 . Additionally, we find this source is associated with the PanSTARRS source PSO J274.4693+06.7773, which is detected in all observing filters except g .

With an optical brightness of 21 mag and an average radio flux density of 113 mJy at 1.39 GHz, this source is most likely an AGN, as shown in Figure fig:StewartFig , further supporting the blazar identification. A variability timescale of 3--6 months (as determined by visual inspection of peaks in the light curve) is relatively short for an AGN but is not inconsistent as shown in Figure fig:PietkaFig . Thus, we conclude that this source is also most likely an AGN.

gmw_00012879.name: SDSS J165501.31+260517.4
gmw_00012879.overview: SDSS J165501.31+260517.4 is a quasar located at a redshift of 1.8671 [arXiv:1311.4710]. It exhibits a disappearing narrow magnesium II absorption system that is likely intrinsic to the quasar and caused by gas moving across the line of sight [arXiv:1311.4710].

sources.summary: The paper reports that quasar SDSS J165501.31+260517.4 at z_e=1.8671 shows a disappearing Mg II absorption system likely intrinsic to the quasar and caused by absorbing gas moving across the line of sight.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we present for the first time, the discovery of the disappearance of a narrow Mg II 2796,2803 absorption system from the spectra of quasar SDSS J165501.31+260517.4 ( z_ e =1.8671 ). This absorber is located at z_ abs =1.7877 , and has a velocity offset of 8,423 km s^ 1 with respect to the quasar. According to the velocity offset and the line variability, this narrow Mg II 2796,2803 absorption system is likely intrinsic to the quasar. Since the corresponding UV continuum emission and the absorption lines of another narrow Mg II 2796,2803 absorption system at z_ abs =1.8656 are very stable, we think that the disappearance of the absorption system is unlikely to be caused by the change in ionization of absorption gas. Instead, it likely arises from the motion of the absorption gas across the line of sight.

gmw_00012880.name: PGC049413
gmw_00012880.overview: PGC049413 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2311.04019]. High-resolution imaging from the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument clearly reveals the presence of its central bar structure [arXiv:2311.04019].

sources.summary: The paper uses PGC049413 as a barred-galaxy example: its DESI image clearly shows a bar, while degraded/redshifted simulations keep the bar detectable but increasingly bias size and ellipticity.

quotes.quote: Figure  fig:eprof_1 uses PGC049413 as an example to illustrate the impact of decreasing resolution on the bar analysis, when the F200W PSF is considered. The images are shown in the left column, while the profiles are shown on the right. The top row shows the result derived from the DESI image. As the DESI image has quite high resolution, indicated by a_ bar / FWHM=19.02 , there is clearly a bar in the image and a peak or equivalently a drop in the profile.

While the peak corresponding to the bar is reduced gradually with increasing redshift, the bar remains detectable both in the image and in the profile for PGC049413. Nevertheless, it's evident that the difference between bar size and bar ellipticity measured from the simulated high-redshift images and their true values are increasing.

gmw_00012881.name: PSS J0121 + 0347
gmw_00012881.overview: PSS J0121 + 0347 is a galaxy identified as a black-hole-mass outlier [arXiv:2310.00271]. Its emission line profile is sharply peaked, with a narrowest component showing a full width at half maximum of 540 km s^-1 [arXiv:2310.00271].

sources.summary: The paper flags PSS J0121 + 0347 as a black-hole-mass outlier; its line profile is sharply peaked, may be broader than modeled, and has a narrowest component with FWHM 540 km s^-1.

quotes.quote: Among those with a greater than 0.3 dex difference between the based single-epoch virial mass estimate and the averaged mass from all measured emission-lines as flagged by the ``Mbh Flag'', we focus on two targets with the highest mass discrepancy that are not flagged by any quality flags, PSS J0121 + 0347 and BR J0714--6455, with M_ mean / M_ = 0.36 and 0.31 , respectively.

In the case of PSS J0121 + 0347, the \ line profile is sharply peaked. As seen from the residual on both sides of the peak, the intrinsic line profile is possibly broader than the model suggests, but complexities of the line near its peak could not be adequately modeled by three Gaussian components. The narrowest \ component has a FWHM of 540 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00012882.name: BR J0714--6455
gmw_00012882.overview: BR J0714--6455 is a galaxy identified as a black-hole-mass outlier [arXiv:2310.00271]. Its line profile may be broadened by non-virial components, showing strong asymmetry and a high relative velocity shift [arXiv:2310.00271].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BR J0714--6455 as a black-hole-mass outlier whose line profile may be broadened by non-virial components, with strong asymmetry and a high relative velocity shift.

quotes.quote: Among those with a greater than 0.3 dex difference between the based single-epoch virial mass estimate and the averaged mass from all measured emission-lines as flagged by the ``Mbh Flag'', we focus on two targets with the highest mass discrepancy that are not flagged by any quality flags, PSS J0121 + 0347 and BR J0714--6455, with M_ mean / M_ = 0.36 and 0.31 , respectively.

For BR J0714--6455, the \ line profile may be broadened by non-virial components, as evidenced by the strong asymmetry present in both \ and the + \ complex. The relative velocity shift between the two lines is v( ) 3300 km s ^ 1 , which is in the top 10 highest relative velocity shifts measured in our sample.

gmw_00012883.name: SDSSJ1202--0054
gmw_00012883.overview: SDSSJ1202--0054 is a galaxy characterized by a sharply peaked H-beta emission-line profile [arXiv:2310.00271]. In contrast, its H-alpha line profile is truncated, leading to discrepant virial mass estimates for the system [arXiv:2310.00271].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSSJ1202--0054 as an outlier in virial mass estimates, showing all three broad-line models, a sharply peaked line model, a truncated profile, and an intermediate mass estimate.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:Hbeta_outlier , we present example emission-line models of all three broad lines for SDSSJ1202--0054, one of the outliers identified due to the discrepancy between \ and based virial mass estimates, M_ / M_ 0.8 . Similar to PSS J0121 + 0347, the \ line model is sharply peaked with FWHM 1750 km s ^ 1 for the narrowest \ component. However, the \ line profile is truncated and its broad component is poorly constrained. For this target, the \ virial mass estimate is intermediate between the \ and based estimates.

gmw_00012884.name: CTS 0216
gmw_00012884.overview: CTS 0216 is a quasar with a sightline containing a strong Lyman-limit system at a redshift of 0.2925 [arXiv:1405.7405]. This system exhibits intergalactic helium II absorption, though its remaining continuum flux is too faint for detailed Hubble Space Telescope absorption studies [arXiv:1405.7405].

sources.summary: The paper finds CTS 0216 has a z=0.2925 Lyman-limit system with residual flux and intergalactic He II absorption, but the remaining continuum is too faint for HST He II studies.

quotes.quote: The sightline to CTS 0216 has a strong Lyman limit system at z=0.2925 with a column density N_ HI =10^ 17.75 cm ^ 2 that is well determined from the residual flux present below the Lyman limit break. The inset of Fig. fig:cycle17lls shows that there are fewer counts detected blueward of He 2 Ly than redward of it, indicating the presence of intergalactic He 2 absorption. However, with this low remaining continuum flux, CTS 0216 is too faint for He 2 Ly absorption studies with HST . The continuum redward of the Lyman limit break is not well described by a simple power law, but might instead show broad emission features of neon, similar to 5 of the 17 analyzed He 2 transmitting quasars (Figs. fig:he2spc_cycle17 and fig:he2spc_archive ).

gmw_00012885.name: CSO 0806
gmw_00012885.overview: CSO 0806 is a quasar with a spectrum truncated by a strong Lyman-limit system at a redshift of z=0.4119 [arXiv:1405.7405]. This intervening system has an inferred lower limit on its neutral hydrogen column density of more than 10^18.08 per square centimeter [arXiv:1405.7405].

sources.summary: The paper finds CSO 0806 is truncated by a strong z=0.4119 Lyman-limit system, with residual source photons, and uses its geocoronal profile to model contamination in science spectra.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of CSO 0806 is truncated by a strong Lyman limit system at z=0.4119 , for which we infer a lower limit on the column density of N_ HI >10^ 18.08 cm ^ 2 from the zero (in fact slightly negative) residual flux in the Lyman continuum. However, the high Lyman series transitions of the system indicate that its column density cannot be much larger than this limit. In fact, our analysis of pulse height statistics in Appendix sect:phaappendix suggests that a few residual source photons are present blueward of the Lyman break. We used the high-S/N profile of geocoronal H 1 Ly and N 1 1200 \ emission present in the COS spectrum of CSO 0806 to model the geocoronal emission in the spectra of our science targets, and thus to extend the usable spectral range for He 2 absorption analysis (Figs. fig:he2spc_cycle17 and fig:he2spc_archive ).

gmw_00012886.name: SDSS J0936 + 2927
gmw_00012886.overview: SDSS J0936 + 2927 is a quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.80 [arXiv:1405.7405]. Observations of its sightline reveal a fully saturated Gunn-Peterson trough, indicating ongoing helium reionization in the intergalactic medium [arXiv:1405.7405].

sources.summary: The paper reports large-scale strong He II absorption toward SDSS J0936 + 2927, a saturated z=2.80 sightline, and a Gunn-Peterson trough implying ongoing He II reionization.

quotes.quote: Blueward of He 2 Ly in the quasar rest frame we detect patchy intergalactic He 2 Ly absorption on a range of spatial scales, varying from large-scale strong absorption (e.g. \ 30 Mpc at z 2.8 toward SDSS J0936 + 2927) to alternating transmission and absorption features on scales of only a few Mpc.

Most of the 12 sightlines sampling the z=2.80 He 2 absorption still have _ eff,HeII 2 , except the fully saturated sightline to SDSS J0936 + 2927 ( _ eff,HeII 5.3 , sensitivity limit _ eff,HeII >4.5 ; Fig. fig:he2spc_cycle17 ).

At z 2.8 the ionization level is still quite homogeneous, but the Gunn-Peterson troughs in HE 2347 4342 and SDSS J0936 + 2927 imply x _ HeII 0.015 on 10 of the probed pathlength, indicating that He 2 reionization is still ongoing.

gmw_00012887.name: SDSS J0924 + 4852
gmw_00012887.overview: SDSS J0924 + 4852 is a helium-II transparent quasar [arXiv:1405.7405]. Its spectrum displays patchy localized absorption along with regions of high hydrogen-I and helium-II transmission [arXiv:1405.7405].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J0924 + 4852 as a He II transparent quasar with patchy localized absorption, H I-correlated substructure, and high H I plus He II transmission regions.

quotes.quote: Initial results on the two He 2 transparent quasars SDSS J0924 + 4852 and SDSS J1101 + 1053 were presented in worseck11b .

Figure fig:h1he2spc presents the coeval H 1 and He 2 Ly absorption spectra of the 6 He 2 transparent sightlines from our Cycle 17 survey, highlighting the variance in He 2 absorption at similar redshifts (e.g. \ the 2.76<z<2.86 He 2 Gunn-Peterson trough in SDSS J0936 + 2927 vs. \ the very patchy localized absorption in SDSS J0924 + 4852 over the full covered redshift range 2.66<z<2.94 ). Some He 2 transmission regions show substructure with corresponding H 1 lines, for example toward SDSS J0924 + 4852 at z 2.86 and z 2.92 . A few regions have high H 1 and He 2 transmission, e.g. \ toward SDSS J0924 + 4852 at z 2.71 and z 2.89 .

gmw_00012888.name: SDSS J1101 + 1053
gmw_00012888.overview: SDSS J1101 + 1053 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=2.68 [arXiv:1405.7405]. It is characterized as a helium-II transparent quasar that features a partial Lyman-limit system along its line of sight [arXiv:1405.7405].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J1101 + 1053 as a He II transparent quasar with high effective optical depth at z=2.68 and a newly recognized partial Lyman-limit system.

quotes.quote: Initial results on the two He 2 transparent quasars SDSS J0924 + 4852 and SDSS J1101 + 1053 were presented in worseck11b .

At z=2.68 8/10 sightlines have _ eff,HeII 2 , while SDSS J0924 + 4852 and SDSS J1101 + 1053 have _ eff,HeII 3 . Uncertain H 1 Lyman continuum absorption at z 0.3 in these two sightlines (Table tab:he2qsolist ) cannot fully account for these large _ eff,HeII values because we observe significant He 2 transmission at higher redshifts (Fig. fig:he2spc_cycle17 ).

Our improved reduction revealed a previously unnoticed partial Lyman limit system toward SDSS J1101 + 1053 ( z=0.3177 , N_ HI 10^ 16.5 cm ^ 2 ; see Table tab:he2qsolist ) that results in a modest decrease in the inferred He 2 effective optical depths compared to the initial analysis presented in worseck11b .

gmw_00012889.name: HS 0911 + 4809
gmw_00012889.overview: HS 0911 + 4809 is a quasar that was observed with the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph [arXiv:1405.7405]. Its sightline exhibits saturated He II absorption on 10 Mpc scales, as well as a transmission spike near z ~ 3.16 [arXiv:1405.7405].

sources.summary: The paper gives HS 0911 + 4809 additional COS coverage and reports saturated He II absorption on 10 Mpc scales plus a significant transmission spike near z 3.16.

quotes.quote: All quasars were observed with the COS G140L grating in the 1105 \ setup, while three (HS 0911 + 4809, SDSS J2346 0016 and SDSS J1253 + 6817) have additional coverage at <1100 , obtained in the 1280 \ setup.

At 2.7 z 3 some sightlines show saturated He 2 absorption on 10 Mpc scales (e.g. \ HE 2347 4342, Q 0302 003, HS 0911 + 4809). These long troughs are unlikely to exist in a fully reionized IGM except in high-density regions furlanetto09,shull10 . Such overdensities may be revealed through analysis of the coeval H 1 \ forest (see Section sect:he2h1 ).
Smaller but still significant spikes exist in the sightlines toward HS 0911 + 4809 ( z 3.16 ), SDSS J1253 + 6817 ( z 3.15 , z 3.33 ) and SDSS J1319 + 5202 ( z 3.45 ).

gmw_00012890.name: SDSS J1253 + 6817
gmw_00012890.overview: SDSS J1253 + 6817 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1405.7405]. Follow-up spectroscopy of the object reveals a long, shallow transmission region and low effective optical depths that indicate large-scale sightline variance [arXiv:1405.7405].
