This source was first detected at radio wavelengths by Brown (1987) and is sometimes referred to as Brown 5. Since then, it has been detected in several radio studies of the core of the Corona Australis molecular cloud (e.g. Suters et al. 1996; Forbrich et al. 2006; Choi et al. 2008; Miettinen et al. 2008; Liu et al. 2014). The radio source is time variable, with centimeter flux densities ranging from 0.2 mJy (Forbrich et al. 2006) to 4.4 mJy (Suters et al. 1996). However, it did not show evidence of variability in the five epochs we observed it.

CHLT 2 is associated with a faint (K = 16.4) near-infrared source that would be a brown dwarf if it lies in the CrA cloud and is < 10 ^7 yr old (Feigelson et al. 1998). Miettinen et al. (2008) note that their radio properties, in particular its persistent strong emission, do not support the brown dwarf classification. Liu et al. (2014) propose that this source is a radio galaxy.

gmw_00013147.name: PG1148+549
gmw_00013147.overview: PG1148+549 is a quasar located at an emission redshift of 0.9759 [arXiv:1710.05023]. Its line of sight is considered atypical because it contains three individual, strong absorber systems [arXiv:1710.05023].

sources.summary: The paper treats PG1148+549 as an atypical quasar sightline containing three strong absorbers, argues its absorber incidence is not cosmologically representative, and folds it into the direct-detection constraints.

quotes.quote: Along the line of sight towards PG1148+549+549 at z_ em = 0.9759 (part of our analysis sample, see Table  tab:sightlines ), one of the spectra with the highest overall S/N, there are not just one, but three individual, strong (log N _ > 13.7) \ absorber systems at redshifts z _ abs = 0.68381, 0.70152, and 0.72478. This separation of z=0.04 constitutes 7200 km/s if treated as a peculiar motion, or a comoving distance of 83 h ^ 1 Mpc if treated as part of the Hubble Flow. For details of these detections see Meiring2013 . Limiting oneself to the portion of our sample which has sufficiently high signal-to-noise to confidently detect these systems (S/N > 20 per pixel), one retains most of our analysis path ( z=0.43 ) for this spectrum.

Also the redshifts are typical of our sample, so based on these most simple criteria PG1148+549 is unexceptional. However, since all other sightlines of such quality show only one or no such absorbers (and only 5 in total), it seems that the sightline towards PG1148+549 is atypical. We quantify the likelihood that this state of affairs arises by chance and in light of our data analysis of 26 sightlines.

gmw_00013148.name: SAMI ID 417424
gmw_00013148.overview: SAMI ID 417424 is a galaxy characterized by a large ellipticity of 0.711 [arXiv:2212.04729]. Its global integral field spectroscopy shows a double-horn shape when reconstructed using an elliptical aperture [arXiv:2212.04729].

sources.summary: The paper uses SAMI ID 417424 to show how large ellipticity and low-S/N minor-axis spaxels affect global IFS spectra; an elliptical aperture recovers the double-horn shape.

quotes.quote: The global spectra created using all spaxels agree well with the spectra created using only those with S/N_ 5 for the top rows, whereas this is not the case for the galaxy in the bottom row. This object (SAMI ID 417424) has large ellipticity ( e=0.711 ), meaning that there are spaxels with low signal off of the galaxy plane on the minor axis that have uncertain kinematics.

Considering the aperture spectra, using either just the S/N_ 5 aperture or a circular one results in excess flux density at =0 , which is most evident in the global kinematic spectra where each spaxel is weighted evenly. In contrast, in the global spectrum, the weighting typically up-weights the major axis and down-weights the minor axis, recovering more of the double horn shape. However, the double-horn shape is recovered in both the IFS spectra when using an elliptical aperture.

gmw_00013149.name: Galaxy A
gmw_00013149.overview: Galaxy A is characterized by an elongated appearance and features strong spiral arms [arXiv:2212.04729]. The galaxy also hosts a galaxy-wide disturbance, with its ionized gas tracing the same kinematics and spatial distribution as its other components [arXiv:2212.04729].

sources.summary: Galaxy A is presented as a case where global IFS spectra match the global spectrum, with a galaxy-wide disturbance, strong spiral arms, elongated appearance, and closely matching spectra.

quotes.quote: The first two rows (galaxies A and B) show cases where both global IFS spectra were identified to match the global \ spectrum. Galaxy A appears to host a galaxy-wide disturbance; the optical image shows that it has strong spiral arms, giving it an elongated appearance , and all three global spectra match closely.
In these galaxies, it seems that the ionised gas traces the same kinematics and spatial distribution as the .

gmw_00013150.name: galaxy B
gmw_00013150.overview: Galaxy B features symmetric, double-horn global spectra [arXiv:2212.04729]. Although a bar dominates the galaxy's photometric position angle, its gas distribution is axisymmetric and its kinematics are undisturbed [arXiv:2212.04729].

sources.summary: The paper describes galaxy B as having symmetric double-horn global spectra, a bar-dominated photometric position angle, axisymmetric gas distribution, and undisturbed kinematics matching the neutral gas profile.

quotes.quote: The first two rows (galaxies A and B) show cases where both global IFS spectra were identified to match the global \ spectrum.

The global spectra for galaxy B are all symmetric, double-horn profiles. While the bar in this galaxy dominates the photometric position angle, the distribution is axisymmetric, and the kinematics are undisturbed. In these galaxies, it seems that the ionised gas traces the same kinematics and spatial distribution as the .

gmw_00013151.name: galaxy D
gmw_00013151.overview: Galaxy D is characterized by symmetric global kinematics [arXiv:2212.04729]. However, it exhibits an off-centre or clumpy spatial distribution that causes a highly asymmetric global spectrum [arXiv:2212.04729].

sources.summary: Galaxy D is an example where a symmetric kinematic spectrum does not match the global spectrum; an off-centre distribution may create a highly asymmetric profile despite symmetric kinematics.

quotes.quote: In the third and fourth rows (galaxies C and D) of Fig. fig:examples , we show galaxies where only one of the IFS spectra match the global \ spectrum.

Conversely, galaxy D has a symmetric global kinematic spectrum that does not match the , but the off-centre distribution causes a highly asymmetric global spectrum that appears to better match the . Indeed, the relative spatial distributions of both the and \ could be lopsided in this galaxy while their kinematics are symmetric. Alternatively, this could just be an offset or clumpy distribution in the galaxy with no connection to the \ distribution. Our inability to decompose the \ spectrum into kinematic and flux-weighted components complicates this interpretation, and although we have categorised this galaxy as an \ match based on its shape, the low of the \ spectrum means that there is a chance that it could also be consistent with the global kinematic spectrum.

gmw_00013152.name: B2013+370
gmw_00013152.overview: B2013+370 is a compact radio source with a flat spectrum and a flux density of 2 Jy at 1 GHz [arXiv:astro-ph/0301424]. Its multiwavelength properties resemble those of blazars, making it a possible counterpart to the source 3EG J2016+3657 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301424].

sources.summary: The paper identifies B2013+370 as a compact flat-spectrum 2 Jy radio source, says its multiwavelength properties resemble EGRET blazars, and treats it as a possible counterpart to 3EG J2016+3657.

quotes.quote: Although it has been suggested that SNR G74.9+1.2 (CTB 87) may be interacting with molecular clouds (Huang et al. 1983, Huang and Thaddeus 1986), the coincident 3EG J2016+3657 source has been proposed as a counterpart of the blazar-like radio source G74.87+1.22 (B2013+370) (Halpern et al. 2001a, Mukherjee et al. 2000). B2013+370 is a compact, flat spectrum, 2 Jy radio source at 1 GHz. Its multiwavelength properties were compiled by Mukherjee et al. (2000), and since they resemble other blazars detected by EGRET, B2013+370 is an interesting possible counterpart for this 3EG source.

gmw_00013153.name: ID 1470
gmw_00013153.overview: ID 1470 is an interacting high-redshift system consisting of three closely separated objects [arXiv:2207.13527]. Two of these components are plausible high-redshift members at z ~ 7, while the third is likely a low-redshift interloper [arXiv:2207.13527].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 1470 as an interacting high-redshift system with three close components: two plausible z~7 members and one likely low-redshift interloper.

quotes.quote: Four of our 19 galaxies are consistent with being interacting systems (21 10 ; ID 6, 1470, 2911, and 2936). The first galaxy likely has a close and faint companion lying within its segmentation map. In the other two cases, the interactions are outside of the segmentation maps, thus we can actually check the photometric redshift of the companions. We also center the cutouts in the asymmetry center of the interacting systems in these cases.

In the galaxy ID 1470 , we notice three closely separated objects with a relative distance of less than 1.0 The two galaxies on the left have a photometric redshift of 7.6 and 6.9 , consistent within their uncertainties, while the object on the right is likely an interloper with a z _ phot =0.55 .

gmw_00013154.name: ID 2911
gmw_00013154.overview: ID 2911 is a bright interacting galaxy system located at a photometric redshift of approximately 7 [arXiv:2207.13527]. The system's morphology changes with wavelength, exhibiting a higher smoothness and lower shape asymmetry at longer wavelengths [arXiv:2207.13527].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID 2911 as a bright z~7 interacting system whose morphology changes with wavelength, with higher smoothness and lower shape asymmetry at longer wavelengths.

quotes.quote: Galaxy ID 2911 , an interacting system, has a higher smoothness and lower shape asymmetry at longer wavelengths, while galaxy ID 2236 has an opposite trend for the smoothness and shape asymmetry, and also a >3 significant morphological diversity as a function of of Gini and M _ 20 .
Four of our 19 galaxies are consistent with being interacting systems (21 10 ; ID 6, 1470, 2911, and 2936).
ID 2911 is a bright interacting system at (photometric) redshift 7 .

gmw_00013155.name: X19
gmw_00013155.overview: X19 is an X-ray quasar located at a redshift of approximately z = 1.29 [arXiv:1107.5039]. Its spectral energy distribution is well-matched by a quasar template, and its X-ray spectrum is fit by an absorbed power law or a bremsstrahlung model [arXiv:1107.5039].

sources.summary: The paper identifies X19 as a z = 1.29 QSO, with SED fitting favoring a QSO template over a brown dwarf and X-ray spectral fits preferring power-law or bremsstrahlung models.

quotes.quote: The X19 spectrum is about equally well fit by an absorbed power law with = 1.7 as by a bremsstrahlung model with kT = 11 keV. A single-temperature blackbody is a worse fit to the data than these two models. There are insufficient data to perform two-component fits to the spectrum.

The best-fitting galaxy or QSO template, a QSO1 from the SWIRE template library swire redshifted to z = 1.29^ +0.16 _ 0.07 , had =1.47 ; the best-fitting stellar model, a brown dwarf from chabrier:00 , was a poor match to the data ( = 24 ). In Fig. fig:x19sed , we show the SED of X19 along with the multiwavelength UV/OIR photometry. The X-ray light-curve and spectrum, and the positional coincidence of the X-ray and UV/OIR sources, make for a convincing argument that X19 is a QSO at z = 1.29 .

gmw_00013156.name: J1654+3508
gmw_00013156.overview: J1654+3508 is a transient source originally identified by the Catalina Real-Time Transient Survey [arXiv:2305.13371]. Its spectrum exhibits a broad emission line, indicating that the object is likely a quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.4 [arXiv:2305.13371].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1654+3508 as a CRTS transient with no follow-up studies, classifies its broad emission-line spectrum as a likely z 2.4 quasar, and notes uncertain positive parallax.

quotes.quote: J1654+3508 This was identified as a transient by CRTS (MLS 160613:165449+350804); no follow-up studies are reported in the literature. The spectrum shows a broad emission line typical of a quasar, which is likely to be Ly at z 2.4 . We note that this quasar has a large positive parallax, albeit with a large uncertainty.

gmw_00013157.name: QSO 03027 0010
gmw_00013157.overview: QSO 03027 0010 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=2.808 with an apparent magnitude of V=21.7 [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374]. Its spectrum reveals a strong associated absorption line system at z=2.804, as well as a potential magnesium II absorber at z=0.750 [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374].

sources.summary: The paper reports QSO 03027 0010 as a newly discovered z=2.808 quasar near Q 0302 003, with associated absorption systems, V=21.7, and a local hardening of the ionizing background.

quotes.quote: There is a strong associated absorption line system in the spectrum of QSO 03027 0010 at z_ abs =2.804 0.001 visible in Ly , N v and C iv . A C iv system is present at z_ abs =2.696 that is also visible in Si iv and that corresponds to a strong Ly absorption line in the Ly forest. Moreover, a Mg ii absorber may be present at z_ abs =0.750 .

The measurements in the spectrum of QSO 03027 0010 are further affected by the associated absorption system. Having these caveats in mind, we adopt a redshift of z=2.290 0.002 for QSO 03027 0027 and a redshift of z=2.808 0.004 for QSO 03027 0010. Apparent magnitudes of both quasars were derived based on the available spectrophotometry, yielding V=21.7 for QSO 03027 0010 and V=19.9 for QSO 03027 0027.
In particular, our newly discovered QSO 03027 0010 shows such a hardness increase despite being associated with an overdensity in the H i Lyman forest.

gmw_00013158.name: QSO 03027 0027
gmw_00013158.overview: QSO 03027 0027 is a high-redshift quasar with an adopted redshift of 2.290 and an apparent magnitude of V=19.9 [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374]. Its spectrum reveals a strong metal-line absorption system at redshift 2.115 and a possible weaker carbon IV absorption system at redshift 2.243 [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374].

sources.summary: The paper confirms QSO 03027 0027 as a quasar, reporting a strong z=2.115 metal-line system, possible weaker C IV absorption, adopted redshift z=2.290, and V=19.9.

quotes.quote: QSO 03027 0027 shows a strong metal line system at z_ abs =2.115 , easily detectable in Mg ii , C iv , Si iv and C ii . Another weaker C iv system may be present at z_ abs =2.243 .

The redshift measurement based on the Mg ii line in QSO 03027 0027 is inaccurate due to the decreasing resolving power of the grism at the longest recordable wavelengths. Consequently, the redshifts have to be measured in high-ionization lines which suffer from systematic blueshifts gaskell82,tytler92,mcintosh99 . The measurements in the spectrum of QSO 03027 0010 are further affected by the associated absorption system. Having these caveats in mind, we adopt a redshift of z=2.290 0.002 for QSO 03027 0027 and a redshift of z=2.808 0.004 for QSO 03027 0010. Apparent magnitudes of both quasars were derived based on the available spectrophotometry, yielding V=21.7 for QSO 03027 0010 and V=19.9 for QSO 03027 0027.

gmw_00013159.name: Q 0302 003
gmw_00013159.overview: Q 0302 003 is a high-redshift background quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374]. Based on low-ionization lines, the systemic redshift of the quasar is determined to be z=3.285 [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374].

sources.summary: The paper treats Q 0302 003 as the STIS-observed background quasar, revises its systemic redshift to z=3.285 from low-ionization lines, and notes the SDSS pipeline redshift was too high.

quotes.quote: Our FORS2 spectrum of Q 0302 003 covers the wavelength range from the UV cutoff to 6350 and has a S/N of 75 in the quasar continuum, so we can measure the positions of the lines Ly + O vi , Ly , N v , O i + Si ii , C ii and Si iv + O iv ] . We base our redshift measurement on the low-ionization lines O i + Si ii and C ii , which should yield the best possible estimate of the systemic redshift. We get a redshift of z=3.285 0.001 for the O i + Si ii line and a redshift of z=3.284 0.001 for the C ii line, which is consistent with the value of 3.286 originally obtained by sargent89 .

The sky region around Q 0302 003 was targeted by SDSS, and Q 0302 003 was observed spectroscopically. We checked the SDSS DR3 database for the spectroscopic redshift of Q 0302 003 and found the redshift to be too high, z_ SDSS 3.295 0.001 . Given this large offset of z=0.009 , we obtained the SDSS spectrum of Q 0302 003 from the SDSS data archive and recovered the redshift values for the low-ionization lines O i + Si ii ( z=3.286 0.002 ) and C ii ( z=3.284 0.002 ).

We conclude that the SDSS redshift assignment for Q 0302 003 is incorrect and adopt a redshift of z=3.285 for this quasar in the following. HST/STIS data Q 0302 003 was one of the few high-redshift quasars observed successfully in the He ii Ly forest below 303.78 \ rest frame wavelength with HST and its Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph at a resolution of 1.8 \ hereafter refered to as H00 heap00 .

gmw_00013160.name: QSO 03020 0014
gmw_00013160.overview: QSO 03020 0014 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately z=3.05 [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374]. The quasar's high luminosity and spectral characteristics favor a transverse proximity effect, which is associated with a closely coinciding He II void [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374].

sources.summary: The paper identifies QSO 03020 0014 (QSO A) with the He II void near z=3.05, saying its redshift, luminosity, and spectral-hardness fit favor a transverse proximity effect.

quotes.quote: Our estimate of redshift and redshift error for QSO 03020 0014 (QSO A) agrees with the values given in J03.

J03 found the closely coinciding quasar QSO 03020 0014 (QSO A) and argued that this QSO most likely was responsible for the void, although they invoked some modest degree of anisotropic radiation in order to explain an apparent shift between the QSO at z=3.050 0.003 and their stated peak of void A at z=3.056 . From our measurement the peak of void A is at redshift z=3.052 which is perfectly consistent with the QSO being located symmetric to the void.

J03 associated QSO A with void A mainly by showing that such an association has a very small probability of chance occurrence, and that the luminosity of QSO A is high enough to account for the void. Figure diff_eta shows clearly that most parts of void A can be fitted with 25 50 , which clearly favours a transverse proximity effect from a quasar.

gmw_00013161.name: Q 0301 005
gmw_00013161.overview: Q 0301 005 is a bright, high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=3.231 [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374]. The object is a very luminous source of hard ionizing radiation that may contribute to a transverse proximity effect in its cosmic neighborhood [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374].

sources.summary: The paper describes Q 0301 005 (QSO C) as a bright z=3.231 quasar whose hard radiation may contribute to the low-redshift end of Q 0302 003's He II proximity zone.

quotes.quote: Q 0301 005 (QSO C) is a bright QSO known since long. It was observed by SDSS and we obtained its spectrum from the SDSS data archive. Previous redshift determinations were based on high-ionization lines sargent89 , so its redshift of z=3.223 measured by sargent89 may have been underestimated. The SDSS spectrum of this QSO has sufficient S/N to measure the low-ionization lines O i + Si ii and C ii , yielding a redshift of z=3.231 for this object in agreement with the SDSS redshift assignment.

A possible additional contributor of hard ionizing photons could be the very luminous QSO C, intercepting the low- z end of the proximity effect region of Q 0302 003. Figures forestratio and diff_eta provide evidence for a very hard radiation field at just the redshift of QSO C which then, towards Q 0302 003, becomes somewhat softer again before finally hardening in the inner proximity effect zone. We suggest that the He ii void at z>3.22 is really generated by the combined actions of a transverse proximity effect of QSO C and a line of sight effect of Q 0302 003.

gmw_00013162.name: Q 0302-D113
gmw_00013162.overview: Q 0302-D113 is a very faint foreground quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374]. It has an estimated R-band magnitude of 24.6, and its spectrum is currently unavailable [arXiv:astro-ph/0601374].

sources.summary: The paper describes Q 0302-D113 (QSO D) as a very faint foreground quasar without an available spectrum and finds only suggestive, noise-consistent He II excess near its redshift.

quotes.quote: Q 0302-D113 (QSO D) was published by steidel03 after the compilation of the veron03 catalog. No spectrum is available for this very faint ( R 24.6 ) object. Its V magnitude in Table foregroundquasars is estimated from R .

However, we see also that Fig. diff_eta suggests a drop down to 100 again at exactly the redshift of a QSO near the line of sight. This corresponds to a small spike of excess flux in the Gunn-Peterson trough (see Fig. he2h1trans ) which is formally still consistent with the noise level. While we therefore cannot really claim to have detected another case of the transverse proximity effect, we cannot exclude it either, and the coincidence is certainly suggestive.

gmw_00013163.name: host galaxy of GRB081008
gmw_00013163.overview: The host galaxy of GRB081008 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.967 [arXiv:1909.00622]. Observations with the Gemini-South GMOS-South spectrograph detected the galaxy, though Hubble Space Telescope F160W imaging could not confirm its detection [arXiv:1909.00622].

sources.summary: The paper says the host galaxy of GRB081008 has a literature column-density estimate matching the authors' fits, a Gemini-South GMOS-S redshift of z=1.967, and no confirmed HST F160W detection.

quotes.quote: GRB081008 Beside the X-ray spectra fitting, there is a column density estimate in the literature for the host galaxy of GRB081008, which is in agreement with our estimates. An associated host galaxy to this GRB was detected with the Gemini-South GMOS-South spectrograph at a redshift of z = 1.967 cucchiara2008a, Cucchiara2015 . derived an intrinsic hydrogen column density of 0.13 0.03 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 from the UVES/VLT optical spectra of the afterglow. However, could not confirm the detection of the host galaxy with HST F160W imaging. Considering that this galaxy is at a relatively high redshift, some of the X-ray absorption could be due to intervening IGM clouds (e.g. Campana2010, Campana2012, Starling2013 ).

gmw_00013164.name: J102358.2 + 003826
gmw_00013164.overview: J102358.2 + 003826 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.449 [arXiv:2305.14509]. It exhibits resolved extended emission with secondary lobes that affect the overall root-mean-square noise level of observations [arXiv:2305.14509].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J102358.2 + 003826 as a z = 0.449 galaxy with resolved extended emission and secondary lobes that affected the VLA noise, requiring cleaning and spectral modeling.

quotes.quote: J102358.2 + 003826 is a well-known galaxy at redshift z = 0.449 ahn14 with a resolved extended emission whose secondary lobes heavily affect the overall rms noise level deller15 . To remove the problematic contribution of J102358.2 + 003826, we first cleaned the image using a mask with the tclean task. We set the nterms parameter equal to 2 to take into account the spectral properties of the galaxy over the wide bandwidth of the observation deller15 . This step provided the model visibilities of J102358.2 + 003826.

gmw_00013165.name: ID 67
gmw_00013165.overview: ID 67 is a star-forming galaxy that harbors a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1303.6282]. The galaxy's host morphology features a bright, compact nucleus surrounded by a faint extended structure, and it exhibits strong radio variability with flaring bursts [arXiv:1303.6282].

sources.summary: ID 67 is the strongest variable source, with flaring radio bursts, a compact extended host morphology, and evidence for a star-forming galaxy containing a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: ID 67: This source has a mean 1.4 GHz flux density of 136 9 Jy and shows the strongest variation among all seven variables. Two flaring bursts are evident from the light curve (Figure Fig:lightCurves ), one of them lasting for about 25 days, and the flux density of the other increasing by at least a factor of 2.5 in 12 days.

The photometric redshift of z=1.005 bon12 implies a mean radio luminosity of log L_R = 30.6 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 . The radio spectral index (defined as where S _ ^ ) between 1.4 GHz and 5.5 GHz is +0.23 hhl+12 . The morphology of the host galaxy as seen from the HST image appears to be that of a bright, compact nucleus surrounded by faint extended structure. The half-light radius of the galaxy according to the GALFIT parameters from the GEMS catalog is 620 10 pc.
Taken together, the radio luminosity, radio spectral index, optical morphology and mid-to-far-infrared flux densities argue that ID 67 is a star-forming galaxy harboring a low-luminosity AGN.

gmw_00013166.name: ID 239
gmw_00013166.overview: ID 239 is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.266 [arXiv:1303.6282]. It harbors a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus and exhibits a radio light curve that gradually rises and declines over approximately 50 days [arXiv:1303.6282].

sources.summary: ID 239 shows a rise and decline in radio flux, has a bright-nucleus HST morphology, and is interpreted as a star-forming galaxy harboring a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: ID 239: This source has a mean 1.4 GHz flux density of 1822 27 Jy. This source is also found in the kfm+08 and naa+06 catalogs where the flux density is 2030 43 and 1640 20 Jy respectively. Its light-curve shows a gradual increase in flux density, over a period of about 50 days, followed by an equally gradual decline.

The redshift z=0.266 tvg+09 implies a mean radio luminosity of log L_R = 30.5 (cgs). The HST cutout (Figure Fig:cutouts ) reveals just a bright nucleus surrounded by faint extended structure, the half-light radius being 3.2 0.5 kpc. The K-corrected mid-to-far-infrared spectral luminosities, L_ (5.8 m ) = 3.6 10^8 L_ and L_ (24 m ) < 1.0 10^ 9 L_ , and the mid-infrared colors (Figure Fig:IRACColors ) show that the host is a star-forming galaxy. Taken together, the radio luminosity, radio spectral index, optical morphology and mid-to-far-infrared flux densities argue that ID 239 is a star-forming galaxy harboring a low-luminosity AGN.

gmw_00013167.name: ID 621
gmw_00013167.overview: ID 621 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.107 [arXiv:1303.6282]. Its optical morphology reveals a bright nucleus surrounded by diffuse emission, and its mid-infrared properties suggest it is also a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1303.6282].

sources.summary: ID 621 brightens then declines, has a bright-nucleus HST morphology and nuclear radio position, and is interpreted as an AGN that is also a star-forming galaxy.

quotes.quote: ID 621: This source has a mean 1.4 GHz flux density of 497 10 Jy. The first epoch in its light-curve reveals an initial brightening of the source followed by a decline and subsequent small-amplitude variations.

The photometric redshift of z=1.107 bon12 implies a mean radio luminosity of log L_R = 31.2 (cgs). The HST image reveals a bright nucleus surrounded by diffuse emission. The half-light radius is 3 kpc. The radio position is consistent with the nuclear source. Taken together, the radio luminosity, spectral index and optical morphology argue that ID 621 is an AGN.

Additionally, the K-corrected 5.8 m luminosity of 5.0 10^9 L_ , the 24 m luminosity upper limit of 6.8 10^ 10 L_ , and the mid-infrared colors (Figure Fig:IRACColors ) suggest that this is also a star-forming galaxy.

gmw_00013168.name: ID 628
gmw_00013168.overview: ID 628 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a photometric redshift of z=0.685 [arXiv:1303.6282]. Optical imaging reveals a bright nucleus surrounded by disk-like diffuse emission, while mid-to-far-infrared properties suggest the active nucleus is embedded within a normal star-forming or starburst galaxy [arXiv:1303.6282].

sources.summary: ID 628 has a steadily varying radio light curve, nuclear radio and HST morphology, and is classified as an AGN embedded within a normal star-forming or starburst galaxy.

quotes.quote: ID 628: This source has a mean 1.4 GHz flux density of 1.34 0.02 mJy. The light-curve indicates a steady increase of the quiescent emission to maximum flux density, followed by a steady decline.

The photometric redshift of z=0.685 naa+06 implies a mean radio luminosity of log L_R = 31.2 (cgs). The HST image reveals a bright nucleus surrounded by disk-like diffuse emission, the half-light radius being 10 kpc. The radio position is consistent with the nuclear source. On the basis of departure from radio-FIR correlation naa+06 classify this source as an AGN, which is consistent with our flat radio spectral index and radio luminosity.

Additionally, the K-corrected 5.8 m luminosity of 3.0 10^9 L_ , the 24 m luminosity of 3.7 10^ 10 L_ , and the mid-infrared colors (Figure Fig:IRACColors ) suggest that this is a normal star-forming or a starburst galaxy. Taken together, the radio and mid-to-far-infrared properties along with the optical morphology argue that ID 628 is an AGN embedded within a star-forming galaxy.

gmw_00013169.name: SN 2017hcc host galaxy
gmw_00013169.overview: The SN 2017hcc host galaxy is an anonymous spiral galaxy coincident with the ultraviolet source GALEX 2674128878581058535 [arXiv:2009.14215]. It exhibits a low upper limit for host galaxy reddening, estimated at an E(B-V) of 0.016 mag [arXiv:2009.14215].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the SN 2017hcc host as an anonymous spiral galaxy coincident with GALEX 2674128878581058535; the supernova lies 1.6 kpc from its nucleus and the host reddening is small.

quotes.quote: SN 2017hcc is located about 0.5 arcsec south and 4.5 arcsec east of the center of its spiral host galaxy (a foreground star or cluster projected near the galaxy nucleus would shift the centroid of the galaxy light slightly to the east in lower-resolution images). SN 2017hcc is therefore projected a total of 4.53 arcsec from center, or 1.6 kpc from the nucleus at the adopted distance of 73 Mpc. Although the host galaxy is anonymous and does not appear in the NASA Extragalactic Database, according to SIMBAD http://simbad.u-strasbg.fr/simbad/ , it is coincident with the UV source GALEX 2674128878581058535. The coordinates listed for this GALEX source are offset a few arcsec from the apparent center of the galaxy. SN 2017hcc appears to be located on the leading edge of an inner spiral arm or bar, although the ground-based angular resolution is insufficient to determine its proximity to any dust lanes or star forming regions.

For the SN 2017hcc host galaxy ISM absorption component of Na i D, we measure smaller equivalent widths of EW(D _2 ) = 0.026 ( 0.005) , EW(D _1 ) = 0.014 ( 0.004) , and EW(D _2 +D _1 ) = 0.039 ( 0.006) . Using the relations from dovi12 , these would translate to E(B-V) values of 0.014 mag (D _2 ), 0.019 mag (D _1 ), and 0.016 mag (D _1 +D _2 ).
We therefore regard E(B-V) =0.016 mag as an upper limit to the host galaxy reddening for SN 2017hcc, corresponding to A_V 0.05 mag.

gmw_00013170.name: PID-66
gmw_00013170.overview: PID-66 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=1.185 [arXiv:1303.5556]. It is identified as a secure Compton-thick candidate with a likely transmission-dominated spectrum and a high rest-frame FeK equivalent width [arXiv:1303.5556].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PID-66 as one of four secure Compton-thick AGN candidates, supported by high FeK equivalent width, joint spectral fits, and a likely transmission-dominated spectrum.

quotes.quote: Four out of nine sources (PID-66, 144, 147, 324) present high rest-frame FeK EW (see table 1). These are suggestive of high absorbing column densities likely corresponding to Compton-thick AGN.

In agreement with the spectral analysis, presented in the previous section, four sources (PID-66, 144, 147 and 324) present FeK lines with high (rest-frame) EW ( > 0.4 keV). Therefore, the joint and analysis corroborates that these four sources have a good probability for being Compton-thick.

Two of the sources are most likely transmission dominated (PID-66 and PID-144) while the other two are reflection dominated (PID-147 and PID-324) . Although our sample is by no means statistically complete, it represents the most well studied examples of Compton-thick AGN at moderate to high redshifts. In particular, the redshifts of the four most secure candidate Compton-thick sources are z=1.185, 1.222, 1.53 and 3.7.

gmw_00013171.name: PID-147
gmw_00013171.overview: PID-147 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=1.53 [arXiv:1303.5556]. It is identified as a secure Compton-thick candidate with a reflection-dominated spectrum and a high rest-frame iron K equivalent width [arXiv:1303.5556].

sources.summary: The paper treats PID-147 as one of four secure Compton-thick AGN candidates, with high FeK equivalent width, joint spectral-fit support, and a likely reflection-dominated spectrum.

quotes.quote: Four out of nine sources (PID-66, 144, 147, 324) present high rest-frame FeK EW (see table 1). These are suggestive of high absorbing column densities likely corresponding to Compton-thick AGN.

In agreement with the spectral analysis, presented in the previous section, four sources (PID-66, 144, 147 and 324) present FeK lines with high (rest-frame) EW ( > 0.4 keV). Therefore, the joint and analysis corroborates that these four sources have a good probability for being Compton-thick.

Two of the sources are most likely transmission dominated (PID-66 and PID-144) while the other two are reflection dominated (PID-147 and PID-324) . Although our sample is by no means statistically complete, it represents the most well studied examples of Compton-thick AGN at moderate to high redshifts. In particular, the redshifts of the four most secure candidate Compton-thick sources are z=1.185, 1.222, 1.53 and 3.7.

gmw_00013172.name: PKS B1322-110
gmw_00013172.overview: PKS B1322-110 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z=3.007 [arXiv:2009.06447]. The source exhibits intra-hour and intra-day radio variability [arXiv:2009.06447, arXiv:1906.01141] caused by scintillation from anisotropic plasma inhomogeneities [arXiv:1906.01141].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS B1322-110 as an intra-hour-variable flat-spectrum radio quasar at z=3.007; Gemini imaging found excess narrowband flux, and spectroscopy attributes it to redshifted He II rather than Galactic H emission.

quotes.quote: Observations with the Australia Telescope Compact Array revealed intra-hour variations in the radio source PKS B1322-110 bignall2019 . As part of an optical follow-up, we obtained Gemini H and H continuum (H C) images of the PKS B1322-110 field. A robust 19 detection of PKS B1322-110 in the H H C image prompted us to obtain the first optical spectrum of PKS B1322-110. With the Gemini spectrum we determine that PKS B1322-110 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar at a redshift of z=3.007 0.002 .

Gemini narrowband imaging resulted in an apparent H detection toward the fast scintillator PKS B1322-110, and we therefore undertook follow-up spectroscopy with Gemini. The spectroscopy demonstrated that Galactic H emission was not responsible for the signal that we observed in our images. In part the signal we observed in our imaging data is due to He ii emission in the quasar redshifted to the wavelength of Galactic H .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS B1322 110 as a radio quasar with intra-day GHz variability and tracks an annual scintillation cycle attributed to anisotropic plasma scattering near Spica.

quotes.quote: PKS B1322 110 is a radio quasar that is located only 8^ .5 in angular separation from the bright B star Spica. It exhibits intra-day variability in its flux density at GHz frequencies attributed to scintillations from plasma inhomogeneities. We have tracked the rate of scintillation of this source for over a year with the Australia Telescope Compact Array, recording a strong annual cycle that includes a near-standstill in August and another in December. The cycle is consistent with scattering by highly anisotropic plasma microstructure, and we fit our data to that model in order to determine the kinematic parameters of the plasma.

gmw_00013173.name: J0502 + 1338
gmw_00013173.overview: J0502 + 1338 is a blazar monitored by the Owens Valley Radio Observatory [arXiv:2308.09626]. It exhibits slow variability on the order of years, with a variability timescale that exceeds the total observing time span [arXiv:2308.09626].

sources.summary: The paper uses J0502 + 1338 as an OVRO blazar example with slow year-scale variations; its structure function fit is shown, and its variability timescale exceeds the observing span.

quotes.quote: Some sources like J0721 + 7120 (lower panel of fig lightcurves and SF ) exhibit variability on timescales on the order of weeks and months, while other sources such as J0502 + 1338 (upper panel of fig lightcurves and SF ) show slower variations on the order of years.

Fig lightcurves and SF shows two examples of the estimated SF and the corresponding SF model fits for the sources J0721 + 7120 (lower panel of fig lightcurves and SF ) and J0502 + 1338 (upper panel of fig lightcurves and SF ).
In 493 out of 1157 sources in our sample, the variability timescales are longer than the total observing time span, such that the SF does not saturate (e.g., J0502 + 1338).

gmw_00013174.name: 2FGL J0031.0+0724
gmw_00013174.overview: The active galactic nucleus 2FGL J0031.0+0724 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1210.3852]. Its high-energy lightcurve is consistent with a temporally variable source, and it is associated with faint radio and X-ray counterparts [arXiv:1210.3852].

sources.summary: The paper revisits 2FGL J0031.0+0724, finding no 42-month exponential cutoff, possible high-energy variability, no angular extent, and a faint radio/X-ray counterpart whose SED and WISE photometry support a BL Lac origin.

quotes.quote: 2FGL J0031.0+0724. The 24-month Fermi LAT data of this source has been intensively studied in Paper I. The analysis carried out here does not demonstrate a preference for an exponential cutoff in the 42-month data set. With a significance of 2 , the lightcurve of high-energy photons (10--300 GeV) is consistent with a temporally variable source, and no indication for angular extended emission was found. In Paper I, we already claimed a possible association of the source with a faint radio source (12 mJy), positionally coincident with a faint X-ray source, f^ unabs (0.2 2 keV ) 2.1 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . The spectral energy distribution (SED) is consistent with a BL Lac origin (see Paper I), which is supported by the photometric infrared data of WISE J003119.70+072453.6.

gmw_00013175.name: 2FGL J0143.6 5844
gmw_00013175.overview: 2FGL J0143.6 5844 is a blazar whose infrared data and multi-wavelength spectral energy distribution support a BL Lacertae object classification [arXiv:1210.3852]. The source exhibits steady, point-like gamma-ray emission, while its ultraviolet and optical data indicate variable emission [arXiv:1210.3852].

sources.summary: The paper finds 2FGL J0143.6 5844 has a preferred exponential-cutoff gamma-ray spectrum, steady point-like emission, and multiwavelength radio/X-ray/UV/IR associations consistent with a BL Lac scenario.

quotes.quote: 2FGL J0143.6 5844. The 42-month data indicates the ray spectrum to be preferentially fit with an exponential cutoff with 3 . Furthermore, the lightcurve is preferred to be steady, but the ray emission is consistent with a point-source. Multi-wavelength observations show the source to be associated with a 27 mJy radio source (SUMSS), positionally coincident with the bright X-ray source SWIFT J014347.3 584551, f^ unabs (0.2 2 keV ) 1.4 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . Together with the simultaneously measured X-ray flux, the UV data seems to indicate variable emission when compared with the optical data. The infrared data supports a BL Lac scenario, which is also consistent with the multi-wavelength SED, see figure subfig:sed_J0143.6 .

gmw_00013176.name: 2FGL J0305.0 1602
gmw_00013176.overview: 2FGL J0305.0 1602 is a variable gamma-ray source positionally associated with PKS J0305 1608 [arXiv:1210.3852]. Its sparse photometric infrared data is consistent with a BL Lacertae object scenario, though its high radio flux is not typical of a high-energy peaked BL Lac [arXiv:1210.3852].

sources.summary: The paper treats 2FGL J0305.0 1602 as a variable gamma-ray source associated with PKS J0305 1608; its sparse infrared data weakly fit a BL Lac scenario, but its bright radio flux argues against an HBL interpretation.

quotes.quote: 2FGL J0305.0 1602. While the ray data shows initial indication (at the 2.5 level) for a spectral cutoff at 10 GeV , the temporal photon distribution of the source excludes a constant flux with 99 confidence. Multi-wavelength searches indicate the source to be positionally associated with PKS J0305 1608, showing a radio flux at the Jy level (NVSS) and an X-ray flux of f^ unabs (0.2 2 keV ) 2.3 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . The photometric infrared data is sparsely consistent with a BL Lac scenario, while the high radio flux is not in accordance with the expectation from a high-energy peaked BL Lac (see figure subfig:sed_J0305.0 ).

gmw_00013177.name: 2FGL J0338.2+1306
gmw_00013177.overview: 2FGL J0338.2+1306 is an active galactic nucleus with a multi-wavelength emission profile that suggests a BL Lacertae object origin [arXiv:1210.3852]. The source is associated with a radio counterpart and shows indications of a variable gamma-ray flux [arXiv:1210.3852].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 2FGL J0338.2+1306 lost its initial cutoff indication after more data, shows possible variability, and has radio, Swift X-ray, and WISE counterparts consistent with a BL Lac origin.

quotes.quote: 2FGL J0338.2+1306. The source was initially selected based upon the 24-month data set, preferring a spectral cutoff with a significance of 3 . As shown in figure subfig:skyplot_J0338.2 , gaining photon statistics revealed a large positional shift fitting the 42-month data, and the initial indication for a spectral cutoff vanished. The updated data set also indicates a variable ray flux at the 2 level. The improved positional accuracy allows to associate 2FGL J0338.2+1306 with a radio source (15.1 mJy), which is positionally coincident with SWIFT J033829.2+130217, see figure subfig:skyplot_J0338.2 . Its WISE counterpart suggests a BL Lac origin, in accordance with the entire multi-wavelength emission (see figure subfig:sed_J0338.2 ).

gmw_00013178.name: 2FGL J1410.4+7411
gmw_00013178.overview: 2FGL J1410.4+7411 is a gamma-ray source that exhibits a steady point-source spectrum [arXiv:1210.3852]. Its position shifts toward a faint X-ray source, and its infrared counterpart suggests a BL Lacertae object origin [arXiv:1210.3852].

sources.summary: The paper finds 2FGL J1410.4+7411 has a cutoff-like steady point-source spectrum and a shifted position toward a faint Swift X-ray source whose infrared counterpart would indicate a BL Lac origin.

quotes.quote: 2FGL J1410.4+7411. The 42-month ray data of this source prefers its spectrum to be fit with a power-law ( = 1.5 ) with exponential cutoff, with a significance of 3.3 . The lightcurve is consistent with steady emission, but no indication for an angular extent was detected. While also being counterpartless after 24 months, the position computed from the larger photon sample seems to shift towards the very faint X-ray source SWIFT J141234.8+741153, see figure subfig:skyplot_J1410.4 , f^ unabs (0.2 2 keV ) 6.3 10^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

Note that the Swift source has no radio counterpart, which might reflect the lacking sensitivity of radio surveys. Assuming the Swift source to be the correct X-ray association, its infrared counterpart WISE J141235.75+741158.0 would indicate a BL Lac origin. Additionally, compared to the USNO and GALEX data, the Swift UVOT observations indicate variable emission.

gmw_00013179.name: ID 47173
gmw_00013179.overview: ID 47173 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy [arXiv:2307.04782]. Spectroscopic observations indicate a systemic redshift of approximately 3.30186, with a Lyman-alpha emission offset of 580 kilometers per second [arXiv:2307.04782].

sources.summary: The paper reports repeated DEIMOS redshifts for ID 47173, finds NIRSpec agreement within z = 0.002, and interprets the difference as a 580 km/s Ly-alpha offset.

quotes.quote: ID 47173 was observed twice in 2020 and 2021 with DEIMOS, resulting in two redshift measurements ( z_ spec = 3.3038 with Q=3 and z_ spec = 3.3056 with Q=2 ). NIRSpec spectroscopy is consistent with the Q=3 measured redshift by z = 0.002 , with z_ NIRSpec = 3.30186 (MSA ID 11699, ArrabalHaro2023 ). The difference in redshift in part represents the offset between the Ly emission ( v_ Ly ) and the associated metal lines observed in NIRSpec spectroscopy, which better trace the systemic redshift of the galaxy. In this case, the observed offset in Ly emission for this galaxy is 580 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00013180.name: ID 37653
gmw_00013180.overview: ID 37653 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 4.9 [arXiv:2307.04782]. It is located near a z ~ 5 overdense region, suggesting it may be a member of this proto-cluster, and it exhibits an unusually large Lyman-alpha emission offset of 970 km/s [arXiv:2307.04782].

sources.summary: The paper gives ID 37653 a coadded DEIMOS redshift confirmed by NIRSpec, places it near a z ~ 5 overdensity, and finds an unusually large Ly-alpha offset.

quotes.quote: For ID 37653, both DEIMOS observations yielded low-quality ( Q = 2 ) redshift measurements. As discussed previously in subsec:multiObs , we use the co-added spectrum of two individual DEIMOS observations to measure a secure redshift for this galaxy. We find the spectroscopic redshift to be z_ spec = 4.8998 , which agrees with the NIRSpec observations at z_ NIRSpec = 4.89651 (MSA ID 707, ArrabalHaro2023 ).

This galaxy also lies close ( 1-2 comoving Mpc, in projection) to a recently identified overdense region at 4.5 < z < 5.5 in the EGS ArrabalHaro2023,Naidu2022 . We find 7 other galaxies as within this region, all with 4.8 < z_ spec < 5.0 . Studies of high-redshift proto-clusters using the Millennium cosmological simulations show that such clusters at z 5 could be as large as 5-20 cMpc Chiang_2013,Muldrew_2015 . Taken together, the redshift and the location of 37653 could indicate that this galaxy is a member of this overdensity at z 5 . Using the redshift given by NIRSpec for 37653, we find the offset of Ly emission is large ( v_ Ly = 970 km s ^ 1 ) compared to offsets found by Cassata_2020 around Ly emitting galaxies at z = 4.4-5.7 ( v_ Ly = 377 km s ^ 1 with a scatter of 329 km s ^ 1 ).

gmw_00013181.name: ID 200415
gmw_00013181.overview: ID 200415 is an emission line galaxy [arXiv:2307.04782]. Its spectrum exhibits a multitude of emission features, including [O II], H-beta, [O III], H-alpha, and [N II] [arXiv:2307.04782].

sources.summary: The paper finds ID 200415 has a DEIMOS redshift discrepant from 3D-HST, argues the grism feature is likely contaminated, and detects several emission lines.

quotes.quote: For ID 200415, however, we find a significant difference in redshift, relative to the 3D-HST measurement of z = 0.73945 . The WFC3 G141 grism spectrum for this object includes an emission feature towards the blue (near 1.14 m ) that is identified as H (yielding z = 0.73945 ). This portion of the 3D-HST grism spectrum, however, suffers from a high level of contamination associated with a nearby bright source, such that the emission feature identified as H is likely spurious. In our Keck/DEIMOS spectrum, we detect a multitude of emission features, including [ O II ] , H , and [ O III ] -- as well as H and [ N II ] .

gmw_00013182.name: unclassified BluDOG (ID=4)
gmw_00013182.overview: The unclassified BluDOG (ID=4) is an astronomical object whose spectral energy distribution has been analyzed [arXiv:1803.09951]. Although initially unclassified, its spectral energy distribution is highly similar to the average of power-law dust-obscured galaxies, indicating that it is intrinsically a power-law dust-obscured galaxy [arXiv:1803.09951].

sources.summary: The paper examines unclassified BluDOG (ID=4), compares its SED with average bump and PL DOG SEDs, and concludes that the object is intrinsically a PL DOG.

quotes.quote: To investigate the nature of this unclassified BluDOG (ID=4), we show the SED of this unclassified BluDOG in Figure fig:BLU_un_sed . By comparing the SED of the unclassified DOG with the average SED of bump and PL DOGs (Figure fig:BUMPave ), the SED of the unclassified DOG looks very similar to the average SED of PL DOGs. Therefore, we conclude that this unclassified BluDOG is also a PL DOG intrinsically.

gmw_00013183.name: SXDF-NB209-34
gmw_00013183.overview: SXDF-NB209-34 is a high-redshift galaxy confirmed to be located at a redshift of z=2.2 [arXiv:1311.4259]. Based on detected emission lines, its line ratios classify it as a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1311.4259].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SXDF-NB209-34 at z=2.2, reports detected emission lines including [O iii], and uses [O iii]/H and [N ii]/H ratios to classify it as star-forming.

quotes.quote: SXDF-NB209-34 has been confirmed to be located at z=2.2 as its and emission lines are detected.

We detected both and [ O  iii ] emission lines for only one target (SXDF-NB209-34). For this object, the line ratios of log ( [ O  iii ] /H )=0.73 and log ( [ N  ii ] /H ) <-0.96 indicate that this is a star-forming galaxy.

gmw_00013184.name: QSO J1504 4152
gmw_00013184.overview: QSO J1504 4152 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus located within the error circle of a detected gamma-ray source [arXiv:1603.00998]. However, an association with the gamma-ray source is disfavored because the quasar lacks variability and the detected source has a hard spectral index [arXiv:1603.00998].

sources.summary: The paper considers QSO J1504 4152 as a possible AGN counterpart to the detected gamma-ray source, noting its location in the error circle but disfavoring the association because variability is absent and the LAT spectrum is hard.

quotes.quote: In addition to the VHE source HESS J1504 418, there is an another known source located in the 2 error circle of the best-fit position, that is the quasar QSO J1504 4152 wl97 . Its position is marked in Figure  fig:tsmap . Given that active galactic nuclei (AGN) are the dominant source class detected by \ LAT 3fgl15 , we thus searched for long-term variability for the purpose of checking any possible association between QSO J1504 4152 and the excess source.

Both the quasar QSO J1504 4152 wl97 and the VHE source HESS J1504 418 ace+10 are within the 2 error circle of the best-fit position of the source. We searched for variability in the source, but no significant variations were found. In addition the spectral index of 1.9 does not favor the association to AGN since AGN generally have soft power-law spectra with photon indices up to 3.0 in the LAT \ energy range 3fagn15 .

gmw_00013185.name: 3-53A
gmw_00013185.overview: The galaxy 3-53A is classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0609569]. It is a warm, distant system that exhibits a high star formation rate and emits the maximum far-infrared luminosity of its sample [arXiv:astro-ph/0609569].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3-53A as a ULIRG in the IDS/ISOCAM sample, with the sample's maximum far-IR luminosity and among the warm, distant, high-SFR 60 m sources.

quotes.quote: One ULIRG, 3-53A, and two broad H emission line galaxies with AGN optical properties (i.e., 3-70A and 3-96A, see Bettoni et al. (2006) for more details), are also included in the complete subsample.
The ULIRG galaxy, 3-53A, emits the maximum far-IR luminosity of the sample, nearly 100 times higher than the median value.
The more luminous 60 m sources, i.e., those with higher SFRs, correspond to the more distant systems, 3-96 and 3-53, two warm sources (Fig. eps4 ).

gmw_00013186.name: low-z-D
gmw_00013186.overview: The low-z-D galaxy is a massive, low-redshift system with a profile that extends beyond 100 kpc [arXiv:2111.03557]. Its outer regions exhibit artificial truncation in SDSS profiles and show residual over-subtraction in HSC profiles [arXiv:2111.03557].

sources.summary: The paper describes low-z-D as a more massive low-redshift galaxy whose profile extends beyond 100 kpc, with SDSS truncation and residual HSC over-subtraction in its outskirts.

quotes.quote: For the more massive galaxies ( low-z-D , low-z-E ), their profiles extend beyond 100 kpc.
We also find that the SDSS profiles of low- z galaxies show early artificial truncation in their outskirt ( low-z-A , B , D , and E ).
For HSC S18A , even though we attempt to empirically correct the local over-subtraction for the 1-D profile, we still see over-subtractions in HSC profiles (especially in low-z-D ).

gmw_00013187.name: LAE
gmw_00013187.overview: LAE is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitter galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.72 [arXiv:1704.03473]. It is situated at a distance of 1384 ckpc/h from a strong C IV absorption system detected along the line of sight to a background quasar [arXiv:1704.03473].

sources.summary: The paper treats the D15 LAE as a z=5.72 galaxy 1384 ckpc/h from a strong C IV absorber, infers its mass-related properties, and rules out its outflow as the metal source.

quotes.quote: Specifically, a CIV absorption system in the line of sight to the background quasar J1030+0524, with a column density of log N _ CIV (cm ^ 2 ) = 14.52 0.08 dodorico2013 at a distance of 212.8 ^ +14 _ 0.4 h^ 1 pkpc (1384 ckpc/ h in the adopted cosmology) from a Lyman emitter galaxy (LAE) of M_ UV = 20.7 at z = 5.72, with inferred parameters log ( M_ /M_ ) = 9.4 using the stellar mass function for z = 6 galaxies from song2016 and log ( M_ h /M_ ) = 10 - 11 derived from z 6.6 LAE clustering from ouchi2010 .

We have explored the likelihood of reproducing the observed LAE -- CIV absorption pair detected by D15 and studied the physical processes that produced the metal enrichment in the IGM at z 5.6. In the context of our feedback model, we rule out the scenario of an outflow produced by the observed LAE at a distance of 1384 ckpc/ h at early times.

gmw_00013188.name: SDSS J003546.29-034118.2
gmw_00013188.overview: SDSS J003546.29-034118.2 is an extreme-Population A quasar that exhibits a difference between its cosmological and virial luminosities [arXiv:2209.05526]. Within its subclass, it is the source with the highest blueshift and the largest full width at half maximum [arXiv:2209.05526].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J003546.29-034118.2 as the xA quasar with the largest blueshift and FWHM, and notes its virial-cosmological luminosity offset with blueshift.

quotes.quote: In our xA sub sample, SDSS J003546.29-034118.2 is the source with the highest blueshift, -1010 80 , that is as well the quasar with the largest FWHM( )=4200 340 .
SDSS J003546.29, J152314.49 and J023055.54 showed a difference between the cosmological and virial luminosities under -0.2 and are sources with an \ blueshift.
J003546.29-034118.2: Highest blueshifted source, shift( ) = -1011.69 81 , FWHM( ) = 4194.93 342 .

gmw_00013189.name: SDSS J152314.49+375928.9
gmw_00013189.overview: SDSS J152314.49+375928.9 is a quasar characterized as one of the strongest blueshifted extreme accretion sources [arXiv:2209.05526]. It exhibits a luminosity offset between its cosmological and virial luminosities, alongside a high amount of iron emission [arXiv:2209.05526].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J152314.49+375928.9 as one of the strongest blueshifted xA sources, with FWHM around 4000, a luminosity offset, and an extra 1914 component.

quotes.quote: Another example is SDSS J152314.49+375928.9, with a shift of -850 30 , and a FWHM( )=4000 400 .
SDSS J003546.29, J152314.49 and J023055.54 showed a difference between the cosmological and virial luminosities under -0.2 and are sources with an \ blueshift.

J152314.49+375928.9: One of the highest blueshift sources: shift( )= -849.96 25 , with a FWHM( )=4000 400 . This source is also the one with an additional component in 1914 due to the high amount of iron emission observed in the red side of .

gmw_00013190.name: HPS067
gmw_00013190.overview: HPS067 is classified as an emission line galaxy [arXiv:2009.07510]. It is characterized by low signal-to-noise ratios in its [O II] and [O III] emission line fluxes [arXiv:2009.07510].

sources.summary: The reanalysis flags HPS067 as one of three HPS galaxies with different posterior covariance shapes, large lower credible limits, and low [O II]/[O III] S/Ns that may drive uncertainty differences.

quotes.quote: Three cases (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) have a bit different covariance shapes from the rest, and it seems that the low S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] emission lines cause it; these three targets have the three lowest S/Ns in [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 .

The uncertainties are more-or-less similar to each other, except for the three targets that have much larger lower-credible-limits (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237). These targets have the three lowest S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 as mentioned above. This low S/Ns may be related to the uncertainty differences for these three targets between this work and .

Excluding three galaxies that have large lower-credible-limits (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) in the estimates from , my 1- uncertainty estimates are comparable to or smaller than those of (Fig. fig-metalcmp-median ). These differences in estimates are probably due to the two factors that differ between and this work---the uncertainties in the strong-line calibration ( Fig. fig-model_test see section ana-res-model ) and the convergence monitoring of MCMC sampling---but it is hard to pinpoint the dominant one. The three targets (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) have the three lowest S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 , but it is not clear whether this is the cause of the big differences in the uncertainty estimates between this work and . This big difference may be caused by a combination of the low S/Ns of the oxygen line fluxes and the different two factors in metallicity estimation mentioned above.

gmw_00013191.name: HPS235
gmw_00013191.overview: HPS235 is classified as an emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2009.07510]. It is characterized by low signal-to-noise ratios in both its [O II] and [O III] emission line fluxes [arXiv:2009.07510].

sources.summary: The reanalysis flags HPS235 as one of three HPS galaxies with different posterior covariance shapes, large lower credible limits, and low [O II]/[O III] S/Ns that may drive uncertainty differences.

quotes.quote: Three cases (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) have a bit different covariance shapes from the rest, and it seems that the low S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] emission lines cause it; these three targets have the three lowest S/Ns in [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 .

The uncertainties are more-or-less similar to each other, except for the three targets that have much larger lower-credible-limits (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237). These targets have the three lowest S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 as mentioned above. This low S/Ns may be related to the uncertainty differences for these three targets between this work and .

Excluding three galaxies that have large lower-credible-limits (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) in the estimates from , my 1- uncertainty estimates are comparable to or smaller than those of (Fig. fig-metalcmp-median ). These differences in estimates are probably due to the two factors that differ between and this work---the uncertainties in the strong-line calibration ( Fig. fig-model_test see section ana-res-model ) and the convergence monitoring of MCMC sampling---but it is hard to pinpoint the dominant one. The three targets (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) have the three lowest S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 , but it is not clear whether this is the cause of the big differences in the uncertainty estimates between this work and . This big difference may be caused by a combination of the low S/Ns of the oxygen line fluxes and the different two factors in metallicity estimation mentioned above.

gmw_00013192.name: HPS237
gmw_00013192.overview: HPS237 is classified as an emission line galaxy [arXiv:2009.07510]. It is characterized by low signal-to-noise ratios in its [O II] and [O III] emission line fluxes [arXiv:2009.07510].

sources.summary: The reanalysis flags HPS237 as one of three HPS galaxies with different posterior covariance shapes, large lower credible limits, and low [O II]/[O III] S/Ns that may drive uncertainty differences.

quotes.quote: Three cases (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) have a bit different covariance shapes from the rest, and it seems that the low S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] emission lines cause it; these three targets have the three lowest S/Ns in [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 .

The uncertainties are more-or-less similar to each other, except for the three targets that have much larger lower-credible-limits (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237). These targets have the three lowest S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 as mentioned above. This low S/Ns may be related to the uncertainty differences for these three targets between this work and .

Excluding three galaxies that have large lower-credible-limits (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) in the estimates from , my 1- uncertainty estimates are comparable to or smaller than those of (Fig. fig-metalcmp-median ). These differences in estimates are probably due to the two factors that differ between and this work---the uncertainties in the strong-line calibration ( Fig. fig-model_test see section ana-res-model ) and the convergence monitoring of MCMC sampling---but it is hard to pinpoint the dominant one. The three targets (HPS067, HPS235, and HPS237) have the three lowest S/Ns of [ O II ] and [ O III ] fluxes Table 3 of Indahl_2019_ApJ_883_114 , but it is not clear whether this is the cause of the big differences in the uncertainty estimates between this work and . This big difference may be caused by a combination of the low S/Ns of the oxygen line fluxes and the different two factors in metallicity estimation mentioned above.

gmw_00013193.name: No.13
gmw_00013193.overview: No.13 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate identified as a possible Lyman-alpha emitter at a redshift of approximately 6.5 [arXiv:1011.5313]. Although its short-wavelength photometry is affected by a nearby bright neighbor, correcting for this effect allows the object to satisfy all selection criteria for this galaxy class [arXiv:1011.5313].

sources.summary: The paper identifies No.13 as a possible z 6.5 LAE whose short-wavelength photometry is affected by a bright neighbour, but whose corrected colors would satisfy the selection criteria.

quotes.quote: In addition, we found one object which may be a LAE at z 6.5 in a careful visual checking of hundreds objects satisfying the magnitude and colour cuts (criteria [ a ] - [ ] ) but not the drop-out criteria ( [ d ] and [ e ] ). The photometry in shorter wavelengths of this object is probably affected by a bright neighbour as found in Figure 5 second line from the bottom. If the effect by the neighbour was removed, this object would satisfy all the selection criteria. As shown in Figure 3 by an open square (brighter one), this object has an excess large enough in z'- NB912. We add this object as No.13 in the final list of our LAE candidates.

gmw_00013194.name: No.14
gmw_00013194.overview: No.14 is a spectroscopic Lyman-alpha galaxy [arXiv:1011.5313]. It features a close probable low-redshift neighbor separated by approximately 1 arcsecond and exhibits a qualifying z'-NB912 color excess [arXiv:1011.5313].

sources.summary: The paper adds No.14 to the LAE list, notes it was reported as a spectroscopic LAE, and describes a close probable low-redshift neighbour plus qualifying z'-NB912 excess.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, we add another object as No.14 in the LAE list. This object is reported in as a spectroscopic LAE. As shown in Figure 5 bottom line, this object has a close neighbour which is probably low- z object. The separation is very close ( 1'' ), so that we missed finding it. The colour excess z'- NB912 of this object satisfies with the criterion (b) shown in Figure 3 (fainter open square).

gmw_00013195.name: J105239.6+572432
gmw_00013195.overview: J105239.6+572432 is a Type I active galactic nucleus located in the Lockman Hole at a redshift of z=1.113 [arXiv:1501.00913]. It exhibits very low absorption, and its X-ray spectrum can be modeled with a simple or broken power law [arXiv:1501.00913].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J105239.6+572432 as a low-absorption Type I AGN in the Lockman hole and fits its X-ray spectrum with simple and broken power-law models.

quotes.quote: J105239.6+572432 (J1052 at z=1.113 ) and J105316.9+573552 (J1053 at z=1.204 ) are two Type I AGN in the Lockman hole with a very low absorption ( N_ H (z)<6 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 and N_ H (z)<9 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 , respectively (Corral et al. 2011). We took the longest \ observation of the Lockman hole not affected by soft proton flare covering 100 ks. Data were extracted as above and binned to 25 counts per spectral bin. J1052 can be fit with a simple power law, resulting in a ^2_ red =1.15 (288 dof) and N_ H (z)<3 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ( N_ H ^ Gal =5.5 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 ). A better fit is obtained with a broken power law, with ^2_ red =0.92 (286 dof) and N_ H (z)<6 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00013196.name: J105316.9+573552
gmw_00013196.overview: J105316.9+573552 is a Type I active galactic nucleus located in the Lockman Hole [arXiv:1501.00913]. It is characterized by very low absorption, and its X-ray spectrum is well-fitted by a single power law [arXiv:1501.00913].

sources.summary: The paper describes J105316.9+573552 as a low-absorption Type I AGN in the Lockman hole, noting detector-column complications and a good single-power-law X-ray fit.

quotes.quote: J105239.6+572432 (J1052 at z=1.113 ) and J105316.9+573552 (J1053 at z=1.204 ) are two Type I AGN in the Lockman hole with a very low absorption ( N_ H (z)<6 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 and N_ H (z)<9 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 , respectively (Corral et al. 2011). We took the longest \ observation of the Lockman hole not affected by soft proton flare covering 100 ks. Data were extracted as above and binned to 25 counts per spectral bin.

J1053 falls on the dead CCD in MOS1 and across dead columns on the pn detector. The fit with a single power law is good ( ^2_ red =1.01 , 129 dof) and returns N_ H (z)<2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 . Both values are consistent with our lower envelope.

gmw_00013197.name: QSO B1345+584
gmw_00013197.overview: QSO B1345+584 is a Type I active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1501.00913] located at a redshift of z = 0.646 [arXiv:1501.00913]. X-ray observations of the source indicate that its intrinsic column density is negligible [arXiv:1501.00913].

sources.summary: The appendix treats QSO B1345+584 as a z=0.646 Type I AGN, filters its X-ray observation for flares, and finds negligible intrinsic absorption under a power-law fit.

quotes.quote: QSO B1345+584 at z =0.646. QSO B1345+584 is a Type I AGN. Corral et al. (2011) derived an upper limit on the intrinsic column density of N_ H (z)<1 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 for this source. It was observed by \ in Jun 2001 for 40 ks, even if it was not on-axis. Because of a strong proton flare activity at the end of the observation only the first 25 ks and 29 ks were retained for the pn and MOS2 instruments, respectively (the source falls on a CCD gap in MOS1).

Data were extracted as above and binned to 25 counts per spectral bin. The fit with a power law (assuming N_ H ^ Gal =1.1 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) provides a ^2_ red =1.19 (308 dof) with a clear under prediction of the high energy spectral part. In this case the intrinsic column density is negligible ( N_ H (z)<2 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 ).

gmw_00013198.name: 2E 1852
gmw_00013198.overview: 2E 1852 is a Type I active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.312 [arXiv:1501.00913]. X-ray spectral analysis of the source reveals an intrinsic column density of approximately 4.3e20 cm^-2 [arXiv:1501.00913].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2E 1852 as a Type I AGN, reprocesses its X-ray spectra after filtering flares, and finds an intrinsic column density above the reported value.

quotes.quote: 2E 1852 at z =0.312. This Type I AGN is reported to have an intrinsic column density N_ H (z)<1 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 (Corral et al. 2011). The object was observed by \ on Apr 2000 in small window with the two MOS and in full window with the pn. Some flaring activity was present and filtered out reducing the exposure time to 54 ks and 22 ks for the MOS and pn, respectively. MOS data were fitted in the 0.6--10 keV range to encompass calibration uncertainties in small window mode.

All data were binned to have at least 25 counts per spectral bin. We fit the spectra with an absorbed power-law model ( N_ H ^ Gal =3.3 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ). We derive a good fit with ^2_ red =1.07 (1316 dof) and N_ H (z)=(4.3 0.3) 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , much larger than the reported value.

gmw_00013199.name: PID 352
gmw_00013199.overview: PID 352 is an optically faint, X-ray bright obscured quasar located at a redshift of z=1.6 [arXiv:2004.07604]. It is a double-lobed radio galaxy with an extended radio morphology and a radio power of log P_1.4 = 27.1 W Hz^-1 [arXiv:2004.07604].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PID 352 as an optically faint, X-ray bright obscured quasar at z=1.6 and a powerful double-lobed radio galaxy with Fe-line redshift fitting.

quotes.quote: These 16 radio-loud AGN include the two brightest X-ray sources in the field, PID 203 and PID 319, and the optically faint, X-ray bright obscured quasar at z=1.6 , PID 352 vignali15 . PID 319 and PID 352 are reported to have an extended radio morphology hales14,huynh15 : PID 352 is a powerful radio galaxy with log P_ 1.4 =27.1 [ W Hz ^ 1 ] with a double radio-lobe vignali15 .

For Fe line driven X-ray redshift measurements, for example, for PID 352 vignali15 and PID 215, the Fe K line is assumed to be a cold line at 6.4 keV, since most Fe lines in our sample are found at that energy, as shown below, and the source redshift was fitted using these fine resolution spectra.

gmw_00013200.name: PID 316
gmw_00013200.overview: PID 316 is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.07604]. It is identified as a heavily absorbed Compton-thick candidate due to its large absorbing column density [arXiv:2004.07604].

sources.summary: PID 316 is treated as a heavily absorbed Compton-thick AGN candidate, the only source exceeding the face-value column threshold and a case where R(Fe) can be biased by continuum curvature.

quotes.quote: A few caveats on R (Fe) are: firstly, R( Fe ) may sometimes overestimate EW when a source is heavily absorbed (a prime example is PID 316). A strongly absorbed spectrum exhibits a strong continuum curvature with a prominent continuum discontinuity around 7 keV due to an Fe K edge. Our simple interpolation method would then tend to place f^ _ 13 lower than actually is, leading to an overestimate of R( Fe ) (that is, the continuum discontinuity is viewed as part of an Fe line excess). A prime example is PID 316 but such a large deviation occurs only in two sources.

Compton-thick AGN candidates were selected by two characteristic spectral features: 1) large absorbing column density 10^ 24 ; and 2) strong 6.4 keV Fe K line emission arising from reflection fom thick, cold matter. On taking face values of \ measurements, the only source with \ exceeding 10^ 24 \ is PID 316. However, given the uncertainty in the \ measurements and the likely complexity of spectra at large \ discussed in Sect. 4.1, we select three more candidates (PID 66, PID 131, PID 245) with log 23.9 [ ] , for which real values of their \ could reach the Compton-thick \ threshold.

gmw_00013201.overview: The object 2406-15603 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy that exhibits broad emission lines and a reddish spectrum [arXiv:2303.12684]. It features a clearly visible extended structure, though its broad H-alpha emission line was not captured in miniJPAS observations [arXiv:2303.12684].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2406-15603 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with broad emission lines, a reddish spectrum, visible extended structure, and an H feature absent from miniJPAS.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, 2406-15603 is rather a Seyfert 1 galaxy with broad emission lines such as H and H . It also has a reddish spectrum, and the extended structure of the galaxy is very clearly visible in the image. Furthermore, while the SDSS spectrum detects the broad emission line of H , the miniJPAS observation does not capture this emission, which is probably the most relevant feature for classifying this object as a QSO.

gmw_00013202.overview: The object 2241-1234 is classified as an extended source [arXiv:2303.12684]. Its spectrum is dominated by emission from an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2303.12684].

sources.summary: The paper treats 2241-1234 as an extended, AGN-dominated source whose ANN classification is correct; its high signal-to-noise spectrum makes that classification more certain.

quotes.quote: Following the SDSS classification criterion, only 2470-3341 and 2241-1234 are correctly classified by the ANN _1 .

Finally, 2241-1234 is correctly classified. Although it is an extended source according to SExtractor , the emission of the AGN dominates the spectrum. The high S/N obtained in this object makes the classification more certain.

gmw_00013203.name: Q0933+733 DLA
gmw_00013203.overview: The Q0933+733 DLA is a high-column-density damped Lyman-alpha system with a kinematically simple structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0409695]. It exhibits a low metallicity of 2.6 percent solar, yet its relative abundance patterns remain similar to other DLAs at its epoch [arXiv:astro-ph/0409695].

sources.summary: The paper describes Q0933+733 DLA as a high-column-density system with narrow, structured metal-line kinematics, low metallicity, and abundance patterns resembling other DLAs at similar epochs.

quotes.quote: The narrow velocity width of the Q0933+733 DLA, = 16 , implies a kinematically simple system, while the edge-leading asymmetries of the unsaturated lines reveal structure even on these small velocity scales.

The high N(HI) value of the Q0933+733 DLA ensures that many metal-line transitions are easily measured even at a metallicity as low as 2.6 solar ( 3.1). This is the second lowest value recorded at z<2 . Even so, relative abundance patterns in this DLA are similar to results for other DLAs, implying that there is minimal evolution in the nucleosynthetic patterns for gas with [ M/H ] <-1 at this epoch.

gmw_00013204.name: Q0948+433 DLA
gmw_00013204.overview: The Q0948+433 DLA is a high-redshift galaxy system characterized by some of the strongest metal-line transitions of any known damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:astro-ph/0409695]. Its saturated Si II 1808 line indicates a high metallicity, which has enabled the detection of Ti II, Co II, and Mg I [arXiv:astro-ph/0409695].

sources.summary: The paper highlights Q0948+433 DLA as having some of the strongest known DLA metal-line transitions; saturated Si 2 implies high metallicity, and strong lines enable Ti 2, Co 2, and Mg 1 detections.

quotes.quote: The Q0948+433 DLA has among the strongest metal-line transitions of any known DLA. The saturated Si 2 1808 line implies a high metallicity ( [ Si/H ] > -1 ) and a significant enhancement. The strong metal lines of this DLA have made possible the detection of Ti 2 1910, Co 2 2012, and Mg 1 2026.

gmw_00013205.name: IVS B2029 215
gmw_00013205.overview: IVS B2029 215 is a low-ionization broad absorption line quasar, classified as an FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:1702.06644]. Its spectrum is dominated by broad Fe II emission and absorption lines, with a red continuum that suggests dust is present in its outflowing gas [arXiv:1702.06644].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IVS B2029 215 as an FeLoBAL quasar dominated by broad Fe II emission and absorption, compares its spectrum to FIRST J121442.3+280329, and suggests its red continuum indicates dust in outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: IVS B2029 215---an FeLoBAL quasar, with very strong broad absorption and emission, predominantly in Fe 2 ; see Section flb and Fig. \ felobal .

An extreme example of broad Fe 2 emission and absorption is shown in the spectrum of the quasar, IVS B2029 215, which is dominated by broad Fe 2 emission and absorption lines. This object belongs to a rare class of low-ionization broad absorption line quasars known as FeLoBALs, so-named because the spectrum is dominated by excited states of Fe 2 . The spectrum, shifted to the emission rest frame, is shown in Fig. \ felobal .

The spectrum of IVS B2029 215 is very similar to that of the z=0.692 quasar FIRST J121442.3+280329 (de Kool et al. \ 2002; Branch et al. \ 2002) from the FIRST Bright Quasar Survey (White et al. 2000). By analogy with the spectrum of FIRST J121442.3+280329 (de Kool et al. \ 2002) we show a tentative continuum in Fig. \ felobal on the assumption that broad emission and blue-shifted broad absorption are superimposed on a power-law continuum from the accretion disk. The IVS B2029 215 continuum, however, is substantially redder than that of FIRST J121442.3+280329, suggesting the presence of dust in the outflowing gas.

gmw_00013206.name: Q0019 15, z = 3.439
gmw_00013206.overview: Q0019 15, z = 3.439 is a high-redshift damped system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381]. Its neutral hydrogen column density is measured at 20.9 [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper treats Q0019 15 as a high-redshift damped system, adopts Keck HI measurements, presents metal-line data, and warns that NiII, Si, and high-ion profiles are unreliable or blended.

quotes.quote: Q0019 15, z = 3.439 This damped system comes from the high redshift survey by Storrie-Lombardi et al. \ (1996) and the adopted HI = 20.9 0.1 was taken from recent Keck measurements of Storrie-Lombardi Wolfe (1998).
