While our observations covered for this system, the profile extends over two echelle orders and an accurate measurement of HI proved impossible. Figure vpQ0019 shows the metal-line transitions and Table Q0019-clm presents the measurements for this system.

The Fe abundance is based on the marginally saturated FeII 1608 profile, yet should be reasonably accurate. The NiII lines are very weak and in poor SNR regions so these measurements are not reliable. The same is true for the Si measurements, although to a lesser extent.

gmw_00013207.name: Q0149 + 33, z = 2.140
gmw_00013207.overview: Q0149 + 33, z = 2.140 is a high-redshift damped system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381]. It is relatively metal-poor and exhibits an atypical chromium-to-zinc ratio that indicates the system is essentially undepleted [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes Q0149 + 33 as a relatively metal-poor LBQS damped system with HI=20.5, conservative O and reliable Al measurements, and atypically positive Cr/Zn implying little depletion.

quotes.quote: Q0149 + 33, z = 2.140 This relatively metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] 1.8 dex) damped system is a also member of the LBQS statistical sample. Table Q0149-clm gives the measured column densities and Figure vpQ0149 plots the metal-lines. In the following analysis, we assume HI = 20.5 0.1 ( ). As with PH957, the O abundance is based on the statistically insignificant OI 1355 profile and provides a very conservative upper limit.

Finally the Al abundance is derived from the marginally saturated AlII 1670 profile but should be a reliable value. The system is notable for exhibiting an atypical Cr to Zn ratio; [ Cr/Zn ] = +0.26 0.1 dex. The Cr measurement is reasonably accurate and the ZnII 2062 transition places a rather strict upper limit to the Zn abundance. Therefore, it is very likely [ Cr/Zn ] > 0 which marks the first such occurrence in a damped system and indicates this system must be essentially undepleted.

gmw_00013208.name: Q0951 04, z = 3.857
gmw_00013208.overview: Q0951 04, z = 3.857 is a high-redshift galaxy system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381]. It is identified as an intervening damped system toward the quasar Q0951 04 [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the z=3.857 Q0951 04 absorber as one of two very high-redshift damped systems, adopts HI=20.6, treats abundances as tentative, and flags a possible NiII blend.

quotes.quote: Q0951 04, z = 3.857 z = 4.203 The QSO Q0951 04 from the survey by Storrie-Lombardi et al. \ (1996) has two intervening damped systems, both at very high redshift. The velocity plots and measurements for the z = 3.857 system are given in Figure vpQ0951A and Table Q0951A-clm , while those for the z = 4.203 system are presented by Figure vpQ0951B and Table Q0951B-clm . We adopt HI = 20.6 0.1 for the system at z=3.857 and HI = 20.4 0.1 for the system at z=4.203 , based on recent Keck measurements ( ). Because all of the column densities are based on either marginally saturated profiles or weaker, low SNR profiles all of these measurements are somewhat tentative.
Finally, we note the feature at v = 180 in the NiII 1370 profile for the z = 3.857 system may be an unidentified blend, although it nearly coincides with a strong feature in the SiII 1526 profile.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the z=4.203 Q0951 04 absorber as a very high-redshift damped system, adopts HI=20.4, presents profiles, and excludes its Fe limits from abundance analysis.

quotes.quote: Q0951 04, z = 3.857 z = 4.203 The QSO Q0951 04 from the survey by Storrie-Lombardi et al. \ (1996) has two intervening damped systems, both at very high redshift. The velocity plots and measurements for the z = 3.857 system are given in Figure vpQ0951A and Table Q0951A-clm , while those for the z = 4.203 system are presented by Figure vpQ0951B and Table Q0951B-clm . We adopt HI = 20.6 0.1 for the system at z=3.857 and HI = 20.4 0.1 for the system at z=4.203 , based on recent Keck measurements ( ). Because all of the column densities are based on either marginally saturated profiles or weaker, low SNR profiles all of these measurements are somewhat tentative. In fact, we consider the limits on Fe^+ for the system at z = 4.203 to be too conservative to include this system in the abundance analysis.

gmw_00013209.name: Q1215 + 33, z = 1.999
gmw_00013209.overview: Q1215 + 33, z = 1.999 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381] classified as a radio-selected damped system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381]. It is a member of the Large Bright Quasar Survey statistical sample [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper describes Q1215 + 33 as a radio-selected LBQS damped system observed during HIRES commissioning, adopts HI=20.95, and uses a lower-limit Fe abundance that may underestimate Fe.

quotes.quote: Q1215 + 33, z = 1.999 This radio-selected damped system ( ) was observed as part of the commissioning run for the HIRES instrument and is a member of the LBQS statistical sample. In the following analysis we assume HI = 20.95 0.067 based on observations by Pettini et al. \ (1994). Figure vpQ1215 presents a plot of the metal-line transitions and the ionic column densities are listed in Table Q1215-clm . As the FeII 1608 profile is saturated and the FeII 1611 transition is marginally detected, we establish only a lower limit to Fe^+ . We will include this system in the abundance analysis, however, by adopting 10 [ Fe^+ ] = 14.648 0.033 based solely on the FeII 1608 profile, noting this value may be an underestimate.

gmw_00013210.name: Q1346 03, z = 3.736
gmw_00013210.overview: Q1346 03, z = 3.736 is a high-redshift damped system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381]. It has a neutral hydrogen column density of 20.7 [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381] and is expected to be a very metal-poor system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper presents Q1346 03 as a very high-redshift damped system with HI=20.7, unusable Fe lines from sky blending, no metallicity indicator, and very low inferred Si metallicity.

quotes.quote: Q1346 03, z = 3.736 This very high z damped system was taken from the survey of Storrie-Lombardi et al. \ (1996). We present the velocity plots and column densities in Figure vpQ1346 and Table Q1346-clm . We adopt HI = 20.7 0.1 ( ) throughout the analysis.

Unfortunately both FeII 1608 and FeII 1611 are blended with B-band sky lines. Therefore, we have no metallicity indicator for this system and it is not included in the subsequent analysis. Measuring [ Si/H ] = 2.3 dex, we expect this is a very metal-poor system.

gmw_00013211.name: Q2231 00, z = 2.066
gmw_00013211.overview: Q2231 00, z = 2.066 is a high-redshift galaxy system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381]. This Large Bright Quasar Survey (LBQS) damped system exhibits TiII 1910 absorption and has an adopted HI value of 20.56 [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper describes Q2231 00 as an LBQS damped system with significant TiII absorption, adopts HI=20.56, presents velocity and column-density data, and finds agreement with Lu et al. measurements.

quotes.quote: Q2231 00, z = 2.066 This LBQS system is another damped system exhibiting significant (5 ) TiII 1910 absorption. As in the damped system toward Q2230 + 02, the TiII profiles overlap, hence we use the technique outlined above to determine Ti^+ . Throughout the analysis we adopt HI = 20.56 0.1 ( ).

The velocity plots are given in Figure vpQ2231 and the column densities are presented in Table Q2231-clm . This is the other system in our data sample which was previously observed by Lu et al. \ (1996). We find our measurements match theirs in nearly every case, although their analysis did not include Ti ^+ .

gmw_00013212.name: Q2359 02, z =2.095
gmw_00013212.overview: The faint quasar Q2359 02 exhibits two intervening damped Lyman-alpha systems [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381]. These high-redshift systems are located at redshifts of z = 2.095 and z = 2.154 [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the z=2.095 absorber toward Q2359 02 as an LBQS damped system, adopts HI=20.7, presents profiles and column densities, and notes weak-transition abundances are suspect.

quotes.quote: Q2359 02, z =2.095 z =2.154 This faint QSO exhibits two intervening damped systems. Both are members of the LBQS statistical survey. The velocity plots and column densities for the z=2.095 system are presented in Figure vpQ2359A and Table Q2359A-clm and those for the z=2.154 are given by Figure vpQ2359B and Table Q2359B-clm .

Both are part of the LBQS statistical sample and we have taken the HI column densities from Wolfe et al. \ (1995): HI = 20.7 0.1 for the z=2.095 system and HI = 20.3 0.1 for the z=2.154 system. The SNR is relatively low for most of the spectrum and therefore abundances established on the weakest transitions are suspect, particularly those for Zn. We intend to make further observations of these two systems to improve the accuracy of our abundance measurements.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the z=2.154 absorber toward Q2359 02 as an LBQS damped system, adopts HI=20.3, presents profiles and column densities, and notes weak-transition abundances are suspect.

quotes.quote: Q2359 02, z =2.095 z =2.154 This faint QSO exhibits two intervening damped systems. Both are members of the LBQS statistical survey. The velocity plots and column densities for the z=2.095 system are presented in Figure vpQ2359A and Table Q2359A-clm and those for the z=2.154 are given by Figure vpQ2359B and Table Q2359B-clm .

Both are part of the LBQS statistical sample and we have taken the HI column densities from Wolfe et al. \ (1995): HI = 20.7 0.1 for the z=2.095 system and HI = 20.3 0.1 for the z=2.154 system. The SNR is relatively low for most of the spectrum and therefore abundances established on the weakest transitions are suspect, particularly those for Zn. We intend to make further observations of these two systems to improve the accuracy of our abundance measurements.

gmw_00013213.name: R1
gmw_00013213.overview: R1 is a faint extragalactic active galactic nucleus located near a pulsar [arXiv:1801.07709]. It is identified as a compact radio core with a relatively stable flux density over timescales of months to years [arXiv:1801.07709].

sources.summary: R1 is one of two faint radio sources observed near the pulsar, with measured signal-to-noise, separation, stable flux, and an interpretation as compact radio cores in extragalactic AGNs.

quotes.quote: We obtained a signal to noise ratio (SNR) of 29 for R1 and 36 for R2 in the final image with naturally weighting. The angular distance to is 13 arcsec for R1 and 45 arcsec for R2. Both sources are in a northeast direction and have a difference of 6 deg in their position angles. At the lower frequency of 1.4 GHz, both sources are fainter, and source R1 is slighter brighter than source R2.

Compared to the average flux density, i.e. the blue line for R1 and the red line for R2, no significant deviation is detected over about 4 yr. Together with the agreement between the VLA and the VLBI results, both sources have a relatively stable flux density with a variability index of 0.15 on time scales of months to years.

Identifications: compact radio cores in AGNs The most reasonable identification of sources R1 and R2 is that they are the compact radio cores, i.e. the stationary jet bases, in extragalactic active galactic nuclei (AGNs). This identification can naturally explain their high brightness temperatures, compact morphologies, flat radio spectra, and relatively stable luminosities over the 19 yr.

gmw_00013214.name: R2
gmw_00013214.overview: R2 is an extragalactic active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1801.07709]. It is identified as a compact radio core, representing a stationary jet base with a relatively stable flux density over timescales of months to years [arXiv:1801.07709].

sources.summary: R2 is one of two faint radio sources observed near the pulsar, with measured signal-to-noise, separation, stable flux, and an interpretation as compact radio cores in extragalactic AGNs.

quotes.quote: We obtained a signal to noise ratio (SNR) of 29 for R1 and 36 for R2 in the final image with naturally weighting. The angular distance to is 13 arcsec for R1 and 45 arcsec for R2. Both sources are in a northeast direction and have a difference of 6 deg in their position angles. At the lower frequency of 1.4 GHz, both sources are fainter, and source R1 is slighter brighter than source R2.

Compared to the average flux density, i.e. the blue line for R1 and the red line for R2, no significant deviation is detected over about 4 yr. Together with the agreement between the VLA and the VLBI results, both sources have a relatively stable flux density with a variability index of 0.15 on time scales of months to years.

Identifications: compact radio cores in AGNs The most reasonable identification of sources R1 and R2 is that they are the compact radio cores, i.e. the stationary jet bases, in extragalactic active galactic nuclei (AGNs). This identification can naturally explain their high brightness temperatures, compact morphologies, flat radio spectra, and relatively stable luminosities over the 19 yr.

gmw_00013215.name: host galaxy of GRB 060729
gmw_00013215.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060729 is located at a redshift of z = 0.54 [arXiv:1012.1466]. Synthetic galaxy evolution models indicate that the host has a young stellar population, low metallicity, and a modest stellar mass [arXiv:1012.1466].

sources.summary: The paper models the host galaxy of GRB 060729 as young and low-metallicity, estimates modest stellar mass and SFR, and finds its F_light lower than in other GRB hosts.

quotes.quote: We used the procedure described in Svensson et al. (2010) to calculate the stellar masses, star-formation rate and the specific star-formation rate of the host galaxy. Using a redshift of z = 0.54 , we fitted our HST magnitudes to synthetic galaxy evolution models (Bruzual Charlot 2003). None of the synthetic models were very well constrained, however the best fit template ( ^ 2 / dof = 6.58 ) is for a galaxy with a young stellar population and low metallicity.

We also find a star-formation rate of (SFR [ M_ /yr ] ) = -0.89^ +0.04 _ 0.07 ; a stellar mass of (Mass [ M_ ] ) = 9.13^ +0.04 _ 0.08 and a specific star-formation rate of (SSFR [ Gyr^ 1 ] ) = -1.03^ +0.18 _ 0.04 . The latter values of the star-formation rates and mass are estimated from an extinction-corrected SED.

For GRB 060729, F_ light = 39.0 in the F622W filter, which is somewhat smaller than that seen in other GRBs by Fruchter et al. (2006). Thus we conclude that the host galaxy of GRB 060729 has a young stellar population with a modest stellar mass ( 10^ 9 \ M_ ) and star-formation rate ( SFR 0.13 \ M_ yr ^ 1 ), suggesting that the host is typical of other GRB host galaxies (e.g., Wainwright et al. 2005; Fruchter et al. 2006; Savaglio et al. 2009; Svensson et al. 2010; Christensen et al. 2004).

gmw_00013216.overview: The galaxy 03.1540 is classified as a luminous compact galaxy [arXiv:1110.4293]. It was detected by the Infrared Space Observatory and is considered peculiar in terms of both its extinction and its star formation [arXiv:1110.4293].

sources.summary: For LCG 03.1540, the paper says the stellar-population model could not reproduce the SED, making its mass likely overestimated; it was ISO-detected and peculiar in extinction and star formation.

quotes.quote: For one of them, 03.1540, the models could not fit the SED correctly. The effect was reduced by using Calzetti law for the extinction law but still, the fit did not reproduce the expected SED. The mass determined for this galaxy was at the upper limit and probably overestimated. This LCG was also detected by ISO and considered peculiar in both term of its extinction and star formation.

gmw_00013217.name: J013301 400628
gmw_00013217.overview: J013301 400628 is a quasar with an emission line at 4160 Å that can make automatic continuum-fitting completely inadequate [arXiv:1810.06136]. Additionally, its manual continuum redward of 4300 Å may be too high in general [arXiv:1810.06136].

sources.summary: The paper uses J013301 400628 to show continuum-fitting limits: its automatic continuum is inadequate because of a 4160 emission line, while the manual continuum may be too high redward of 4300.

quotes.quote: However, for the lower- \ spectrum of J013301 400628, the automatic continuum is completely inadequate. This is caused mainly by the emission line at 4160 . The manual fits shown in f:Lyacont are clearly more accurate and useful for statistical studies of the \ forest, and even for more detailed studies of these individual lines of sight.
For example, the manual continuum redwards of 4300 \ in f:Lyacont for J013301 400628 may by too high in general, perhaps by as much as 2 .

gmw_00013218.name: J051707 441055
gmw_00013218.overview: J051707 441055 is a quasar [arXiv:1810.06136]. Its spectrum has been utilized in detailed studies to constrain cosmological variations in the fine-structure constant [arXiv:1810.06136].

sources.summary: The paper cites J051707 441055 as a detailed fine-structure-constant study whose DR1 spectrum includes corrections for UVES wavelength-scale distortions and slit-alignment velocity shifts.

quotes.quote: One example is the very detailed study of J051707 441055 to constrain cosmological variations in the fine-structure constant by Kotus:2017:3679 . Beyond the basic cleaning steps outlined above, this study focussed on correcting the individual exposures for known, long-range distortions of the UVES wavelength scale e.g. Rahmani:2013:861,Whitmore:2015:446 and velocity shifts between exposures caused by varying alignment of the quasar within the UVES slits. Such improvements are included in the DR1 versions of the spectra when available.

gmw_00013219.name: PID 352
gmw_00013219.overview: PID 352 is a luminous, obscured quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.6 [arXiv:1612.00955]. It exhibits an ionized iron absorption feature that indicates an outflowing gas velocity of 0.14c [arXiv:1612.00955].

sources.summary: The paper presents PID 352 as a luminous, obscured z=1.6 CDFS quasar with Fe K emission and absorption, an ionized outflow at 0.14c, and enough counts to detect the X-ray feature.

quotes.quote: The \ spectrum of a luminous ( L_ 2-10 keV 4 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 ), obscured ( N_H 2 10 ^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) quasar in the CDFS (PID 352) at z = 1.6 is shown in Figure 4. The source is characterized by an emission and absorption line complex in the 6--7 keV iron K band. While the emission line is interpreted as being due to neutral iron (consistent with the presence of cold absorption), the absorption feature is due to highly ionized iron transitions (FeXXV, FeXXVI) with an outflowing velocity of 0.14 ^ +0.02 _ 0.06 . The mass outflow rate is similar to the source accretion rate ( 2 M _ yr ^ 1 ) , and the derived mechanical energy rate of almost 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 corresponds to about 10 of the source bolometric luminosity.

PID352 represents one of the few cases where evidences of X--ray outflowing gas have been observed at high redshift thus far (Vignali et al. 2015). The relatively deep \ exposure -- 800 ks because the source is at the edge of the field -- and the PID352 intrinsic luminosity, made possible to collect enough source counts (4500 in EPIC cameras) to detect the outflowing X--ray feature.

gmw_00013220.name: GRB 030115 host galaxy
gmw_00013220.overview: The GRB 030115 host galaxy is a faint source with an R-band magnitude of 25.2 and an estimated photometric redshift between 2 and 2.5 [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574]. Although its red R-K color indicates reddening, submillimeter observations suggest it is not a massive, dusty starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a possible R=25.2 host for GRB 030115 with photometric redshift z=2-2.5, significant reddening, and no SCUBA evidence for a massive dusty starburst galaxy.

quotes.quote: A possible R = 25.2 host galaxy was found (Garnavich ; Dullighan et al. ). Although no spectroscopic redshift has been reported to date, the photometric redshift lies in the range z=2-2.5 (Levan et al. ).

Whilst the red R-K color of the afterglow and host galaxy indicates significant reddening (Levan et al. ), the non-detection by SCUBA, albeit with a fairly large uncertainty, suggests that it is not a massive star-bursting, dusty galaxy.

gmw_00013221.name: GRB 041006 host galaxy
gmw_00013221.overview: The GRB 041006 host galaxy is located at a redshift of 0.716 [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574]. It has an R-band magnitude of approximately 25 and is not a bright submillimeter source [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574].

sources.summary: The paper reports an R 25 host for GRB 041006 at redshift 0.716 and finds no significant quiescent submillimeter emission, so it is not a bright submillimeter source.

quotes.quote: The redshift of the optical counterpart was determined to be 0.716 (Fugazza et al. ; Price et al. ). A host galaxy with R 25 was found (Fynbo et al. ; Covino et al. ).

As shown in Table table:host , the combination of our ToO observations gives a good limit to the quiescent sub-millimeter flux of the host galaxy. Given the relatively low redshift, it is not surprising that it is not a bright sub-millimeter source.
In particular, the host galaxies for GRBs 030226 and 041006 had 850 m rms values < 1.4 mJy.

gmw_00013222.name: PC1643+4631A
gmw_00013222.overview: PC1643+4631A is a quasar located at a redshift of z=3.79 [arXiv:astro-ph/9801150]. Its field contains a damped Lyman-alpha system at a redshift of z=3.14 [arXiv:astro-ph/9801150].

sources.summary: The paper treats PC1643+4631A as a z=3.79 quasar field with a z=3.14 damped Lyman system, testing a reported massive cluster and finding no supporting overabundance.

quotes.quote: We found the field around quasar PC1643+4631A to be a good candidate for this technique for a couple of reasons: This z=3.79 QSO has a Damped Lyman- System (DLS) at z=3.14 (Schneider et al. ) whose Lyman break would be perfectly positioned between Johnson U and V filters. Besides, a possible very massive cluster might exist between this quasar and a second one (PC1643+4631B) that lies just 200 arcsec away and has roughly the same redshift ( z=3.831 ). Jones et al. detected a significant decrement in the CMB in a region between these two QSOs that could be related with a Sunyaev-Zeldovich effect arising from a M 10^ 15 M _ intervening cluster. However, Saunders et al. imaged the region in R , J and K bands, looking for ellipticals that should abound in such a cluster, and found no evidence.

gmw_00013223.name: AllWISE J170109.82-483550.8
gmw_00013223.overview: AllWISE J170109.82-483550.8 is a galaxy that is coincident with a variable radio source [arXiv:2203.09806]. Although classified as a star by the TESS Input Catalogue due to its point-like morphology, its spectral energy distribution is consistent with a radio-quiet active galactic nucleus or a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2203.09806].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AllWISE J170109.82-483550.8 as a counterpart to a variable MeerKAT source, gives infrared/optical photometry, and finds its SED consistent with a radio-quiet AGN or star-forming galaxy despite TIC star classification.

quotes.quote: This source is coincident with a 2MASS source, 2MASS 17010986-4835508, with a separation of 0 39. 2MASS 17010986-4835508 has a K-magnitude of 14.465 0.123, but has poor-quality photometry in the J and H bands 2003yCat.2246....0C . The 2MASS source was matched to an AllWISE source, AllWISE J170109.82-483550.8, by the TESS Input Catalogue TIC; 2019AJ....158..138S with W1 magnitude 13.670 0.040 and W2 magnitude 13.617 0.046. The 2MASS/AllWISE source is classified as a star by the TIC due to its point-like morphology.

The SED of (shown in Figure fig: VAR MKTJ170109.9-483550 MKTJ170222.5-483144 SED ) is consistent with a radio-quiet AGN or star-forming galaxy, despite being classified as a star by the TIC based on the round morphology.

gmw_00013224.name: ( ,  )= (05^ h 36^ m 07^ s .2 , 82^ 23'14''.5)
gmw_00013224.overview: The object at coordinates (05h 36m 07s.2, 82d 23m 14s.5) is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.05 [arXiv:1101.1604]. It is classified as an extended source and resides in a region of low galactic extinction [arXiv:1101.1604].

sources.summary: The survey found a coordinate-identified AGN in the NCCS field; it is bright, at redshift z = 0.05, and classified by the pipeline as an extended source.

quotes.quote: The object is located at ( , )= (05^ h 36^ m 07^ s .2 , 82^ 23'14''.5) , which is a region of low galactic extinction: E _ B-V = 0 ^m .06 ( SCH98 ), and it is relatively bright: R = 15 ^m .12, I = 14 ^m .71, NUV = 18 ^m .64, FUV = 19 ^m .42. The object was confirmed spectroscopically as an AGN at a redshift z = 0.05 ( XU01 ). The object was identified as an extended source by NCCS pipeline.

gmw_00013225.name: J132216.25+052446.3
gmw_00013225.overview: J132216.25+052446.3 is a broad-absorption line quasar at a redshift of z = 2.04806 [arXiv:2205.12285]. Over a 19-year monitoring period, the quasar exhibited correlated absorption line variability driven by changes in the ionizing continuum, which coincided with the emergence of a new wind component [arXiv:2205.12285].

sources.summary: The paper monitors BAL QSO J132216.25+052446.3 over ten epochs, finding line-locked BAL-A variability, an emerging BAL-C component, correlated absorption changes, and outflow locations tied to ionizing continuum changes.

quotes.quote: We present a time variability analysis of broad absorption lines (BAL; spread over the velocity range of 5800-29000 ) seen in the spectrum of J132216.25+052446.3 ( = 2.04806) at ten different epochs spanning over 19 years. The strongest absorption component (BAL-A; spread over 5800-9900 ) is made up of several narrow components having velocity separations close to \ doublet splitting. The , \ and \ absorption from BAL-A show correlated optical depth variability without major changes in the velocity structure.

A very broad and shallow absorption (BAL-C; spread over the velocity range 15000-29000 ) emerged during our monitoring period coinciding with a dimming episode of 13. All the identified absorption lines show correlated variability with the equivalent widths increasing with decreasing flux. This together with the \ emission line variability is consistent with ionization being the main driver of the correlated variability.

We suggest BAL-A is produced by a stable clumpy outflow located beyond the broad emission line region and BAL-C is a newly formed wind component located near the accretion disk and both respond to changes in the ionizing continuum.

gmw_00013226.name: host galaxy of SN 2018lfe
gmw_00013226.overview: The host galaxy of SN 2018lfe is an extended source with a half-light radius of 0.5 arcseconds [arXiv:2109.06970]. It has a star formation rate of 0.77 solar masses per year, sub-solar metallicity, and no detected host galaxy extinction [arXiv:2109.06970].

sources.summary: The paper says the SN 2018lfe host was undetected in PS1 but detected in deeper Binospec imaging; its spectra imply little extinction, a 0.77 M_sun yr^-1 SFR, and sub-solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: No galaxy is detected at the position of SN 2018lfe in the PS1/ 3 catalog down to the nominal 5 limiting magnitudes of g 23.3 , 23.2 , i 23.1 , and z 22.3 Chambers2019 . We obtained deeper images on 2020 November 15 using Binospec and detected an extended source with a half-light radius of 0.5'' , measured using sep sep . From extrapolating the observed light curve (see Section sec:phot ), we expect the apparent magnitude of the SLSN to be dimmer than 25 mag in the -band, therefore the host should be the only source present.

We measured the strength of the galaxy emission lines by fitting narrow Gaussian profiles (FWHM 40 ) to the three spectra at phases of 35, 41, and 84 days. The flux ratio of L_ H /L_ H =2.75 0.37 is consistent with the expectation for case B recombination ( L_ H /L_ H =2.86 ), suggesting no significant host galaxy extinction (in agreement with the lack of extinction inferred from modeling of the SN light curves). The H line luminosity is L_ H = (9.8 0.3) 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . This corresponds to a star formation rate (SFR) of 0.77 0.02 M _ yr ^ 1 kennicutt98 To calculate the metallicity we use the R_ 23 method kobulnicky99 and adopt the lower branch solution due to the lack of an N 2 detection. We determine a value of 12 + ( O/H )=8.18 0.12 , or Z = 0.31 0.05 Z _ asplund09 .

gmw_00013227.name: F36376 1453
gmw_00013227.overview: F36376 1453 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.886 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701841]. The object is very bright and exhibits a red color, with a round shape observed in both Ic and z' bands [arXiv:astro-ph/0701841].

sources.summary: The paper describes F36376 1453 as a very bright, red object with a single emission line identified as Ly at z=4.886 and a round shape in Ic and z' images.

quotes.quote: F36376 1453 is another object with a single emission line in the spectrum which was identified as Ly at z=4.886 by daw01 . This object is very bright ( z'=21.97 ) and its colours in both V-I_c and I_c-z' are red ( V-I_c=3.25 , I_c-z'=0.78 and this object is outside of plotted area of Fig. fig:twocol1 ). It has a round shape in our images in I_c and z' bands (FWHM 1.0'' ).

gmw_00013228.name: MaNGA 1-209980
gmw_00013228.overview: MaNGA 1-209980 is an active galaxy characterized by widespread active galactic nucleus or LINER-like emission across almost all of its spaxels [arXiv:1710.09389]. While its center is dominated by active galactic nucleus emission, gas signatures at larger radii are consistent with diffuse ionized gas or post-asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1710.09389].

sources.summary: The paper describes MaNGA 1-209980 as an ancillary MaNGA galaxy with widespread AGN/LI(N)ER-like spaxels, central high-equivalent-width AGN emission, and outer gas signatures consistent with pAGB or diffuse ionized gas.

quotes.quote: We note that MaNGA 1-209980 is an ideal example of a galaxy in which multiple processes contribute to the fact that almost all spaxels are flagged as AGN or LI(N)ER-like. While the center of the galaxy is clearly dominated by AGN-like emission with high H equivalent widths, the gas signatures at larger radii are more consistent with expectations from the pAGB or diffuse ionized gas scenario.

Computing H equivalent widths by averaging over all AGN or LI(N)ER classified spaxels in this particular galaxy would have not resulted in a mean equivalent width of > 5 \ because the contributions from diffuse ionized gas regions and hot stars would have smeared out the signal.

gmw_00013229.name: PKS 0837+012
gmw_00013229.overview: PKS 0837+012 is a flat-spectrum radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of z=1.123 [arXiv:astro-ph/0412660]. High-resolution radio observations reveal that the source possesses a rare two-sided milliarcsecond-scale jet structure extending from its compact core [arXiv:astro-ph/0412660].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0837+012/J0839+0104 as a flat-spectrum calibrator quasar whose EVN and VLA images suggest an unusually rare two-sided mas-scale jet structure.

quotes.quote: The high resolution radio structure of the phase-reference calibrator quasar J0839+0104 in our 1.6-GHz EVN experiment shows extended features on both sides of the compact core (Fig. 1 in Frey et al. \ ). There is a low surface brightness jet to the SW seen in the naturally weighted image, extending to 30 mas. If uniform weighting is employed, the improved angular resolution reveals another component at 6.6 mas to the E of the core.

Analysis of the EVN images of the phase-reference calibrator, the flat-spectrum quasar PKS 0837+012 (J0839+0104, z=1.123 ) made at both 1.6 and 5 GHz frequencies suggests a two-sided mas-scale jet structure, supported by an arcsecond-resolution VLA image at 5 GHz. This is almost unique among radio-loud quasars. It makes this source attractive for more detailed studies in the future.

gmw_00013230.name: SDSS J1251+2935
gmw_00013230.overview: SDSS J1251+2935 is a quadruply imaged gravitationally lensed quasar at a redshift of z=0.802 [arXiv:2311.05619]. The system's crossbow-like configuration is produced by the gravitational field of a foreground galaxy at a redshift of z=0.410 [arXiv:2311.05619].

sources.summary: The paper reports detecting SDSS J1251+2935 as a GLQ in 2022 preliminary observations, describes it as a quadruply imaged quasar at z=0.802, and gives its lens configuration, image separations, and brightnesses.

quotes.quote: During preliminary observations in May-June 2022, we were able to detect images of the GLQ SDSS J1251+2935 on 3 frames taken with the i and filters. This quasar was discovered by and represents a quadruply imaged source with redshift z_ q =0.802 . The configuration of the system resembles a crossbow, which is formed after the light rays from the quasar pass the gravity field of a relatively nearby galaxy ( z_ g =0.410 ). The angular separation of component D from the other three ones is approximately 1.79 arcsec, however, the latter are no more than 1.08 arcsec apart from each other, so in ground-based observation they merge into one clot with a total brightness of about 18.9 mag. The brightness of component D is about 20.2 mag, which was approximately at that time at the sensitivity limit of the ILMT and detector.

gmw_00013231.name: Galaxy A
gmw_00013231.overview: Galaxy A is identified as the host galaxy of the supernova SN 2002ic [arXiv:astro-ph/0408306]. Observations of an emission feature in the nuclear region of the galaxy indicate a redshift of z=0.0667 [arXiv:astro-ph/0408306].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy A as the host of SN 2002 ic, deriving z=0.0667 from an H emission feature in the galaxy's nuclear region and correcting an earlier erroneous host redshift.

quotes.quote: Fortuitously, during our UVES observation of the supernova, the extreme eastern end of the slit intercepted the nuclear region of galaxy A. We noticed an emission feature at the corresponding spatial position (along the slit) in the spectra, and in the same order as the H feature of SN 2002 ic, shifted only slightly in wavelength. Assuming the feature was also due to H emission (but from Galaxy A), we derive z=0.0667 . This indicates that Galaxy A must be the host of SN 2002 ic. Hamuy (priv. comm.) confirms that, owing to a target acquisition error, the redshift given for the host galaxy in hamuy:03 is incorrect.

gmw_00013232.name: Source 4
gmw_00013232.overview: Source 4 is a bright Type-1 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.784 [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166]. It exhibits a high rest-frame X-ray luminosity and has an absorption level consistent with the Galactic value [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166].

sources.summary: The paper describes Source 4 as a bright Type-1 AGN at z=0.784, with a well-fitted simple power-law X-ray spectrum, Galactic-consistent absorption, high rest-frame X-ray luminosity, and measured X-ray-to-optical flux ratio.

quotes.quote: Source 4 (ROSAT 29 ): this source was already observed by ROSAT (Lehmann et al. ) and optically classified as a Type-1 AGN at z=0.784 . This is one of the brightest sources in our sample (3164 EPIC-pn counts in the [ 0.5-7 ] keV band). This source is very well fitted ( _ ^2=1.03 ) by a simple power law model with =2.02^ +0.04 _ 0.04 and N_H consistent with the Galactic value ( 5.7 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 ). We measure L_X = 4.2 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 in the [ 0.5-10 ] keV rest-frame band, and log ( f_x f_R ) =0.6 .

gmw_00013233.name: Source 6
gmw_00013233.overview: Source 6 is a Type-1 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.586 [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166]. X-ray observations of the source reveal a high rest-frame luminosity and an absorption level consistent with Galactic values [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166].

sources.summary: Source 6 is described as a ROSAT-observed Type-1 AGN at z=0.586 whose EPIC-pn spectrum is well fitted by a simple power law with Galactic-consistent absorption and high rest-frame X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: Source 6 (ROSAT 16 ): it was observed by ROSAT (Schmidt et al. ) and classified as a Type-1 AGN at z=0.586 . This source (1537 EPIC-pn counts in the [ 0.5-7 ] keV band) is well fitted ( _ ^2=1.15 ) by a simple power law model with =2.47_ 0.03 ^ +0.08 and N _ H consistent with the Galactic value ( 5.7 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 ). It has L _ X = 1.2 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 in the [ 0.5-10 ] keV rest-frame band.

gmw_00013234.name: Source 25
gmw_00013234.overview: Source 25 is a high-luminosity X-ray active galactic nucleus located in the Lockman Hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166]. It has a photometric redshift of approximately 2.71 and exhibits an intrinsic absorption of 3e23 cm^-2 [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166].

sources.summary: Source 25 is treated as an absorbed high-luminosity X-ray source in the Lockman Hole sample, with a ROSAT HRI association, photometric redshift near 2.7, intrinsic absorption, and very large rest-frame luminosity.

quotes.quote: Source 25 (ROSAT 84 ): this object was part of the ROSAT ultradeep HRI survey (Hasinger et al., ). Lehmann et al. ( ) give a photometric redshift z_ phot =2.71^ +0.29 _ 0.41 . The spectrum extracted from the EPIC-pn data (332 counts in the [ 0.5-7 ] keV band) is well fitted ( _ ^2=1.09 ) by a wabs zwabs(powerlaw) model, with an intrinsic absorption of N_H =3^ +1 _ 1 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and =2.3^ +0.3 _ 0.3 ; the unabsorbed rest-frame luminosity in the [ 0.5-10 ] keV band is L_X =5.49 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 .

gmw_00013235.name: Source 26
gmw_00013235.overview: Source 26 is an active galactic nucleus optically classified as a Type-2 AGN [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166]. Located at a redshift of z=0.780, it has an unabsorbed rest-frame X-ray luminosity of 4e43 erg/s in the 0.5-10 keV band [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166].

sources.summary: Source 26 is identified as a ROSAT-observed Type-2 AGN at z=0.780; its X-ray spectral fit indicates intrinsic absorption and a rest-frame 0.5-10 keV luminosity of 4e43 erg/s.

quotes.quote: Source 26 (ROSAT 117 ): was observed by ROSAT (Schmidt et al. ) and optically classified as a Type- 2 AGN at z=0.780 . From the fit of the X-ray spectra we get the values, N_H =2^ +1 _ 1 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and =1.5^ +0.4 _ 0.3 . The unabsorbed X-ray luminosity in the [ 0.5-10 ] keV rest-frame band is L_X =4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

gmw_00013236.name: Source 50
gmw_00013236.overview: Source 50 is a Type-2 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.204 [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166]. Its X-ray spectrum reveals a soft component superimposed on a heavily absorbed power law, resulting in an unusually low X-ray-to-optical flux ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166].

sources.summary: Source 50 is described as a Type-2 AGN with a soft component superposed on a heavily absorbed power law, requiring a double power-law fit and showing unusually low X-ray-to-optical flux ratio.

quotes.quote: Source 50 (ROSAT 901 ): this source was classified as a Type-2 AGN at z=0.204 by Lehmann et al. ( ). As noted in Paper I, a very soft component superimposed on a heavy absorbed power law, is likely present in this source as suggested by the unusually large value of the hardness ratio HR3. The XMM-Newton spectrum clearly shows such a feature. By fitting a double power law model ( wabs(zwabs(powerlaw)+powerlaw) ), we obtain: N_H =4^ +2.5 _ 1.5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , =3^ +1 _ 1 for the hard component and =3.3^ +0.4 _ 0.5 for the soft component ( _ ^2=1.2 ). We also find an unabsorbed X-ray luminosty L _ X =5.7 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 in the [ 0.5-10 ] keV rest-frame band and a ratio log ( f_x f_R ) =-2.4 , unusually low for an AGN, which is probably due to the strong intrinsic absorption.

gmw_00013237.name: Source 96
gmw_00013237.overview: Source 96 is optically classified as a Type-1 quasar located at a redshift of z=3.279 [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166]. Its X-ray spectrum exhibits clear absorption, which suggests a mismatch with its optical classification that may be caused by an atypical gas-to-dust ratio or chemical composition [arXiv:astro-ph/0207166].

sources.summary: Source 96 is described as a Type-1 QSO at z=3.279 with clear X-ray absorption; the paper links its optical/X-ray mismatch to altered gas-to-dust ratio or chemical composition.

quotes.quote: Source 96 (ROSAT 39 ): this object is optically classified as a Type-1 QSO at z=3.279 (Lehmann et al., ). A clear absorption is present in the X-ray spectrum and the fit yields N_H =5^ +5 _ 3 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . As already argued in Sect. sec:param , this mismatch between the optical and X-ray classifications could be due to a gas-to-dust ratio or a chemical composition different from that of the Galactic interstellar gas (Akiyama et al. ; Maiolino et al. ).

gmw_00013238.name: N14
gmw_00013238.overview: The galaxy N14 is an X-ray-emitting source characterized by a high X-ray luminosity [arXiv:1107.1049]. Its spectral energy distribution fit is of average quality, and the presence of a ultraviolet bump is not visually obvious [arXiv:1107.1049].

sources.summary: The paper identifies N14 as an X-ray-emitting galaxy with high X-ray luminosity; its SED fit is average quality, and the apparent UV bump is not visually obvious.

quotes.quote: For the first one, N14 (MUSYCID-30046), E_b/err_ E_b =2.7 . This galaxy has a high X-ray luminosity, (L_X/L_ ) = 44.1 with a hardness ratio equal to 0.63 cardamone08 . The fit is of average quality with ^2_ min =4.56 and the presence of a bump is not obvious from a visual inspection of the SED. It is at least partly due to the fact that the best fit model corresponds to a large width of the bump: 500 .

gmw_00013239.name: N21
gmw_00013239.overview: The galaxy N21, also identified as MUSYCID-55660, has a possibly spurious hard X-ray detection [arXiv:1107.1049]. Its spectral energy distribution is well-fitted and displays a clear 2175 Angstrom absorption feature [arXiv:1107.1049].

sources.summary: The paper describes N21 as a galaxy with a possibly spurious hard X-ray detection and a well-fitted SED showing a clear 2175 absorption feature.

quotes.quote: The second one is N21 (MUSYCID-55660). This galaxy (G02:638) is detected in the hard X-rays with a signal to noise ratio of 2 by giacconi02 only using Sextractor and not with a method based on wavelet transform; alexander03 did not detect this source, so the X-ray detection may be spurious. Its SED shows a clear absorption feature at 2175 with E_b/err_ E_b =5.1 and the SED fitting is good ( ^2_ min =1.71) .

gmw_00013240.name: Object 31
gmw_00013240.overview: Object 31 is a CANDELS galaxy that was initially selected as a Lyman-alpha emitter candidate [arXiv:2112.04505]. However, its photometric redshift of z = 1.49 suggests it is a lower-redshift source with flux excesses likely caused by oxygen and hydrogen emission lines [arXiv:2112.04505].

sources.summary: Object 31 is a CANDELS galaxy selected as an LAE candidate, but its z=1.49 photometric redshift and likely O III/H emission make it a lower-redshift contaminant.

quotes.quote: Object 31 belongs to subsample 1 and is placed above the selection curve. It is roughly three magnitudes brighter in the NB1257 filter than the F125W. It is clearly detected in all the available bands, although the nucleus is especially bright in the red bands (F125W and F160W). This object was identified as galaxy CANDELS-ID 13218.

Nevertheless, the photometric redshift available for this source is z = 1.49 , suggesting that the O III 5007 emission-line might be the cause of its flux excess in the NB1257 filter. In this context, the bright signal observed in the F160W band would be produced by H emission. Nonetheless, spectroscopic follow-up would be needed to derive firmer conclusions. Ultimately, the two initial selected candidates were thereby rejected as possible LAEs at z 9.3 .

gmw_00013241.name: SDSSJ031947.01 + 003504.4
gmw_00013241.overview: SDSSJ031947.01 + 003504.4 is classified as an early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0901.2346]. It hosts a central bar structure that has been detected through multiple analytical methods, showing a profile maximum at approximately 5 arcseconds [arXiv:0901.2346].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ031947.01 + 003504.4 as an early-type spiral example whose bar is detected by both methods, with matching bar lengths and a profile maximum near 5 arcsec.

quotes.quote: The early-type spiral SDSSJ031947.01 + 003504.4 and the late-type spiral SDSSJ020159.33 081441.9 are analysed by both methods. The bar of the early-type spiral was detected by both methods and the measured bar lengths are in agreement. In fact, the radial profiles of and I_ 2 /I_ 0 show a local maximum at about 5'' , where the PA and _2 are constant.

gmw_00013242.name: SDSSJ020159.33 081441.9
gmw_00013242.overview: SDSSJ020159.33 081441.9 is a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0901.2346]. It contains a galactic bar structure that is detectable through ellipse fitting, though the bar is not detected by the Fourier method due to interference from the spiral arms [arXiv:0901.2346].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSSJ020159.33 081441.9 as a late-type spiral example where ellipse fitting detects the bar, while the Fourier method fails because the maximum lies in spiral arms.

quotes.quote: The early-type spiral SDSSJ031947.01 + 003504.4 and the late-type spiral SDSSJ020159.33 081441.9 are analysed by both methods.

On the contrary, the bar of the late-type spiral was detected only by the ellipse fit method. The radial profiles of and I_ 2 /I_ 0 show a local maximum at different radii (about 10'' ). The I_ 2 /I_ 0 maximum is located in the spiral arm region, where _2 is not constant. Therefore, the bar of this galaxy was not detected by the Fourier method.

gmw_00013243.overview: The galaxy 192129 is detected in X-rays and is cataloged as a type-2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2012.09447]. It exhibits a normal optical-to-near-infrared spectral energy distribution and a narrow hydrogen emission line [arXiv:2012.09447].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 192129 as an ALMA target detected in X-rays and cataloged as a type-2 AGN, notes its normal optical-NIR SED, and keeps it in analyses while marking it separately.

quotes.quote: Although the potential AGNs were excluded from our ALMA targets, we found that one of the ALMA targets, 192129, is detected in X-ray with Chandra elvis09,civano12,civano16 and included in the X-ray-selected AGN catalog of kalfountzou14 as a type-2 AGN. The optical--NIR spectral energy distribution (SED) of this source is not peculiar as compared to the other galaxies and as shown in the best-fit SEDs in Appendix sec:appendix1 onodera16 and its H emission line is narrow, which is likely to be consistent with the classification by kalfountzou14 . Although we expect that the optical--NIR emission is dominated by emission from the host galaxy, the physical quantities, such as stellar mass, SFR, gas-phase metallicity, and ionization parameter (Section subsec:magphys and subsec:ism ), may be affected by the emission from the AGN. On the other hand, the dust continuum observed at ALMA Band-6 ( _ rest 280 \ m ) is expected to be dominated by cold dust emission from star-forming regions ( T 20--40 K). We do not exclude this source in the following analyses but distinguish it from the other sources on each figure.

gmw_00013244.overview: The galaxy 208681 is a metal-rich system with a high dust content comparable to submillimeter galaxies [arXiv:2012.09447]. A detection of the CO(9-8) line suggests that the galaxy likely hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2012.09447].

sources.summary: The paper treats 208681 as the brightest 1.3 mm source, with a possible CO(9--8) detection indicating an AGN host; it also classifies the source as SMG-like and the sample's most metal-rich galaxy.

quotes.quote: The brightest source at 1.3 mm, 208681, appears to be detected with the CO(9--8) line. Given that quasar host galaxies tend to have more extreme CO excitation states than normal star-forming galaxies carilliwalter13 , the CO(9--8) line detection may suggest that this source hosts an AGN. We will discuss the CO(9--8) line of this source in a forthcoming paper (Suzuki et al. 2020 in preparation). We note that the contribution of the CO(9--8) line to the dust continuum flux is negligible.

The brightest source at 1.3 mm among our sample, 208681 (Table table:obssummary and Figure fig:ms-mdust ), has a dust mass of log(M_ dust /M_ )=8.89_ 0.08 ^ +0.11 , which is comparable to those of SMGs at z \ 3--4 tan14,dudzeviciute20 . This source can be classified as a SMG in terms of its dust content.

This brightest source, 208681, is also the most metal-rich galaxy with 12+log(O/H)=8.59_ 0.05 ^ +0.04 among our sample and appears to distribute apart from the other galaxies in the right panel of Figure fig:ms-mdust .

gmw_00013245.overview: The galaxy 434618 is a star-forming system [arXiv:2012.09447]. Spectral energy distribution fitting indicates that the galaxy has a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 9.39 solar masses [arXiv:2012.09447].

sources.summary: SED fitting revised 434618 to a lower stellar mass than prior estimates, so the authors excluded it from the non-detection stack used to measure average dust and gas properties.

quotes.quote: As a result of SED fitting in Section subsec:magphys , one source, 434618, turns out to be only log(M_*/M_ )=9.39_ 0.01 ^ +0.12 . This is probably due to using a different SED fitting code and/or different photometric catalog with deeper NIR and Spitzer data from the previous estimate onodera16 . Because the stellar mass of 434618 is 0.6 dex smaller than those of the other non-detected sources, we excluded this source so that we can investigate the average dust and gas properties of star-forming galaxies with similar stellar masses of log(M_ * /M_ )= \ 10.0--10.4.

gmw_00013246.name: CFHT-NBJ-C339
gmw_00013246.overview: CFHT-NBJ-C339 is a galaxy identified as a potential active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:1407.1047]. It exhibits a high central N2 value of 0.1, though its overall line ratio gradient is consistent with being flat [arXiv:1407.1047].

sources.summary: The paper flags CFHT-NBJ-C339 as the only sample galaxy with N2>0.0, suggesting possible AGN contamination, and notes its high central N2 but flat line-ratio gradient.

quotes.quote: In terms of AGN contamination only one of the galaxies in the entire sample has N2>0.0 (CFHT-NBJ-C339) which may indicate that it is an AGN kew2001 .

There is no evidence for the central annuli of any galaxy being dominated by AGN contamination except for the potential AGN CFHT-NBJ-C339 identified above. This galaxy has a high central N2 value of 0.1, although we note it has a line ratio gradient consistent with being flat so there is no central concentration.

gmw_00013247.name: U108
gmw_00013247.overview: U108 is an extragalactic source associated with an optical object [arXiv:2003.00135]. This optical counterpart has been interpreted as a system of interacting galaxies [arXiv:2003.00135].

sources.summary: The paper treats U108 as a previously identified extragalactic source: its radio counterpart lies near the Chandra position and matches an optical object interpreted as interacting galaxies.

quotes.quote: By cross-matching the 5 radio catalogue with our updated X-ray catalogue, we sought radio sources that lie within P_ err , and found 7 radio sources positionally coincident with Chandra sources, including U12 (PSR 1740-5340), U18, U97, U108 and 3 new sources (W127, W129 and W135) that are likely AGNs.

U108 is near an extended object on the optical images, which was interpreted as interacting galaxies by Cohn10 . We found a 7.7 radio source 0.57 ( 1.2P_ err ) from the nominal X-ray position, slightly outside the 95 error circle; however, the radio position agrees closely with the optical counterpart, suggesting that the radio and X-ray sources are probably associated with these galaxies (Fig. fig:all_finders ).

Our studies of the Chandra and ATCA observations reveal radio continuum counterparts to 7 X-ray sources including a known MSP (U12; PSR 1740-5340), a ``hidden" pulsar (U18) likely obscured by matter stripped from the companion, a BH candidate (U97), a previously identified extragalactic source (U108) and three new sources (W127, W129 and W135), of which two (W127 and W135) are likely to be extragalactic, while the other, W129 has a definite match with a galaxy.

gmw_00013248.name: W127
gmw_00013248.overview: W127 is an extragalactic source that is likely an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2003.00135]. It exhibits a deficiency of soft X-ray counts, which suggests that the source is affected by high X-ray absorption [arXiv:2003.00135].

sources.summary: The paper identifies W127 as a new radio/X-ray source likely to be extragalactic, citing its cluster offset, high absorption, soft-count deficiency, absent Gaia counterpart, and corroborating radio detection.

quotes.quote: W127, W129 and W135 are also likely to be extragalactic sources. Arguments for their non-member nature include their large offsets from the cluster centre and, more importantly, their apparently high X-ray absorption (see below) , common in AGNs from the obscuring torus.
W127 is deficient of soft X-ray counts, so seems also to be affected by absorption (see Tab. tab:x_ray_catalog ).

Moreover, searches for possible optical counterparts to either W127 or W135 in the Gaia DR2 database did not reveal any objects up to twice the X-ray error radii (Sec. sec:updated_X-ray_catalog ), suggesting an extragalactic nature.

gmw_00013249.name: W129
gmw_00013249.overview: W129 is an extragalactic source associated with an extended, likely early-type galaxy [arXiv:2003.00135]. It is identified as a likely active galactic nucleus that exhibits heavy X-ray absorption and clear radio detections [arXiv:2003.00135].

sources.summary: The paper presents W129 as a definite galaxy match and likely AGN: an HST extended source, probably early type, coincides with a heavily absorbed radio/X-ray source.

quotes.quote: W127, W129 and W135 are also likely to be extragalactic sources. Arguments for their non-member nature include their large offsets from the cluster centre and, more importantly, their apparently high X-ray absorption (see below) , common in AGNs from the obscuring torus. W129 has a relatively ``soft" photon index of =2.7^ +1.4 _ 1.2 (Tab. tab:tab_fluxes ), but this fit requires heavy absorption of N_ H =1.2^ +6.6 _ 4.7 10^ 23 cm^ 2 (see Fig. fig:spec_w35_w125 ). In fact, we searched in the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST) http://archive.stsci.edu/ and found that the position of W129 is marginally covered by an HST WFC3/IR observation, where we visually identified that W129 is associated with an extended source, likely an early-type galaxy (Fig. fig:all_finders ).

A typical example is W129, which is heavily absorbed and has zero counts in the 0.5 1.5 keV band. The best-fitting power-law model (Fig. fig:spec_w35_w125 ) suggests an N_ H =11.8^ +6.6 _ 4.7 10^ 22 cm^ 2 , which is clearly above the cluster value of N_ H =1.57 10^ 21 cm^ 2 . Moreover, clear detections in the radio bands (Sec. sec:sec_likely_agns ) further corroborate their AGN nature.

gmw_00013250.name: W135
gmw_00013250.overview: W135 is a likely extragalactic source [arXiv:2003.00135] that is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2003.00135]. It exhibits high X-ray absorption [arXiv:2003.00135] and has no detected optical counterpart in the Gaia database [arXiv:2003.00135].

sources.summary: The paper describes W135 as a new likely extragalactic radio/X-ray source, supported by large cluster offset, high absorption, a very hard power-law fit, no Gaia counterpart, and radio detection.

quotes.quote: W127, W129 and W135 are also likely to be extragalactic sources. Arguments for their non-member nature include their large offsets from the cluster centre and, more importantly, their apparently high X-ray absorption (see below) , common in AGNs from the obscuring torus.

A power-law fit to W135 results in a very hard =-0.2^ +1.0 _ 1.2 . A negative value in this case fits the soft part of the spectrum, since we fixed the N_ H to the cluster value, which is too low to compensate for the dearth of soft photons.

Moreover, searches for possible optical counterparts to either W127 or W135 in the Gaia DR2 database did not reveal any objects up to twice the X-ray error radii (Sec. sec:updated_X-ray_catalog ), suggesting an extragalactic nature.

gmw_00013251.name: J1625 55
gmw_00013251.overview: The galaxy J1625 55 exhibits a large discrepancy in its measured linewidth-offset across different surveys [arXiv:1601.07162]. This offset is attributed to a single dominant profile peak that affected the measurement in the HIZOA-S catalog [arXiv:1601.07162].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J1625 55 as the largest linewidth-offset case, comparing narrow-band, HICAT, and HIZOA-S measurements and attributing the discrepancy to one dominant profile peak.

quotes.quote: This is shown clearly in the most notable offset (316 km s ^ 1 , for J1625 55). This galaxy is measured in the narrow band observations to have W_ P50 = 405 km s ^ 1 , which agrees within the uncertainty in the measurement of the same galaxy in the HICAT catalog 2004MNRAS.350.1195M which was W_ P50 = 453 km s ^ 1 . In contrast, the same galaxy was measured in the HIZOA-S catalog to have W_ P50 of 89 km s ^ 1 because one of the two peaks dominate their signal. This can be further explained by looking at the W_ P20 in the three surveys (narrow-band, HICAT and HIZOA-S) which all agree within the uncertainty.

gmw_00013252.name: J1059-66
gmw_00013252.overview: The galaxy J1059-66 is characterized by a lopsided profile [arXiv:1601.07162]. This profile features two peaks, with one peak being notably more prominent than the other [arXiv:1601.07162].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1059-66 as one of two galaxies with a notable offset; its displayed lopsided profile explains the large difference because one peak is more prominent than the other.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, J1059-66 J1149-64, both show a notable offset. They are identified by name on the left panel of the figure itself. The profiles of these two galaxies are displayed in the right panel of the plot. They demonstrate quite well why there is such a large difference: in both cases one of the two peaks is more prominent than the other peak in these lob-sided profiles.

gmw_00013253.name: J1149-64
gmw_00013253.overview: The galaxy J1149-64 is characterized by a lopsided profile [arXiv:1601.07162]. This lopsidedness is demonstrated by an offset in its profile, where one of its two peaks is stronger than the other [arXiv:1601.07162].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1149-64 as one of two galaxies with a notable offset; its displayed lopsided profile explains the large difference because one peak is more prominent than the other.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, J1059-66 J1149-64, both show a notable offset. They are identified by name on the left panel of the figure itself. The profiles of these two galaxies are displayed in the right panel of the plot. They demonstrate quite well why there is such a large difference: in both cases one of the two peaks is more prominent than the other peak in these lob-sided profiles.

gmw_00013254.name: 2FGL J2356.1+4034
gmw_00013254.overview: The source 2FGL J2356.1+4034 is associated with an uncertain BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1503.04202]. Its optical spectrum is dominated by emission from its elliptical host galaxy, which is located at a redshift of z=0.131 [arXiv:1503.04202].

sources.summary: The paper treats 2FGL J2356.1+4034 as the exception among four 2LAC BZB targets, finding an optical spectrum dominated by an elliptical host galaxy at z=0.131 rather than nonthermal BL Lac continuum.

quotes.quote: The only difference with respect to the 2LAC catalog concerns the remaining source 2FGL J2356.1+4034, which according to our analysis appears as an elliptical galaxy at z= 0.131 in the optical band, not dominated by a nonthermal continuum in the optical band, as might be expected for a BZB at z= 0.331 as reported in Ackermann et al. (2011).

All of them are indeed BZBs, two also previously confirmed by Shaw et al. (2013a), with the exception of the 2FGL J2356.1+4034 counterpart. According to our analysis, 2FGL J2356.1+4034 seems to be associated to an ``uncertain'' BL Lac that in the optical appears to be dominated by its host galaxy emission (see Sect.  sec:results for more details).

gmw_00013255.name: the brightest galaxy
gmw_00013255.overview: The brightest galaxy near the southwest density peak of the A3266 cluster is a dumbbell galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0508682]. Its components are oriented in a northeast-southwest direction, a morphology likely formed by the collision of two cluster cores [arXiv:astro-ph/0508682].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the brightest galaxy near the SW density peak as a NE-SW oriented dumb-bell galaxy and links its morphology to the A3266 cluster-core collision.

quotes.quote: The brightest galaxy is located at R.A. = 04^h31^m13^s Dec.= 61^ 27 13 , to the NE extreme of the SW peak. This galaxy is a dumb-bell galaxy hdd00 , with components oriented in the NE-SW direction.
In addition, the brightest galaxy shows a dumb-bell morphology and these structures are thought to form when the cores of two galaxy clusters collide tremaine90,qrw96 .
Since the cores of the two clusters have already been in contact, this collision can also explain the formation of the dumb-bell galaxy.

gmw_00013256.name: DES J0228-04
gmw_00013256.overview: DES J0228-04 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 1.905 [arXiv:1902.04206]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with an estimated mass of 4.4 billion solar masses [arXiv:1902.04206].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DES J0228-04 as an OzDES AGN at z = 1.905, measures a C iv lag of 358 days, and derives a 4.4 10^9 M black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: For this preliminary study using the partial data set, we recovered C iv lags for two of the AGN in this subset. The lags were measured for DES J022828.19-040044.30 at z = 1.905 and DES J003352.72-425452.60 at z = 2.593 (DES J0228-04 and DES J0033-42 respectively in future).
Only 0.3 of the realisations for DES J0228-04 failed to recover a lag resulting in an observed lag of _ obs = 358^ +126 _ 123 days.
The black hole masses are 4.4^ +2.0 _ 1.9 10 ^9 M for DES J0228-04 and 3.3^ +1.1 _ 1.2 10 ^9 M for DES J0033-42 (see Table tab:lagResults ).

gmw_00013257.name: DES J0033-42
gmw_00013257.overview: DES J0033-42 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 2.593 [arXiv:1902.04206]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with an estimated mass of 3.3 billion solar masses and exhibits a measured C IV emission line lag of 343 days [arXiv:1902.04206].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DES J0033-42 as an OzDES AGN at z = 2.593, measures a C iv lag of 343 days, and derives a 3.3 10^9 M black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: For this preliminary study using the partial data set, we recovered C iv lags for two of the AGN in this subset. The lags were measured for DES J022828.19-040044.30 at z = 1.905 and DES J003352.72-425452.60 at z = 2.593 (DES J0228-04 and DES J0033-42 respectively in future).
The analysis for DES J0033-42 only failed to recover a lag for 7 the realisations with a resulting time lag of _ obs = 343^ +58 _ 84 days.
The black hole masses are 4.4^ +2.0 _ 1.9 10 ^9 M for DES J0228-04 and 3.3^ +1.1 _ 1.2 10 ^9 M for DES J0033-42 (see Table tab:lagResults ).

gmw_00013258.name: MaNGA ID 1-44379
gmw_00013258.overview: MaNGA ID 1-44379 is a late-type active galactic nucleus (AGN) host galaxy [arXiv:1805.08655]. Its central region is dominated by an old stellar population, while younger and intermediate-age populations are present further out from the nucleus [arXiv:1805.08655].

sources.summary: Paper uses MaNGA ID 1-44379 as a late-type AGN example, finding old central populations, younger populations just outside the nucleus, enhanced intermediate-age light, reduced old fraction, and lower mean ages than controls.

quotes.quote: As an example, we have selected to show two typical sets of results in Figs. fig:late-comp and fig:early-comp , the results for one ``late-type" AGN (MaNGA ID 1-44379) and one ``early-type" AGN (MaNGA ID 1-95092) and their control galaxies. In the case of the late-type AGN of Fig. fig:late-comp , it shows that the RGB maps are dominated by the contribution of old populations in the centers for both the AGN hosts and the control galaxies. Just outward of the nucleus, younger populations dominate the AGN and the control galaxy CRT2 a bit further out, while the control galaxy CRT1 shows larger contribution of older age components also outside the nucleus.

The extinction at the nucleus is stronger for the second control than the AGN while outwards they reach similar values that are higher than those of the first control. Regarding the contribution of the different stellar population age bins to the light at 5700 , there is no difference between the AGN and the controls for the youngest age bins, while for the intermediate age one, its contribution in the AGN is larger than in its control galaxies at all radii. There is also a difference for the old age bin X_ O , that is lower in the AGN than in the control galaxies everywhere inside R_ e . These results also reflect in the mean age <t> : the mean age of the stellar population is lower everywhere in the galaxy for the AGN than for the control galaxies.

gmw_00013259.name: 1RXS J043445.0 + 030623
gmw_00013259.overview: 1RXS J043445.0 + 030623 is an active galactic nucleus and quasar candidate [arXiv:1007.1798]. The object exhibits zero proper motion, archival optical variability, and a lack of soft X-ray emission [arXiv:1007.1798].

sources.summary: The paper treats 1RXS J043445.0 + 030623 as an AGN/quasar candidate, citing prior AGN-candidate status, archival optical variability, zero proper motion, and X-ray hardness.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J043445.0 + 030623 = USNO-B1.0 0931-0068625: R.A. =04 : 34 : 44.443 , Dec. =+03 : 06 : 16.18 , J2000, proper motion (hereafter R.A., Dec.): ( 0 , 0 ) mas/year. Bai et al. (2007) mention this object among the Active Galactic Nuclei candidates but with no spectroscopic or photometric detail. The object is 2.1m brighter on the 1955 Red Palomar 1st epoch plate than on the corresponding 1990 image (see Fig. fig:J0434p0306 ). Zero proper motion in the USNO-B1.0 catalog as well as lack of soft emission in X-ray band (hardness according to 1RXS catalog is HR1 =1.00 0.43 , HR2 =0.83 0.53 ) favor quasar nature of the object.

gmw_00013260.name: 1RXS J184542.4 + 483134
gmw_00013260.overview: 1RXS J184542.4 + 483134 is a likely quasar that exhibits a large variability amplitude of at least five magnitudes [arXiv:1007.1798]. The object experienced a long outburst in 1990 followed by a gradual, monotonous fading over several years [arXiv:1007.1798].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1RXS J184542.4 + 483134 as a likely optically violent quasar, noting a long 1990 outburst, multi-year fading, large variability amplitude, and quasar-like X-ray hardness.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J184542.4 + 483134 = USNO-B1.0 1385-0291789: R.A. =18 : 45 : 42.622 , Dec. =+48 : 31 : 30.84 , J2000, proper motion: ( 0 , 0 ) mas/year. This source is of special interest. The object was at outburst on the Blue POSS-II Palomar plates taken on June 26th and July 21st, 1990 then gradually faded during several years. The source is still clearly seen on the Red plate of 1991 August 7th, while on 1992 August 26th it is already at the limit of sensitivity. The duration of outburst maximum (no less than 25 days) and monotonous fading of magnitude during more than two years are hardly consistent with the behavior of cataclysmic variables.

Most likely the object is a quasar with large amplitude of variability (at least 5m), and ROSAT observations were coincident with its flare. The extragalactic nature of this source is also supported by the parameters of X-ray hardness in 1RXS catalog ( HR1 =0.03 0.15 , HR2 =0.26 0.19 ) which are quite typical for quasars.

gmw_00013261.name: UDS-11484
gmw_00013261.overview: UDS-11484 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.186 [arXiv:2105.04025]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy cover the H-beta and oxygen emission line regions, providing measurements of its velocity dispersion [arXiv:2105.04025].

sources.summary: The paper uses UDS-11484 as a repeated-observation example, comparing KMOS and MOSFIRE spectra at z=2.186, noting similar signal-to-noise and two line-width measurements for the target.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:comparison for UDS-11484 we show an example of the spectra obtained for the same object (UDS-11484 at z=2.186) with KMOS and MOSFIRE in the region covering [ ] 4959, 5007 \ and H . Both spectra have similar S/N per unit time and unit wavelength.
The target UDS-11484 at z=2.2 appears twice (black squares) as its was measured from both H and [ ] .
Note that, as in Fig. fig:sigmas_comparison , there are 10 points instead of 9 in the figure for the two velocity dispersion values obtained using H and [ ] 5007 \ in UDS-11484.

gmw_00013262.name: COSMOS-18358
gmw_00013262.overview: COSMOS-18358 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:2105.04025]. Observations of the galaxy show a slight redshift discrepancy between its MOSFIRE and KMOS spectra due to sky emission line subtraction issues near the H-alpha line [arXiv:2105.04025].

sources.summary: The paper identifies COSMOS-18358 as an outlier in repeated velocity-dispersion measurements and traces the discrepancy to poorer KMOS sky subtraction near H compared with MOSFIRE.

quotes.quote: We can see from Fig. fig:sigmas_comparison that the measurements are in agreement within 1 except for the outlier source COSMOS-18358. In order to understand this discrepancy, we compare its KMOS and MOSFIRE 1D and 2D spectra (see Fig. fig:comparison for COSMOS18358 ).

It is clear from the figure that for this object, the sky subtraction for the MOSFIRE spectrum is better than for the KMOS one for which a sky emission line coincides with H . From the redshifts measured in both spectra, we find that z _ MOSFIRE is slightly lower than z _ KMOS (1.6492 vs 1.6494) which confirms that the blue side of H in the KMOS spectrum is affected by a bad subtraction of a sky emission line.

gmw_00013263.name: 3C 161
gmw_00013263.overview: 3C 161 is a compact, steep-spectrum extragalactic radio source [arXiv:0804.0849]. The source is heavily reddened and exhibits arcsecond-scale radio structure with a westward extension, though it lacks a secure optical identification [arXiv:0804.0849].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 161 as a heavily reddened compact steep-spectrum extragalactic radio source with arcsecond-scale structure, a westward extension in VLA imaging, and no secure optical identification.

quotes.quote: We present MERLIN observations of Galactic 21-cm absorption at an angular resolution of 0.1-0.2 arcsec and a velocity resolution of 0.5 km s ^ 1 , in the direction of three moderately low latitude (--8 <b< -12 ) extragalactic radio sources, 3C 111, 3C 123 and 3C 161, all of which are heavily reddened.

3C 161 This low-latitude heavily reddened field ( l = 215 44, b = --8 07) remains an empty field with no certain optical identification. 3C 161 is compact ( ujpf81 ; p82 ; ppr85 ) with observed radio structure on the arcsec scale. The later authors present a VLA image at 5 GHz (beam 0.4 arcsec) showing an obvious extension to the West. et04 have pointed out that 3C 161 is a CSS (compact steep spectrum source) with a spectral index of 1.2 . The source has also been discussed by km96 .

gmw_00013264.name: CBS 2
gmw_00013264.overview: CBS 2 is an active galactic nucleus with a redshift of approximately 1.78 [arXiv:1201.5038]. Its optical spectrum displays broad emission lines originating from gas moving at high speeds around a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1201.5038].

sources.summary: The paper classifies CBS 2 as an AGN with broad emission lines from gas around a central supermassive black hole, redshift z 1.78, and consistent repeat detections.

quotes.quote: Based on its optical spectrum, we classify CBS 2 as an AGN. Broad emission lines, coming from gas moving at high speeds around the central supermassive black hole, are prominently visible and constrain the redshift to z 1.78 . Our database includes two detections of CBS 2: the one analyzed here and a detection in ObsID 835 (2000 Jan 5; 26.5 ks GTI), in which the source is about half as bright (4.9 0.5 versus 10 0.7 cts ks ^ 1 ). The energy quantiles are consistent.

gmw_00013265.name: 2MASX J15040364 4155511
gmw_00013265.overview: 2MASX J15040364 4155511 is an interacting radio galaxy [arXiv:1302.4678] and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2311.12140] located at a redshift of approximately z=0.039 [arXiv:1302.4678, arXiv:2311.12140]. The galaxy features an unpolarized core and two polarized radio jets that extend for at least 100 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1302.4678].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J15040364 4155511 as a z=0.039 interacting/extragalactic radio source projected on SN 1006, with polarized jets, a mostly unpolarized core, and SNR emission contaminating part of the flux.

quotes.quote: Finally, these observations allowed us to measure the polarization of a bright extragalactic source that lies on the remnant's shell at a position angle of about 90 . This source is cataloged in NED as , an interacting galaxy Lau82 ; it is also coincident with a 2MASS galaxy (2MASX J15040364 4155511) at a redshift of z=0.039 and is also likely the radio source PMN J1503 4145. rg93 noted that the fractional polarization was too high to be interpreted as the core of a radio galaxy or quasar.

Our data reveal two polarized jets emerging from a clearly unpolarized core (Fig.  galaxy ). The jets extend for at least 2 ^ ( 100 kpc) and have typical polarizations of 18 5 on the shell of SN 1006 and 65 15 beyond the rim, and the core has p= 3 0.8 , more in line with the value expected for the core of an extragalactic source e.g., saikia . The high polarization of that portion of the jet that lies beyond the rim is probably close to its true intrinsic value; however, over most of its extent the jet is superposed on the SNR emission which makes the fractional polarization appear lower than its true value. To support this argument, we note the remarkable difference between the flux density of the galaxy just to the East of the edge of SN 1006 ( 0.65 ) and immediately to the West ( 2 ), while the polarized flux for the same regions is about 0.46 and 0.52 \ respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J150403.60-415550.9 as a background FRI AGN whose polarized jet sightlines probe Faraday depth and depolarization through supernova remnant G327.6+14.6.

quotes.quote: A background FRI AGN crosses the limb of the remnant. This AGN is WISEA J150403.60-415550.9 at z=0.039207 2012ApJS..199...26H and a closeup of the peak Faraday depth around the AGN is given in Figure fig:G327.6+14.6_Close_RM . The background AGN can be a probe of the Faraday depth and depolarization through the remnant. Since sightlines to this background AGN pass through intermittent layers of polarized emission and Faraday rotating magnetized plasma, the Faraday spectra are expected to contain multiple components showing the difference in Faraday rotation between the polarized emission layers.

Spectra of sightlines toward the AGN jet passing through the SNR and close enough to the limb that the polarized emission from the SNR is detected (sightlines E-G) show two components. The first appears with a peak between 0 and 12 rad m ^ 2 and is the polarized emission from the AGN and the second, weaker component, appearing between +12 and +20 rad m ^ 2 is the polarized emission from the SNR.

gmw_00013266.name: Segue 1
gmw_00013266.overview: Segue 1 was discovered as an over-density of resolved stars [arXiv:1606.08778]. While initially suggested to be an extended globular cluster, kinematic measurements indicate it is a dark matter dominated dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1606.08778].

sources.summary: The paper treats Segue 1 as an ultra-faint dwarf/cluster candidate and tests a dark-matter-free star-cluster progenitor whose disruption near apogalacticon can reproduce its structure and boosted velocity dispersion.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Segue 1 is located at equatorial coordinates of _ 2000 151.77^ o , _ 2000 16.08^ o

quotes.quote: Segue 1 was discovered by bel07 as an over-density of resolved stars in imaging data from the SDSS and the authors suggest that Segue 1 is an extended globular cluster, possibly associated with the Sagittarius stream. This interpretation was contested by geh09 , who demonstrated that the kinematics of stars in Segue 1 clearly indicates that it is a dark matter dominated object. They measured the radial velocities of 24 stars in Segue 1 and claimed that this object is a dwarf galaxy rather than a globular cluster with a mass to light ratio of 1320 . That makes Segue 1 one of the most DM dominated objects known until this day.

Using a Plummer sphere as initial model, we find that the progenitor of Segue 1 can be a small star cluster that formed 10  Gyr ago with a scale-length of less than 6  pc and an initial mass of about 6 10^ 3  M _ . Today, Segue 1 has, according to our model, lost most of its mass, it is at the brink of destruction, and it is located close to its apo-galacticon. Therefore, we see an extremely boosted velocity dispersion (more than an order of magnitude) stemming almost entirely from unbound stars.

gmw_00013267.name: ID 43
gmw_00013267.overview: ID 43 is a faint high-redshift galaxy at a redshift of z = 2.4174 [arXiv:1911.12967]. It is classified as a radio-quiet, X-ray active galactic nucleus with an inverted radio spectrum [arXiv:1911.12967].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 43 as a faint inverted-spectrum galaxy at z=2.4174, with HST and Chandra detections; its X-ray luminosity marks it as an X-ray AGN classified as radio-quiet.

quotes.quote: ID 43 is a faint inverted spectrum source which is detected at 5.5 GHz and 9.5 GHz, but not detected in VLA 1.4 GHz imaging. It has a 5.5 GHz flux density of 92 11 Jy (H15) and 9.0 GHz flux density of 144 29 Jy from this work, and hence a spectral index of = 0.91 0.47 . It has a faint HST F606W band magnitude of 28.37 giavalisco2004 . The photometric redshift for this galaxy has been determined by several teams to range from 2.30 to 2.531 wuyts2008,bundy2009,rafferty2011,hsu2014 , which is consistent given differences in data and SED templates used.

This source also has a Chandra detection, and given z = 2.4174 its XRay (2 - 10 keV) luminosity is 10^ 42.877 erg s ^ 1 , so this source is an XRay AGN. If we extrapolate the radio SED then the 1.4 GHz flux density is expected to be 26 Jy, consistent with the non-detection in the VLA 1.4 GHz imaging (4 limit of 30 Jy). Given no Spitzer MIPS 24 m detection and a S_ 24 obs limit of 30 Jy from then this source is expected to have q_ 24 obs < 0.0 . This ratio is borderline between radio-loud and radio-quiet (see Bonzini et al. 2013 Figure 2), however we class it as a radio-quiet AGN given it is so faint in the radio bands.
