sources.summary: The paper treats IGR J14043 6148 as a hard X-ray source whose XRT position contains an SNR/AGN candidate; Chandra non-detection and lower flux imply variability favoring the AGN scenario.

quotes.quote: A XRT observation of the field was performed on 2010 June 2 (i.e., around 6 months before our \ pointing) revealing an X-ray source at R.A. (J2000) = 14^ h 04^ m 29 s . 63 , and Dec. = 61^ 47^ 19 . 7 with a 90 confidence error circle of 4 . 5 lan11 . In other words, the XRT source is compatible with the ISGRI position, but it is 2 . 4 away from (and incompatible with) the 2XMM source. Within the XRT error circle lies G311.45-0.13 which has been proposed to be either a supernova remnant (SNR) or a background active galactic nucleus (AGN).

IGR J14043 6148 We did not detect IGR J14043 6148 during our observation, nor were any other sources detected inside the ISGRI error circle of 4 . 5 radius. The 3 upper-limit on the source count rate is 0.008 counts s ^ 1 in the 0.3--10 keV band. A power law whose parameters are fixed to those derived from the XRT observation \ = 7 10^ 22 , and = 1.8 , lan11 yields an absorbed 2--10-keV flux 1.7 10^ 12 \ which is less than the flux measured with XRT 2.9 10^ 12 , lan11 or with ISGRI 4.6 10^ 12 , bir10 when translated to the same energy range (2--10 keV). This suggests that the source is variable in the X-rays which rules out a SNR, and points instead towards the AGN scenario proposed originally by bir10 and by lan11 .

gmw_00013329.name: WD J105223.24+313012.81
gmw_00013329.overview: WD J105223.24+313012.81 is a quasar identified from its SDSS spectrum [arXiv:2402.11015]. Despite its classification, the object exhibits a Gaia parallax of 5.1 ± 1.2 mas, making it an extreme parallax outlier [arXiv:2402.11015].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WD J105223.24+313012.81 as a quasar from its SDSS spectrum, noting catalogue quasar classifications and an unusually large Gaia parallax outlier.

quotes.quote: Only one match was found, WD J105223.24+313012.81, which has an SDSS spectrum. The features in the spectrum (Fig. fig:J1052spec ) clearly indicate that, despite the high white dwarf probability (Table tab:wd_vlass ), WD J105223.24+313012.81 is a quasar. The significant Gaia parallax of 5.1 1.2 mas is likely the main cause for the high P_ WD , but it is worth noting that GentileFusillo+21 do classify this object as a quasar in their Gaia SDSS spectroscopic sample. This object also appears as a quasar candidate in Gaia DR3.
WD J105223.24+313012.81 seems to be one of the more extreme examples ( 5 from the mean) of a quasar parallax outlier.

gmw_00013330.name: WD J105211.93-335559.93
gmw_00013330.overview: WD J105211.93-335559.93 is a quasar identified by its broad emission line [arXiv:2402.11015]. It exhibits a Gaia parallax of 2.52 ± 0.44 mas, serving as an example of how parallax precision does not imply accuracy [arXiv:2402.11015].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WD J105211.93-335559.93 as a quasar from a broad emission line and uses its significant Gaia parallax as an example of precise but inaccurate astrometry.

quotes.quote: The broad emission line shown by WD J105211.93-335559.93 points at a quasar nature. Like the other quasar identified in this sample, it also has a significant parallax of 2.52 0.44 mas. These two objects exemplify how parallax precision does not imply accuracy.

gmw_00013331.name: X6
gmw_00013331.overview: X6 is an extragalactic source identified as a Chandra X-ray source coincident with a GMRT radio emitter [arXiv:2211.15245]. It is classified as a plausible highly obscured blazar, specifically a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2211.15245].

sources.summary: The paper identifies X6 as a Chandra source coincident with a GMRT radio emitter and argues that its multiwavelength behavior makes it a plausible highly obscured blazar/FSRQ with an extragalactic origin.

quotes.quote: It is the one labeled as X6 in our survey (see Fig. fig:Chandra ), which is remarkably coincident with source X17 in Pooley et al paper. Its location is RA = 18:48:48.20 and DEC = -1:29:58.7 J2000.0, with an uncertainty of 0.3 arc-second in each coordinate. Therefore, it is consistent with the peak of the GMRT radio emission (see Fig. fig:Chandra ), while it is not coincident with MAXI J1848-015.

As no infrared counterpart is observed (see Section 2.4), we assume the blazar to be a Flat-Spectrum Radio Quasar (FSRQ) source with X-ray emission well described by a non-thermal spectrum dominated by jet synchrotron emission 2008Landt . So, we assume a power-law emission model of index 2.0 to simulate the X-ray spectrum with 16 photons.

Therefore, all the observational evidences taken together tip the scale in favor of the extragalactic origin of our source. Here we have presented a plausible blazar coincident with \ that may be contributing to its gamma-ray emission, thus proposing an alternative nature for this source.

gmw_00013332.name: J123120
gmw_00013332.overview: J123120 is a quasar classified as a clear non-extreme accretion source [arXiv:2208.02387]. Its spectrum is dominated by a broad component with a faint blue component [arXiv:2208.02387], and its line fits improve significantly when accounting for emission from a narrow-line region [arXiv:1702.08046].

sources.summary: The paper treats J123120 as a special quasar whose line fits improve when narrow-line-region emission is included, adopting a narrow-component fit and noting especially evident narrow structure in one line.

quotes.quote: A special case is represented instead by J123120, for which we originally considered only broad lines but the fit improved considerably taking into account an emission from the NLR too (see Section section:results and Fig. fig:fits_figures_J123120 ).

For J123120 we decided to do two fits, one using only the broad components (black points) and one including also the narrow ones. The latter fit was done in two ways: the first one leaving the width of the narrow component as a free parameter and the other one fixing it to the the one of ] in the UV range and to the one of [ ] and H in the optical range. The magenta points were adopted for the following analysis and correspond to the case in which we tie the narrow component in the UV spectrum and to the case in which we left it free in the optical range. While in the UV we could use the obvious technique of linking together the widths of the narrow components, we could not do the same in the optical because the narrow H component is much broader than the [ ] one and they cannot be reasonably linked together. The presence of a narrow component in J123120 is particularly evident for the \ line, although it improved the fits also fot the other lines.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1231+0725 as a clear non-xA outlier, with a BC-dominated spectrum, faint BLUE component, and BLUE-ratio discrepancies attributed to strong absorptions.

quotes.quote: The outlying sources identified were J0103 1104, a low EW source with a strong \ emission, and J1231+0725, a BC-dominated spectrum with a faint BLUE.
An example is J1231+0725, a clear non-xA source.
BLUE ratios black dots (e.g J1231+0725, J0034 0326, J2145 0758): what causes the discrepancies for the BLUE ratios is the strong absorptions in these three features.

gmw_00013333.name: J121911
gmw_00013333.overview: The quasar J121911 is characterized by a complex line profile with a boxier shape than similar sources [arXiv:1702.08046]. This profile is asymmetric and features an extended red wing, which makes its line-dispersion measurement unreliable [arXiv:1702.08046].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J121911 as the lone outlier in one linewidth comparison, with a boxier asymmetric profile and extended red wing; it remains in the sample, but its line-dispersion measurement is deemed unreliable.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:FWHM_comparison (first column) we notice the presence of only one outlier, J121911, for which the ] complex appears to have a more ``boxy'' shape with respect to those the other sources, resulting in an more asymmetric ] profile with an extended red wing (see the figures in Appendix for the results on the complete sample and Fig. all_lines_comparison for a comparison of the line profiles). We have checked that including or excluding this point does not affect the fit and we decide to leave it in the sample. The reason for this is that this point has larger errors, since the IDL routine we use to fit the linear relation (MPFITEXY, Williams2010 , based on the MPFIT package Markwardt2009 ) considers the errors in both x and y variables. J121911 does not stand out evidently in the case of the line dispersion (Fig. fig:sigma_comparison , first column), but for the same reason we do not consider it as a reliable measurement.

gmw_00013334.name: SDSS J205449.64+004149.8
gmw_00013334.overview: SDSS J205449.64+004149.8 is a quasar-galaxy pair and an intervening absorber [arXiv:2010.01769]. Observations of the system showed no detectable OH 1667 absorption, yielding an extremely low optical-depth limit [arXiv:2010.01769].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J205449.64+004149.8 as a QSO-galaxy pair/intervening absorber; its two-hour integration gave the lowest OH optical-depth limit, dominated the stack rms, and showed no OH 1667 absorption.

quotes.quote: Notes: (a) SDSS J205449.64+004149.8 is a QSO-galaxy pair (QGP) with the QSO redshift z=0.284 Dutta17 .
The extremely low limit of 0.03 resulted from a two hour integration toward J205449.64+004149.8.

We further added the spectrum of the intervening absorber J205449.64+004149.8 in the stacking and the result is shown in the right panel of Fig. fig:stack . The rms of the stacked spectrum, 0.00041, is then dominated by J205449.64+004149.8. We do not see any obvious OH 1667 absorption line in the stacked spectrum either.

gmw_00013335.name: U28
gmw_00013335.overview: U28 is a background galaxy that resembles an edge-on spiral [arXiv:1106.1927]. Its high X-ray-to-optical flux ratio suggests that the galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1106.1927].

sources.summary: The paper classifies U28 as a background galaxy-like counterpart, describing an edge-on spiral appearance and an X-ray-to-optical flux ratio consistent with an active galactic nucleus.

quotes.quote: Two objects, U28 and U108, were classified as background galaxies based on the extended appearance of their images. U28 resembles an edge-on spiral, while U108 has a more complex structure suggestive of interacting galaxies. The high X-ray to optical flux ratio of U28 (see flux_ratio ) suggests that it is an active galactic nucleus (AGN) .

gmw_00013336.name: U108
gmw_00013336.overview: U108 is a background galaxy that exhibits a complex optical structure [arXiv:1106.1927]. This morphology suggests that U108 may consist of interacting galaxies, with detected X-ray emission potentially produced by a galaxy collision [arXiv:1106.1927].

sources.summary: The paper classifies U108 as a background galaxy-like counterpart with complex structure suggesting interacting galaxies and possible X-ray emission from a galaxy collision.

quotes.quote: Two objects, U28 and U108, were classified as background galaxies based on the extended appearance of their images. U28 resembles an edge-on spiral, while U108 has a more complex structure suggestive of interacting galaxies. The high X-ray to optical flux ratio of U28 (see flux_ratio ) suggests that it is an active galactic nucleus (AGN) . While the flux ratio of U108 was not determined, due to the complex nature of the optical image, it is clearly much lower than that of U28 . One possibility is that we are detecting X-ray emission from U108 that is produced by a galaxy collision.

gmw_00013337.name: A bright submillimeter source
aliases_external: bright submillimeter source east of AU Mic
gmw_00013337.overview: A bright submillimeter source, which is most likely a galaxy, is located to the east of the AU Mic debris disk [arXiv:2207.05277]. This source has an integrated flux of 1.59 mJy and exhibits an elliptical Gaussian major axis FWHM of 448 mas [arXiv:2207.05277].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a bright submillimeter source east of AU Mic as most likely a galaxy, measures its Gaussian size, flux, position angle, and coordinates, then subtracts it from ALMA visibilities.

quotes.quote: A bright submillimeter source --- most likely a galaxy --- was detected to the East of the AU Mic debris disk (we note that the offset of the galaxy from the star is so large that it is not visible within the boundaries of Figure fig:AUMic ). In order to subtract this galaxy from the visibilities we used a mix of CASA and the Python package galario tazzari2018 .

We used the elliptical Gaussian fitting tool in the CASA task viewer to fit a 2-D Gaussian at the position of the galaxy. The fit yielded a major axis FWHM = 448 mas, a minor axis FWHM = 365 mas, an integrated flux of 1.59 mJy, a position angle of 175.7 , as well as a position of = 20h 45m 10.28s, = -31 20' 33.5".

Because the data is a three pointing mosaic, we multiplied each part of our mosaicked model galaxy by a 2D Gaussian with a FWHM = 9 29 at its respective pointing. This primary beam correction was necessary in order to replicate the effects of the primary beam in the ALMA data, which has a different effect on the galaxy for each of three pointings.

gmw_00013338.name: J1853 0010
gmw_00013338.overview: J1853 0010 is a background quasar that has been utilized as a calibrator in astronomical observations [arXiv:2303.09129]. The source exhibits an extended structure toward the east-northeast, which is likely caused by jetted emission [arXiv:2303.09129].

sources.summary: The paper uses J1853 0010 as a background quasar calibrator; it was missed in one epoch, showed jetted extended structure, produced a noisy parallax, and was discarded.

quotes.quote: Phase-reference blocks involved switching between G034.43+0.24 and three background quasars selected from the BeSSeL calibrator survey 2011ApJS..194...25I in the following sequence: T,Q1,T,Q1,T,Q1, T,Q2,T,Q2,T,Q2, T,Q3,T,Q3,T,Q3 ..., where T is the target G034.43+0.24, and Q1, Q2, Q3 are J1848+0138, J1853 0010 and J1855+0251, respectively.
The background quasars were detected in synthesis maps at all epochs with SNR 10, except for J1853-0010 on 2014 July 22 which was not detected.

The background quasar J1853 0010 exhibited extended structure toward the ENE direction, most likely owing to jetted emission, which significantly degraded the parallax fitting in the R.A. direction. For this background quasar, we obtained a parallax estimate of 0.235 0.047 mas, which has twice the formal uncertainty compared to the unresolved quasars J1855+0251 and J1848+0108. Since the extended structure seen toward J1853 0010 is likely variable, and thus could systematically affect the parallax estimate, we discarded those data.

gmw_00013339.name: J1855+0251
gmw_00013339.overview: J1855+0251 is a background quasar [arXiv:2303.09129]. It has been utilized as a phase-reference calibrator in astronomical observations to determine absolute positions and parallax solutions [arXiv:2303.09129].

sources.summary: The paper uses J1855+0251 as a BeSSeL background quasar calibrator, retains it in the final parallax solution, and gives its reference position.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The absolute position was determined by referencing the background quasar J1855+0251 located at _ J2000 =18^ h 55^ m 35 4364 , _ J2000 = +02^ 51^ 19 5630 .

quotes.quote: Phase-reference blocks involved switching between G034.43+0.24 and three background quasars selected from the BeSSeL calibrator survey 2011ApJS..194...25I in the following sequence: T,Q1,T,Q1,T,Q1, T,Q2,T,Q2,T,Q2, T,Q3,T,Q3,T,Q3 ..., where T is the target G034.43+0.24, and Q1, Q2, Q3 are J1848+0138, J1853 0010 and J1855+0251, respectively.

For our final result, we only used background quasars J1855+0251 and J1848+0108. Table  tab:ppm lists the combined results for the 24 good maser spots relative to these background quasars. We achieved a ^2 per degree-of-freedom ( ^2 in Table  tab:ppm ) near unity for error floor values of 0.055 and 0.118 mas in the R.A. and decl. directions, respectively.
The absolute position was determined by referencing the background quasar J1855+0251 located at _ J2000 =18^ h 55^ m 35 4364 , _ J2000 = +02^ 51^ 19 5630 .

gmw_00013340.name: PG0935+417
gmw_00013340.overview: PG0935+417 is a luminous, radio-quiet quasar with a standard blue spectral shape and ordinary broad emission lines [arXiv:1009.1890]. It hosts an extreme high-velocity mini-broad absorption line outflow that exhibits complex, asymmetric profiles and line variability [arXiv:1009.1890].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PG0935+417 as a luminous radio-quiet quasar with an extreme high-velocity, variable mini-BAL outflow, complex absorption profiles, and weak X-ray absorption relative to typical BAL quasars.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of \ and \ absorption in a system of ``mini-broad" absorption lines (mini-BALs) previously reported to have variable \ in the quasar PG0935+417. The formation of these lines in an extreme high-velocity quasar outflow (with v 50000 ) is confirmed by the line variability, broad smooth absorption profiles, and partial covering of the background light source. \ and lower ionization metals are not clearly present. The line profiles are complex and asymmetric, with Full Widths at Half Minimum (FWHMs) of different components in the range 660 to 2510 .

Nonetheless, there does not appear to be anything unusual about PG 0935+417 that would produce an unusually fast flow. It does have a high luminosity, but the Eddington ratio is very modest, L / L_ Edd 0.2 (based on our value of M_ BH 2 x 10 ^ 10 M_ , 4.2). It is also radio-quiet, it has a standard blue spectral shape and ordinary looking broad emission lines for a luminous quasar. One interesting property of PG 0935+417 is that it appears to have much less X-ray absorption than typical BAL quasars.

gmw_00013341.name: 4C 46.09
gmw_00013341.overview: 4C 46.09 is a background active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.195 [arXiv:2004.07465]. It belongs to a cluster of galaxies and is seen in projection toward the supernova remnant HB 9 [arXiv:2004.07465].

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 46.09 as a background AGN at z=0.195 projected toward HB 9, but notes prior work found it and surrounding intracluster gas could not explain the Ginga hard X-ray flux.

quotes.quote: YK93 speculated about different mechanisms that might produce it: the background active galactic nucleus (AGN) known as 4C 46.09 seen in projection toward HB 9 ( z =0.195: ), an intracluster gas associated with the cluster of galaxies to which 4C 46.09 belongs, or a second X-ray emitting plasma associated with HB 9. The authors argued that 4C 46.09 and the intracluster gas cannot explain the total flux.

The authors argued that the hard X-ray component may not be fully attributable to 4C 46.09 and surrounding intracluster gas, and at least 30 of the observed flux ( 2 10 ^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ) is likely attributable to HB 9.

gmw_00013342.name: 3FGL J0427.3 3900
gmw_00013342.overview: 3FGL J0427.3 3900 is a high-redshift blazar candidate of uncertain type [arXiv:2002.07740]. Photometric redshift estimates using power-law templates place the source at a redshift of z=1.42 [arXiv:2002.07740].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3FGL J0427.3 3900 as a high-redshift BCU II source; power-law SED fitting gives z=1.42, preferred over galaxy-template redshifts with broad emission lines.

quotes.quote: Based on the criteria mentioned in the previous section ( z>1.3 , P_z > 90 and E(B-V) 0.30 ), we found 3 sources: 3FGL J0427.3 3900, 3FGL J0609.4 0248 and 3FGL J1110.4 1835, at redshifts greater than 1.3, which is consistent with the success rate of 10 for this technique observed in and . The photometric redshifts for these 3 sources were found to be z=1.42^ +0.12 _ 0.10 , z=1.73^ +0.11 _ 0.10 and z=1.56^ +0.09 _ 0.11 , respectively, using the power-law templates. The galaxy library which assumes hybrid QSO templates from yielded z values of 1.25^ +0.10 _ 0.10 , 1.34^ +0.09 _ 0.06 and 1.42^ +0.05 _ 0.05 , respectively. But since these sources have been identified as BCU II (3FGL J0427.3 3900) and BL Lacs (3FGL J0609.4 0248 and 3FGL J1110.4 1835) in the 3LAC, the redshifts determined by the galaxy templates with dominant broad emission lines are unlikely. Hence, we base our analysis on the z values derived from the power law templates.

gmw_00013343.name: 3FGL J0609.4 0248
gmw_00013343.overview: 3FGL J0609.4 0248 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2002.07740]. Power-law photometric fitting indicates the galaxy is located at a high redshift of approximately z=1.73 [arXiv:2002.07740].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 3FGL J0609.4 0248 as a BL Lac with z=1.73 from power-law photometric fitting; its 65.8 GeV photon places it near the cosmic gamma-ray horizon.

quotes.quote: Based on the criteria mentioned in the previous section ( z>1.3 , P_z > 90 and E(B-V) 0.30 ), we found 3 sources: 3FGL J0427.3 3900, 3FGL J0609.4 0248 and 3FGL J1110.4 1835, at redshifts greater than 1.3, which is consistent with the success rate of 10 for this technique observed in and . The photometric redshifts for these 3 sources were found to be z=1.42^ +0.12 _ 0.10 , z=1.73^ +0.11 _ 0.10 and z=1.56^ +0.09 _ 0.11 , respectively, using the power-law templates. The galaxy library which assumes hybrid QSO templates from yielded z values of 1.25^ +0.10 _ 0.10 , 1.34^ +0.09 _ 0.06 and 1.42^ +0.05 _ 0.05 , respectively. But since these sources have been identified as BCU II (3FGL J0427.3 3900) and BL Lacs (3FGL J0609.4 0248 and 3FGL J1110.4 1835) in the 3LAC, the redshifts determined by the galaxy templates with dominant broad emission lines are unlikely. Hence, we base our analysis on the z values derived from the power law templates.

The highest-energy photons of the two new sources are at 65.8 GeV (3FGL J0609.4 0248; z =1.73) and 76.6 GeV (3FGL J1110.4 1835; z =1.56), placing them right at the horizon. Thus, with this technique, we are able to locate sources that can be used to constrain the CGRH since they lie at a region where the optical depth, , is between 1.2-1.8 , making them extremely valuable in these studies where redshift data are scarce. Providing a redshift for these sources will enable better and more accurate measurement of the EBL.

gmw_00013344.name: 3FGL J1110.4 1835
gmw_00013344.overview: 3FGL J1110.4 1835 is a BL Lacertae object with an estimated redshift of z=1.56 [arXiv:2002.07740]. The galaxy has emitted a high-energy photon at 76.6 GeV, placing it near the cosmic gamma-ray horizon [arXiv:2002.07740].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 3FGL J1110.4 1835 as a BL Lac with z=1.56 from power-law photometric fitting; its 76.6 GeV photon places it near the cosmic gamma-ray horizon.

quotes.quote: Based on the criteria mentioned in the previous section ( z>1.3 , P_z > 90 and E(B-V) 0.30 ), we found 3 sources: 3FGL J0427.3 3900, 3FGL J0609.4 0248 and 3FGL J1110.4 1835, at redshifts greater than 1.3, which is consistent with the success rate of 10 for this technique observed in and . The photometric redshifts for these 3 sources were found to be z=1.42^ +0.12 _ 0.10 , z=1.73^ +0.11 _ 0.10 and z=1.56^ +0.09 _ 0.11 , respectively, using the power-law templates. The galaxy library which assumes hybrid QSO templates from yielded z values of 1.25^ +0.10 _ 0.10 , 1.34^ +0.09 _ 0.06 and 1.42^ +0.05 _ 0.05 , respectively. But since these sources have been identified as BCU II (3FGL J0427.3 3900) and BL Lacs (3FGL J0609.4 0248 and 3FGL J1110.4 1835) in the 3LAC, the redshifts determined by the galaxy templates with dominant broad emission lines are unlikely. Hence, we base our analysis on the z values derived from the power law templates.

The highest-energy photons of the two new sources are at 65.8 GeV (3FGL J0609.4 0248; z =1.73) and 76.6 GeV (3FGL J1110.4 1835; z =1.56), placing them right at the horizon. Thus, with this technique, we are able to locate sources that can be used to constrain the CGRH since they lie at a region where the optical depth, , is between 1.2-1.8 , making them extremely valuable in these studies where redshift data are scarce. Providing a redshift for these sources will enable better and more accurate measurement of the EBL.

gmw_00013345.name: source 204
gmw_00013345.overview: Source 204 is a genuine Type II quasar with a narrow-line optical spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0407293]. X-ray observations reveal an absorbed spectrum with a column density of approximately 1 to 2 times 10^22 cm^-2 and an intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity of 10^44.6 to 10^44.7 erg/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0407293].

sources.summary: The paper describes source 204 as a clear XMM/ROSAT detection whose absorbed spectrum, luminosity, column density, and narrow-line optical spectrum fit a genuine Type II quasar.

quotes.quote: Source 204 has 510 counts in the pn + MOS1 + MOS2 data.

A single power-law model is clearly not an adequate fit to \ data; the quality of the fit is poor ( ^ 2 /d.o.f.=61.2/32) and the photon index is very flat ( =0.74^ +0.12 _ 0.13 ) , suggesting the presence of absorption. If an absorber at the source redshift ( z=0.732 ) is added to the previous model, the fit improves significantly ( ^ 2 /d.o.f.=45.4/31), although some residuals are still present at energies around 1.5 keV. These can be partially accounted for by the addition of a thermal component ( mekal model in xspec ), perhaps related to the AGN host galaxy or to a circumnuclear starburst, but the spectral parameters of this component are poorly constrained, and therefore it will not be considered in the following. The best-fitting spectrum [ shown in Fig. 3, panel (a) ] is parameterized by a photon index =1.30^ +0.16 _ 0.24 and a column density N_ H =1.26^ +0.75 _ 0.51 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

The intrinsic 2--10 keV luminosity (10 ^ 44.6-44.7 ) , the column density ( 1--2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) , and the narrow-line optical spectrum (Z03) are fully consistent with source 204 being a genuine Type II quasar.

gmw_00013346.name: source 239
gmw_00013346.overview: Source 239 is classified as a candidate Type II quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0407293]. The object was detected at a six-sigma level in the ROSAT All-Sky Survey due to an unusually long exposure time [arXiv:astro-ph/0407293].

sources.summary: The paper identifies source 239 as a candidate Type II quasar detected at the six-sigma level in RASS, aided by unusually long exposure and also appearing in Zakamska et al.'s RASS counterpart list.

quotes.quote: One candidate Type II quasar is detected (at the 6 level) in the RASS (source 239 in Table 1). The detection of this source in the RASS was possible due to a longer than typical exposure ( 1100 s as compared to typical values of a few hundred seconds for most of the other Z03 sources).

Among these, source 239 is in common with our list (see Table 1), while all of the remaining sources are below the detection threshold adopted in the present paper (three have 3.0 detections, one is detected at the 3.4 level, and one at the 3.6 level).

gmw_00013347.name: GRB 060714 host
gmw_00013347.overview: The GRB 060714 host is a high-redshift galaxy that exhibits Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0609450]. This emission corresponds to an uncorrected star formation rate of approximately 0.8 solar masses per year, which serves as a strict lower limit for the galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0609450].

sources.summary: The paper reports Ly emission from the GRB 060714 host, converts it to luminosity, derives a 0.8 solar-mass-per-year star-formation rate, and notes this is an extinction-uncorrected lower limit.

quotes.quote: There is clearly a Ly emission in the centre of the GRB 060714 trough (Fig ly-emission.fig , see also Fig dla.fig ), with an approximate flux of 1.3 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . In our assumed cosmology, ( _ m , _ , h ) = (0.3, 0.7, 0.7), this corresponds to a luminosity of 7.9 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 . We can use this result to derive the star-formation rate (SFR), assuming that a Ly luminosity of 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 corresponds to a SFR of 1 M_ yr ^ 1 (Kennicutt ; Cowie Hu ). The Ly SFR in the GRB 060714 host is thus 0.8 M_ yr ^ 1 , which is within the range of values found for other GRB hosts (e.g. Fynbo et al. ; Jakobsson et al. ). We note that this value has not been corrected for host extinction, and is therefore a strict lower limit to the actual SFR.

gmw_00013348.name: PKS 0446+112
gmw_00013348.overview: PKS 0446+112 is a high-redshift blazar located at a redshift of z=2.153 [arXiv:2405.10684]. The source exhibits strong jet activity and is classified as intermediate between a flat-spectrum radio quasar and a BL Lac object [arXiv:2405.10684].

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0446+112 as a high-redshift MeV blazar, confirming z=2.153 and finding continuum brightening, CIV weakening, strong jet activity, and an intermediate FSRQ/BL Lac classification.

quotes.quote: Following the high activity of the ray Fermi source 4FGL J0449.1+1121 (PKS 0446+112), possibly associated with a IceCube neutrino event IC-240105A, we obtained optical spectroscopy with the Gran Telescopio Canarias of the counterpart. We detect a clear emission line at 3830 \ identified as Ly that confirms the redshift of source at z=2.153. Comparing with previous spectroscopy, we find an increase of the continuum by a factor 10, and a significant decrease of the CIV 1550 emission line flux by a factor 5.

This produces a dramatic drop of the Equivalent Width from 20 \ to 0.8 , which is suggestive of a very high jet activity. The Full Width Half Maximum of the emission lines are midway (1000 - 2000 km/s) between those typical of the broad and narrow regions of quasars. Based on this, the source classification is intermediate between Flat Spectrum Radio Quasar and BL Lac object.

gmw_00013349.name: Great Dust Cloud
gmw_00013349.overview: The Great Dust Cloud is a compact structure located west of the star Fomalhaut [arXiv:2305.10480]. Although initially detected within Fomalhaut's Kuiper belt analog, its rising mid-infrared spectrum suggests it is most likely a background ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2305.10480].

sources.summary: The paper argues that the Great Dust Cloud near Fomalhaut is a chance-aligned background source, most likely a background galaxy, because ALMA/Keck/JWST detections and its rising mid-infrared spectrum disfavor a stellar source.

quotes.quote: Another JWST discovery at 23.0 and 25.5 m is a compact structure that lies west of the star within the KBA, dubbed the ``Great Dust Cloud'' (GDC). This source is 89 Jy at 25.5 m, compared to the star's 2.5 Jy, so extremely faint. Because it appears in both the 23.0 and 25.5 m data, it is confirmed as a real astrophysical source as opposed to an instrumental or data reduction artifact. The fact that the GDC lies almost perfectly within Fomalhaut's narrow KBA (i.e. a special location in the image) suggests that it is less likely to be a background source, though 2023NatAs.tmp...93G make clear that with only a single epoch of JWST observation the possibility that the GDC is a background source cannot be ruled out.

First considering the nature of the GDC, a stellar source is unlikely because it would require a bright infrared excess component; the Keck flux is about 4 Jy and therefore roughly 10--20 times fainter than in the mid-IR and millimeter fluxes, which implies L_ excess /L_ > 10 , a level seen for bright protoplanetary disks. The spectrum is rising from 23 to 25.5 m 2023NatAs.tmp...93G , which implies the peak is somewhere in the far-IR at a level higher than seen with JWST, which could easily imply L_ excess /L_ 1 , which is energetically impossible unless the excess component is self-luminous (which is not normally the case for circumstellar material). Such a spectrum is however consistent with an ultraluminous infrared galaxy ULIRG, e.g. 2020NatAs...4..467A . Thus, based on the spectrum alone, we conclude that it is most likely that the GDC is a background galaxy.

gmw_00013350.name: 1H 0419-577
gmw_00013350.overview: 1H 0419-577 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2401.17291]. Observations of the source have been used to measure oxygen column density and constrain Galactic absorption in its direction [arXiv:2401.17291].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1H 0419-577 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy observed with XMM-Newton RGS, giving its coordinates, exposure, and z=0 absorption-line measurement used to constrain Galactic absorption.

quotes.quote: We have found and analyzed all the observations with available data from the reflection grating spectrometer (RGS) onboard XMM-Newton, of the Seyfert 1 galaxy 1H 0419-577, collected between the years 2000-2018. The source is located at coordinates (RA,DEC)=(04h 26m 00.80s, 57d 12 01.0 ) or (l,b)=(266.987, -41.996) deg. The total available exposure covering the z=0 wavelength ( _ OVII =21.60 ) amounts to 369 ks.

In this work we add the constraints on the column density obtained in the direction of the bright source 1H 0419-577. By detecting the line and measuring an equivalent width of EW_ OVII =42 31 m , we derive an oxygen column density of N_O=(8.4 6.2) 10^ 15 cm^ 2 (converted to N_H=(1.7 1.3) 10^ 19 cm^ 2 assuming a O/H ratio of 4.9 10^ 4 , 2003ApJ...591.1220L ).

gmw_00013351.name: J152645.61+193006.7
gmw_00013351.overview: J152645.61+193006.7 is a quasar located at a redshift of 2.5771 [arXiv:1310.6799]. Multi-epoch observations revealed the disappearance of a high-velocity narrow Mg II absorption system, which may be intrinsic to the quasar [arXiv:1310.6799].

sources.summary: The paper reports that J152645.61+193006.7 was observed twice by SDSS; a high-velocity Mg II absorber disappeared and is likely intrinsic, though a foreground-object explanation remains possible.

quotes.quote: Quasar J152645.61+193006.7 ( z_ e =2.5771 ) was observed by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) on 2006 May 31, and again on 2011 April 9. The time interval of the SDSS two observations is 497.4 days at the quasar rest frame. From the spectra of this quasar, we detect a phenomenon of disappearance of a narrow Mg II 2796,2803 absorption system with a velocity up to 166,129 km s^ 1 with respect to the quasar.

The coverage fraction analysis shows that this absorber partially covers the background emission sources with an effective coverage fraction of C_ f =0.40 0.06 . The time variation analysis and the coverage fraction analysis imply that this absorber might be intrinsic to the quasar. However, the scenario of a cosmologically separated foreground object located at z=0.9170 accounting for the phenomenon cannot be ruled out according to current available data.

gmw_00013352.name: Source 69
gmw_00013352.overview: Source 69 is an extended radio source identified with a symmetric galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058]. Its radio morphology is closer to a Fanaroff-Riley class I galaxy, featuring diffuse lobes without hot spots and a strong symmetric jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source 69 with a symmetric galaxy and describes it as an FRI-like extended radio source with diffuse lobes and a strong symmetric jet rather than hot spots.

quotes.quote: 069. This extended radio source is identified with a symmetric galaxy. The contour plot of the entire radio source (Fig. 8) shows a morphology closer to FRI than FRII (lobes are diffuse with no hot spot).
The other three sources are FRI, including Source 69 that does not have hot spots but a strong symmetric jet.

gmw_00013353.name: source 212
gmw_00013353.overview: Source 212 is an extended active galactic nucleus with a radio core identified with a faint galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058]. It is the only such extended active galaxy to exhibit a classic Fanaroff-Riley II structure, featuring two symmetric, optically blank radio lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058].

sources.summary: The paper treats source 212 as an extended active galaxy: a faint-galaxy radio core with symmetric, optically blank lobes, making it the only extended AGN with classic FRII structure.

quotes.quote: For example, source 212 contains a radio core that is identified with a faint galaxy. Two asymmetric radio sources, about 40'' away, have no co-located optical counterpart, have a structure that is suggestive of an FRII double source and are symmetrically disposed with respect to the suggested core component.
Only one extended AGN has a classic FRII structure, source 212.

gmw_00013354.name: Source 304
gmw_00013354.overview: Source 304 is an extended active galaxy located in the SSA13 field [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058]. It exhibits an intermediate Fanaroff-Riley class I-II radio morphology with well-resolved hot spots and a largest angular size exceeding 4 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Source 304 as an extended active galaxy with intermediate FRI-II morphology, resolved hot spots, and a largest angular size above 4 arcsec.

quotes.quote: 304. An extended active galaxy with an intermediate FRI-II morphology.
Source 503 is closer to an FRI than an FRII (the small component the north is source 507), and Source 304 is intermediate type with well-resolve hot spots.
Five of the 13 sources larger than 4'' in the SSA13 field are extended active galaxies (212, 304, 446, 503 and 621).

gmw_00013355.overview: The radio source 446 is an active galaxy located in the SSA13 field [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058]. Its peak radio emission coincides with a circular galaxy, which is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy with jets extending north and south of its core [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058].

sources.summary: The paper says 446 is a circular-galaxy radio source with north-south jets, classified as an FRI galaxy and among the large extended active galaxies in SSA13.

quotes.quote: 446. The peak of the radio emission is coincident with a circular galaxy. Radio jets emanate north and south of the core for 5'' , and the source has been classified as an FRI galaxy.
Five of the 13 sources larger than 4'' in the SSA13 field are extended active galaxies (212, 304, 446, 503 and 621).

gmw_00013356.name: Source 503
gmw_00013356.overview: Source 503 is an extended active galaxy with a radio morphology that is closer to a Fanaroff-Riley class I than a class II [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058]. A compact source, designated source 507, is located near the northern lobe or tip of this active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source 503 as an extended active galaxy closer to FRI than FRII and notes compact source 507 near its northern lobe or tip.

quotes.quote: 503. An extended active galaxy with a morphology closer to FRI than to FRII.
Source 503 is closer to an FRI than an FRII (the small component the north is source 507), and Source 304 is intermediate type with well-resolve hot spots.
Another uncertainty concerns the compact source 507 near the northern tip of the extended AGN, source 503.

gmw_00013357.name: Source 549
gmw_00013357.overview: Source 549 is an extended active galaxy that features a strong radio core [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058]. It exhibits radio emission extending to both the east and the west, but its lobes are too weak to definitively classify it as either a Fanaroff-Riley I or II source [arXiv:astro-ph/0607058].

sources.summary: The paper associates Source 549's strong radio core and east-west emission with an extended active galaxy, but says weak lobes make its FRI/FRII classification uncertain.

quotes.quote: 549. The strong radio core and the emission to the west and east are associated with an extended active galaxy. It could be an FRII source, but the lobes are too weak to be certain.
Source 549 is too weak to determine if it is FRI or FRII.

gmw_00013358.name: J0311 1722: DLA at z_ abs =3.73400
gmw_00013358.overview: J0311 1722: DLA at z_ abs =3.73400 is a high-redshift protogalaxy [arXiv:1106.2805]. It is identified as a very metal-poor damped Lyman-alpha system at an absorption redshift of 3.734, featuring unsaturated metal absorption lines modeled by four distinct cloud components [arXiv:1106.2805].

sources.summary: The paper re-analyses the J0311 1722 sightline DLA/sub-DLA, deriving a higher neutral hydrogen column, unsaturated metal absorption, and a four-component cloud model.

quotes.quote: The VMP DLA along the line-of-sight to J0311 1722 (J2000.0: 03 11 15. 20 , 17 22 47. 4 ) was first identified by Per01 . Follow-up UVES spectroscopy by Per05 revealed a \ absorber at z_ abs =3.734 with [ /cm ^ 2 ] =19.48 0.10 , which is lower than the conventional limit for DLAs set by Wol86 , [ /cm ^ 2 ] 20.3 .

Having re-analysed these data, giving higher priority to the \ and \ lines, we find that a better fit results if [ /cm ^ 2 ] =20.30 0.06 (see top panel of Figure fig:J0311m1722 ), provided that the Doppler parameter is b 30 km s ^ 1 .

In the lower panels of Figure fig:J0311m1722 we present a selection of the metal lines associated with this VMP DLA. All of the available metal absorption lines are unsaturated, leading to reliable estimates of the metal column densities. A four component model was found to accurately reproduce the metal-line profiles, whilst maintaining reasonable estimates of the parameter errors (see Section sec:pfaa ).

gmw_00013359.name: J0831 + 3358: DLA at z_ abs =2.30364
gmw_00013359.overview: The high-redshift protogalaxy J0831 + 3358 is a damped Lyman-alpha system located at a redshift of 2.30364 [arXiv:1106.2805]. Its metal-line profiles are modeled as two components of roughly equal strength separated by 14 kilometers per second [arXiv:1106.2805].

sources.summary: The paper reports HIRES data for the J0831+3358 DLA, adopting Penprase et al.'s H I column density, fitting two metal-line components, and measuring or limiting several ions.

quotes.quote: Thus, for this DLA, we adopt the \ column density N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =20.25 0.15 derived by Pen10 from their observations of this QSO at R 5000 , which is sufficient to resolve the broad damped profile of the \ line. The metal-lines in our data are well fit by a model with two components of roughly equal strength separated by 14 km s ^ 1 (see Figure fig:J0831p3358 ). Details of the derived cloud model are presented in Table tab:cloud_models , with the associated column densities given in Table tab:J0831p3358_cd . The \ lines are saturated in this DLA, however, we have a clean measurement of N ( ) from a number of unsaturated \ lines. In Table tab:J0831p3358_cd we also provide 3 upper limits to the \ and \ column densities which are undetected at the signal-to-noise of our data.

gmw_00013360.name: J1001 + 0343: DLA at z_ abs =3.07841
gmw_00013360.overview: The damped Lyman-alpha system J1001 + 0343: DLA at z_ abs =3.07841 is classified as an extremely metal-poor protogalaxy [arXiv:1106.2805]. Its metal absorption lines are unsaturated and well-fit by a single-component cloud model [arXiv:1106.2805].

sources.summary: The paper finds the J1001+0343 DLA is extremely metal poor, has a well measured H I column, unsaturated metal lines, and a single-component cloud model consistent with prior work.

quotes.quote: The DLA inline to J1001 + 0343 was also investigated by Pen10 , being amongst the most metal-poor in their sample. From our observations we derive an \ column density of N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =20.21 0.05 from the wings of the \ absorption line. This compares well with the estimate by Pen10 , N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =20.15 0.10 .

The remaining panels of Figure fig:J1001p0343 showcase a number of the available absorption lines that were used in deriving the cloud model. In fact, all of the available metal absorption lines are unsaturated in this DLA, thus providing reliable measurements of the elemental abundances. Several metal-line transitions of varying strength are well fit by a single component cloud model with a Doppler parameter of b=7.0 0.1 km s ^ 1 (see Table tab:cloud_models ). The curve of growth analysis by Pen10 yielded a Doppler parameter of 7.5 km s ^ 1 , which is in good agreement with that found here.

gmw_00013361.name: J1037 + 0139: DLA at z_ abs =2.70487
gmw_00013361.overview: J1037 + 0139: DLA at z_ abs =2.70487 is a high-redshift protogalaxy [arXiv:1106.2805]. Its metal absorption is concentrated in a single component, and it has a measured neutral hydrogen column density of 10 to the power of 20.50 per square centimeter [arXiv:1106.2805].

sources.summary: The paper derives an H I column for the J1037+0139 DLA, fits its narrow metal absorption with a single component, and reports robust ion columns despite saturation.

quotes.quote: The \ line falls on the edge of the blue detector, but this does not affect the accuracy of the \ column density since the blue wing of the damped \ line is still intact, and can be fit using the redshift derived from the well-defined narrow metal absorption lines. Moreover, we have access to , which also exhibits damping wings (although not as strong as that of ). We derive an \ column density of N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =20.50 0.08 , which is consistent with estimates derived from the SDSS spectrum ( N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =20.45 ; ProWol09 ), as well as that derived by Pen10 ( N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =20.40 0.25 ).

Again, the metal absorption is concentrated in a single component, in this case with a Doppler parameter of 5.9 km s ^ 1 . The corresponding column densities for all of the available ions are listed in Table tab:J1037p0139_cd . For this system, both 1036 and 1334 are saturated and blended; however, we have a robust measure of the \ and \ column densities from several unsaturated transitions. We also have a clear detection of the \ triplet near _ 0 =1200 .

gmw_00013362.name: J1340 + 1106: DLA at z_ abs =2.50792
gmw_00013362.overview: J1340 + 1106: DLA at z_ abs =2.50792 is a high-redshift protogalaxy system containing a damped Lyman-alpha absorber at a redshift of 2.50792 [arXiv:1106.2805]. This system features narrow metal absorption lines [arXiv:1106.2805] and a dominant neutral gas component that is accompanied by nearby ionized gas [arXiv:1106.2805].

sources.summary: The paper reobserves the lower-redshift J1340+1106 DLA, deriving its H I column, modelling narrow metal lines, and separating the dominant neutral component from nearby ionized gas.

quotes.quote: Since the broad damped profile of the \ line is independent of the spectral resolution, we combined the three datasets to obtain a high S/N ratio near the damped \ line, from which we derived N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =20.09 0.05 . This model fit, along with the combined data, is shown in the top panel of Figure fig:J1340p1106a . The profiles of the metal absorption lines, on the other hand, are narrow (FWHM 10 km s ^ 1 ); therefore, their observed profiles are not independent of the spectral resolution. Thus, we separately combined the data of equal spectral resolution and individually read these three reduced spectra into vpfit , which convolved the fitted model with the spectral resolution appropriate to the data.

Unfortunately, both of the \ absorption lines are saturated, and partially arise from nearby ionized gas, exhibiting a similar profile shape to the 1670 line. In fact, most of the metal absorption lines exhibit a second component on the blue wing (at v = -23 km s ^ 1 relative to the main component at z_ abs = 2.507921 ), which appears to arise from ionized gas. This is confirmed by the absence of this blue component in \ absorption (see 1302 , and note that the absorption on the blue wing of the 1039 line is due to unrelated absorption), which has long been known to accurately trace neutral gas FieSte71 . The presence of ionized gas is also confirmed by the higher / \ ratio exhibited by the blue component.

sources.summary: The paper treats the second J1340+1106 DLA separately, deriving a high H I column and a well constrained two-component metal-line cloud model from high signal-to-noise data.

quotes.quote: From the combined data we derive an \ column density of N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =21.00 0.06 . The combined spectrum in the region of the damped \ line, together with the profile fit, is reproduced in the top panel of Figure fig:J1340p1106b . Turning now to the metal absorption lines, we have found that a two component cloud model (with Doppler parameters of 9.2 0.1 and 6.55 0.05 km s ^ 1 separated by 22 km s ^ 1 ) provides a good fit to the data. This cloud model is perhaps the best determined in our dataset, given the high S/N of the data, and the numerous atomic transitions available. Our Voigt profile fits to the metal lines are shown in the lower panels of Figure fig:J1340p1106b .

gmw_00013363.name: J1419 + 0829: DLA at z_ abs =3.04973
gmw_00013363.overview: J1419 + 0829 is a high-redshift protogalaxy observed as a damped Lyman-alpha system at a redshift of 3.04973 [arXiv:1106.2805]. Its metal absorption profile is well-fitted by a two-component cloud model with a separation of 14 kilometers per second [arXiv:1106.2805].

sources.summary: The paper measures the J1419+0829 DLA's H I column from high signal-to-noise spectra and fits its metal absorption with a two-component cloud model constrained by multiple lines.

quotes.quote: This high S/N in combination with a virtually uninterrupted red wing to the \ absorption allows a very accurate measurement of the \ column density, N( H i )/ cm ^ 2 =20.40 0.03 . The Voigt profile fit to the \ line is shown in the top panel of Figure fig:J1419p0829 , with a selection of the associated metal absorption lines in the remaining panels. A cloud model with two components separated by 14 km s ^ 1 (Table tab:cloud_models ) provides a good fit to the data. This cloud model is well determined by a host of \ and \ lines (see Table tab:J1419p0829_cd ). Both \ 1334 and 1036 are saturated; column densities for , , and \ are listed in Table tab:J1419p0829_cd .

gmw_00013364.name: 3FGL J0049.0+4224
gmw_00013364.overview: 3FGL J0049.0+4224 is a blazar and BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of z = 0.302 [arXiv:1711.05476]. Its optical spectrum is characterized by power-law emission with diluted galaxy starlight, stellar absorption features, and weak emission lines [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3FGL J0049.0+4224 with coincident X-ray, optical, and radio sources, finding a BL Lac-like spectrum with diluted galaxy starlight, stellar absorption, weak emission lines, and z = 0.302.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0049.0+4224 : The analysis of Swift XRT data reveals one X-ray object (F=5.7 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ) in the 3FGL error box, that is spatially coincident with the optical source SDSS J004859+422351 (g = 19.9 ) and the radio source NVSS J004859+422350. Our optical spectrum is characterized by a power law (PL) emission ( = -0.16) with a signature of diluted galaxy starlight typical of BLL. We clearly detect stellar absorption features identified as Ca II (3934 , 3968 ), G-band (4305 ), and Mg I ( 5175), and two weak emission lines due to [ O II ] (3727 ) and [ O III ] (5007 ) at the redshift z = 0.302. This confirms the blazar nature of this ray source (see Fig. fig:spectra and Fig. fig:spectraCU ).

gmw_00013365.name: 3FGL J0102.1+0943
gmw_00013365.overview: 3FGL J0102.1+0943 is a high-synchrotron-peaked blazar candidate associated with the optical source SDSS J010217+094409 [arXiv:1711.05476]. Its optical spectrum is dominated by power-law emission, showing a weak Ca II break that suggests a tentative redshift of 0.42 [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3FGL J0102.1+0943 as a high-synchrotron-peaked blazar candidate, with a power-law optical spectrum and a weak Ca II break giving a tentative redshift near 0.42.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0102.1+0943 : This source is proposed to be associated to the optical source SDSS J010217+094409 as an high-synchrotron-peaked sources peak blazar at a redshift of 0.4 - 0.5 paiano2017ufo . Our optical spectrum is dominated by a PL emission ( = -0.10 ). We find a weak signature of a Ca II break at 5700 (see Fig. fig:spectraCU ) yielding a tentative redshift of 0.42.

gmw_00013366.name: 3FGLJ0239.0+2555
gmw_00013366.overview: The gamma-ray source 3FGLJ0239.0+2555 is associated with the optical counterpart SDSS J023853+255407 [arXiv:1711.05476]. This counterpart exhibits an optical spectrum dominated by power-law emission and has a proposed redshift of 0.584 based on a detected absorption doublet [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper proposes SDSS J023853+255407 as the optical counterpart of 3FGLJ0239.0+2555 and reports a power-law spectrum plus a possible Ca II doublet implying z = 0.584.

quotes.quote: 3FGLJ0239.0+2555 : Through the XRT data analysis, we find only one X-ray source within the 3FGL error box with a flux at the same level of the ray emission flux (see Fig. fig:0239 ). We propose the spatially coincident object SDSS J023853+255407 (g = 20.2) as the likely optical counterpart for this source (see Fig. fig:opt_skymap ). Our optical spectrum for this source is clearly dominated by a PL emission ( = -0.39 ) and a doublet absorption features (6231 , 6286 ) is detected. If identified as Ca II (3934 , 3968 ), the redshift is 0.584. Note that the red component of this doublet is partially contaminated by the telluric band at 6280 .

gmw_00013367.name: 3FGLJ0305.2-1607
gmw_00013367.overview: 3FGLJ0305.2-1607 is a gamma-ray emitting source associated with the radio source PKS 0302-16 [arXiv:1711.05476]. Located at a redshift of z=0.311, its optical spectrum exhibits stellar absorption features and [O II] emission indicative of modest star formation [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper links 3FGLJ0305.2-1607 to PKS 0302-16, reports BCU-II classification, and detects stellar absorption at z=0.311 plus [O II] emission suggesting modest star formation.

quotes.quote: 3FGLJ0305.2-1607 : This ray emitter is associated with the radio source PKS 0302-16 and classified as BCU-II in the 3LAC catalog, but no optical spectrum for this target is available in literature. The GTC optical spectrum clearly exhibits absorption features of the overall stellar population superimposed onto the non-thermal emission. In particular we detect absorption lines of Ca II (3934 , 3968 ), G-band (4305 ), Mg I (5157 ) and Na I (5893 ) at z=0.311, and moreover an emission line due to [ O II ] (L=6.2 10 ^ 40 erg/s) indicative of modest star formation gilbank2010 .

gmw_00013368.name: 3FGL J0409.8-0358
gmw_00013368.overview: The galaxy 3FGL J0409.8-0358 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1711.05476]. Its optical counterpart exhibits a featureless, lineless spectrum that establishes a redshift lower limit of z > 1.1 [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper confirms 3FGL J0409.8-0358 as a lineless BL Lac object and uses the high-S/N featureless spectrum to set a redshift lower limit of z > 1.1.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0409.8-0358 : The optical counterpart of this source was identified with a BLL by massaro2015b on the basis of the featureless optical spectrum. We obtain a much better S/N spectrum and we confirm that it is lineless. We can set a lower limit of the redshift of z > 1.1, following the procedure reported in paiano2017tev .

gmw_00013369.name: 3FGLJ0937.9-1435
gmw_00013369.overview: The active galactic nucleus 3FGLJ0937.9-1435 is associated with the X-ray source XRTJ093754-143350 and the optical source USNOB0754-0223141 [arXiv:1711.05476]. Its optical spectrum displays a characteristic nonthermal emission with host galaxy signatures and absorption lines at a redshift of z=0.287 [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XRTJ093754-143350 and USNOB0754-0223141 with 3FGLJ0937.9-1435, reporting nonthermal emission, host-galaxy features, and absorption lines at z=0.287.

quotes.quote: 3FGLJ0937.9-1435 : We found an X-ray emission within the 3FGL error box (XRTJ093754-143350) that is coincident with the optical source USNOB0754-0223141. The same association was also proposed using IR objects from the WISE survey massaro2013b The optical spectrum shows a characteristic non thermal emission with signature of host galaxy. We detect Ca II (3934 , 3968 ), G-band (4305 ), Mg I (5157 ) and Ca+Fe (5269 ) at z=0.287.

gmw_00013370.name: 3FGLJ0952.8+0711
gmw_00013370.overview: 3FGLJ0952.8+0711 is associated with the optical counterpart SDSS J095249.57+071329.9, which exhibits an optical spectrum dominated by non-thermal power law emission [arXiv:1711.05476]. Faint absorption features indicate a redshift of z=0.573, and two neighboring galaxies are located at the same redshift within a projected distance of 300 kpc [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper selects SDSS J095249.57+071329.9 as the plausible counterpart of 3FGLJ0952.8+0711, finding nonthermal emission, faint absorption at z=0.573, and nearby galaxies at the same redshift.

quotes.quote: 3FGLJ0952.8+0711 : Based on XRT data, the most plausible optical counterpart is the source SDSS J095249.57+071329.9 (g=18.9). No optical spectra are found in the literature. Our optical spectrum is clearly dominated by non-thermal power law emission and we are able to detect faint absorption features of Ca II (3934 , 3968 ) and G-band (4305 ) at z=0.573 . It is worth noting that from the SDSS data in the environment of this source there are two galaxies (projected distance < 300 kpc at z = 0.573) that exhibit the same redshift of the target.

gmw_00013371.name: 3FGL J1129.0+3758
gmw_00013371.overview: The source 3FGL J1129.0+3758 is classified as a high-redshift blazar candidate [arXiv:1711.05476]. Its optical spectrum exhibits numerous absorption features, including a strong magnesium II doublet that establishes a lower redshift limit of 1.211 [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3FGL J1129.0+3758 as a high-redshift blazar candidate whose optical spectrum contains many absorption features, including an Mg II system giving a lower limit z = 1.211.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J1129.0+3758 : This source is classified as UGS in the 3FGL catalog with a flux of F _ (1-100 GeV) = 6.99 10 ^ 10 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . paiano2017ufo proposed as optical counterpart the source SDSSJ112903+375656 with g = 20.3, classifying as a blazar at high redshift (z 1.4 - 1.8). This object is located at 5 arcsec West of a g = 14. star. In its optical spectrum, there are many clear absorption features in the spectral range between 4000 and <6000 . The strongest one, at 6189 , is a doublet that is consistent with an intervening absorption system due to Mg II (2800 ) at redshift z = 1.211.

gmw_00013372.name: 3FGLJ1704.4-0528
gmw_00013372.overview: 3FGLJ1704.4-0528 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1711.05476] associated with the optical source USNOB0845-0308445 [arXiv:1711.05476]. Its featureless optical spectrum confirms this classification, and the galaxy has an estimated redshift lower limit of greater than 0.7 [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper identifies USNOB0845-0308445 as the optical source for 3FGLJ1704.4-0528, confirms its BL Lac nature, and sets a host-galaxy-based redshift lower limit of z > 0.7.

quotes.quote: 3FGLJ1704.4-0528 From the Swift XRT data analysis we found only one bright X-ray source within the 3FGL error box, coincident with the optical source USNOB0845-0308445 (B = 19.5) (see Fig. fig:opt_skymap ). We obtained a S/N 100 optical spectrum that is featureless and confirms the BLL nature for this object. Based on the assumption of the typical BLL host galaxy, we can set a lower limit of the redshift of > 0.7.

gmw_00013373.name: 3FGLJ2115.2+1215
gmw_00013373.overview: The gamma-ray source 3FGLJ2115.2+1215 is associated with X-ray, radio, and optical counterparts [arXiv:1711.05476]. Its optical spectrum exhibits a nonthermal continuum with multiple absorption features that suggest a spectroscopic redshift lower limit of z > 0.497 [arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper links 3FGLJ2115.2+1215 to X-ray, radio, and optical counterparts, finding a nonthermal continuum and multiple absorption systems that imply redshift lower limits up to z = 0.633.

quotes.quote: 3FGLJ2115.2+1215 From the XRT analysis, we found the X-ray object XRTJ211522+121801 coincident with the radio source NVSSJ211522+121802 and the optical source SDSSJ211522+121802. The source is relatively bright (g = 17.2) and the optical spectrum exhibits a non thermal continuum. We detect a prominent absorption feature at 4191 (EW = 5.0) that, if interpreted as Mg II (2800 ), implies a spectroscopic redshift lower limit of z > 0.497. In addition, we detect two other fainter absorptions at 4270 and 4571 . They are likely two faint absorption systems that could be interpreted as intervening Mg II at z = 0.525 and z = 0.633.

gmw_00013374.name: 4C 31.03
gmw_00013374.overview: 4C 31.03 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z=0.603 [arXiv:2404.09137]. The source exhibits log-normal gamma-ray variability, which includes a double flux state representing both prolonged quiescent and active phases [arXiv:2404.09137].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C 31.03 as an FSRQ/blazar at RA=18.2227, DEC=32.1399, studies its 2023 outburst, and argues its ray production occurs beyond the BLR within the molecular torus.

quotes.quote: Recently, in January 2023, Fermi LAT reported an enhancement in the ray activity from the source 4C 31.03, consistent with the coordinates RA=18.2227 and DEC=32.1399 . 4C 31.03 is recognized as a flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) (z=0.603, ) and is included in the Fermi 4FGL point source catalog with identifier J0112.8+3208 . This source is also historically known in other names such as, B2 0110+31, OC 317, and NRAO 62. Further, it is considered as a possible candidate for the study of neutrino emission from Blazars ( ).

Our study supports the idea that the ray production in 4C 31.03 happens beyond the BLR, but within the molecular torus. The study of the variability of the ray emission from 4C 31.03 has been extended by performing a comprehensive long-term distribution study. The ray emission from 4C 31.03 exhibits log-normal variability and showcase a double flux state corresponding to the prolonged quiescent and the recent active phases.

gmw_00013375.name: S82X 0242+0005
gmw_00013375.overview: S82X 0242+0005 is a reddened X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.01578]. The galaxy exhibits hydrogen emission that requires two broad Gaussian components to fit, alongside a blue wing in its narrow oxygen doublet profile [arXiv:1709.01578].

sources.summary: The paper describes S82X 0242+0005 as a reddened X-ray AGN with H emission, two broad H components, and a blue [O III] wing in the narrow-line profile.

quotes.quote: For the remaining eight sources, we detected H emission and in two objects (S82X 0242+0005 and S82X 0141-0017), we also detect [ O 3 ] emission.
In two cases (S82X 0242+0005 and S82X 0302-0003), two broad Gaussian components were required to adequately fit the H emission.

We note that in S82X 0242+0005, the [ O 3 ] doublet has a blue wing to the narrow profile, which we accommodated with additional Gaussian components; we comment more on this feature in Section outflows .

gmw_00013376.name: S82X 0302-0003
gmw_00013376.overview: S82X 0302-0003 is a reddened active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.01578]. The source is radio-intermediate, detected by the FIRST survey, and requires two broad Gaussian components to fit its H-alpha emission [arXiv:1709.01578].

sources.summary: The paper describes S82X 0302-0003 as a reddened AGN with two H components, FIRST radio detection, and radio-intermediate properties based on SED and radio diagnostics.

quotes.quote: In two cases (S82X 0242+0005 and S82X 0302-0003), two broad Gaussian components were required to adequately fit the H emission.
The FIRST survey covers the full Stripe 82 region helfand , and only two of our 12 sources are detected by FIRST: S82X 0011+0057 and S82X 0302-0003.
We point out, however, that S82X 0302-0003, which is radio-intermediate appears to be truly reddened based on the E(B-V) values derived from SED decomposition.

gmw_00013377.name: S82X 0022+0020
gmw_00013377.overview: S82X 0022+0020 is an active galactic nucleus detected in hard X-rays [arXiv:1709.01578]. The source exhibits moderate X-ray obscuration and displays a blue wing in its [O III] doublet spectrum [arXiv:1709.01578].

sources.summary: The paper reports hard X-ray and [O III] detections for S82X 0022+0020, infers moderate X-ray obscuration, estimates its black hole mass, and fits a blue [O III] wing.

quotes.quote: Two of these sources, S82X 0022+0020 and S82X 0040+0058, are detected in hard X-rays and are at sufficiently low redshift that [ O 3 ] 5007 \ is observed in the SDSS spectrum. Based on our fits to the optical spectra (see Section supp_sdss_fit ), we find rest-frame [ O 3 ] 5007 \ flux values of (2.06 0.17) 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and (3.2 0.2) 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 for S82X 0022+0020 and S82X 0040+0058, respectively. With observed hard X-ray fluxes of (1.7 0.2) 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and (1.5 0.2 ) 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , we obtain log( F_ 2-10keV / F_ OIII ) values of 0.92 0.06 dex and 2.68 0.07 dex for S82X 0022+0020 and S82X 0040+0058.
These values are consistent with the hardness ratios: S82X 0022+0020 is moderately X-ray obscured while S82X 0040+0058 is X-ray unobscured.

An apparent blue wing to the [ O 3 ] doublet is present in S82X 0022+0020, which we are able to fit with broad Gaussian components in addition to narrow Gaussians to fit the narrow line doublet (Figure 0022_spec ).

gmw_00013378.name: S82X 0040+0058
gmw_00013378.overview: S82X 0040+0058 is an active galactic nucleus that is classified as X-ray unobscured [arXiv:1709.01578]. The galaxy is detected in hard X-rays and exhibits optical emission lines, including [O III] and Mg II [arXiv:1709.01578].

sources.summary: The paper reports hard X-ray and [O III] detections for S82X 0040+0058, finds it X-ray unobscured, and uses blended optical lines to estimate black hole properties.

quotes.quote: Two of these sources, S82X 0022+0020 and S82X 0040+0058, are detected in hard X-rays and are at sufficiently low redshift that [ O 3 ] 5007 \ is observed in the SDSS spectrum. Based on our fits to the optical spectra (see Section supp_sdss_fit ), we find rest-frame [ O 3 ] 5007 \ flux values of (2.06 0.17) 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and (3.2 0.2) 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 for S82X 0022+0020 and S82X 0040+0058, respectively. With observed hard X-ray fluxes of (1.7 0.2) 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and (1.5 0.2 ) 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , we obtain log( F_ 2-10keV / F_ OIII ) values of 0.92 0.06 dex and 2.68 0.07 dex for S82X 0022+0020 and S82X 0040+0058.
These values are consistent with the hardness ratios: S82X 0022+0020 is moderately X-ray obscured while S82X 0040+0058 is X-ray unobscured.
Since H is blended with the [ O 3 ] doublet and Fe 2 emission in S82X 0040+0058, we use the Mg 2 FWHM (1500 500 km s ^ 1 ; see Figure 0040_spec ) and the virial relation above to estimate M_ BH .

gmw_00013379.name: S82X 0011+0057
gmw_00013379.overview: S82X 0011+0057 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.01578]. It has been detected by both the FIRST survey and GALEX, and its red color is attributed to synchrotron emission [arXiv:1709.01578].

sources.summary: The paper classifies S82X 0011+0057 as radio loud, reports it as one of two FIRST-detected sources, notes a GALEX detection, and attributes its red color to synchrotron emission.

quotes.quote: The FIRST survey covers the full Stripe 82 region helfand , and only two of our 12 sources are detected by FIRST: S82X 0011+0057 and S82X 0302-0003.
While S82X 0302-0003 can be classified as radio-intermediate 1 < R < 2 ; miller , S82X 0011+0057 is radio loud.
Thus we conclude that the red R-K color for S82X 0011+0057 is due to synchrotron emission boosting the K band flux and the low E(B-V) value is to be expected.

gmw_00013380.name: SDSS J072659.52+373423.0
gmw_00013380.overview: SDSS J072659.52+373423.0 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1805.03888]. While previously suggested to have a tentative redshift of 1.577, its redshift is revised to 0.791 based on the detection of a Ca II absorption doublet [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper revises SDSS J072659.52+373423.0 from a tentative z=1.577 to z=0.791 by rejecting the earlier emission feature and detecting Ca II absorption.

quotes.quote: SDSS J072659.52+373423.0 : On the basis of emission feature at 7200 in the SDSS spectrum suggested a tentative redshift z = 1.577. We do not confirm the presence of this feature in our spectrum (SNR = 20) while we detect an absorption doublet at 7043-7107 that we identify as Ca II (3934-3968 ) at z = 0.791 . This feature, although it is encompassed by two atmospheric absorption bands, is firmly detected since it is revealed in both R1000B and R1000R spectra (see Figure 3).

gmw_00013381.name: SDSS J092902.42+194525.1
gmw_00013381.overview: SDSS J092902.42+194525.1 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1805.03888]. Its spectrum lacks detectable emission or absorption lines, leaving its redshift unknown, though a lower limit of z > 0.35 has been set from the non-detection of host galaxy starlight [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper rejects the previous narrow-line redshift for SDSS J092902.42+194525.1, finds no spectral lines, leaves the redshift unknown, and sets a lower limit from host starlight non-detection.

quotes.quote: SDSS J092902.42+194525.1 : proposed a tentative redshift z = 1.774 based on a narrow emission line at 7770 . We do not confirm this structure and suspect that it might be an arctifact due to filtering procedure of SDSS spectrum since it is not present in the unfiltered data. No other emission or absorption lines are detected in our spectrum (SNR = 25), therefore the redshift remains unknown. From the minimum detectable EW _ min of starlight features from the host galaxy paiano2017 we set a lower limit to z 0.35 . According to the expected number of Mg II intervening systems for sources at z > 1 , the probability of not detecting any one of them as in the case of this object is roughly 0.25 suggesting that it is unlikely that this BLL lies at high redshift.

gmw_00013382.name: SDSS J094257.81-004705.2
gmw_00013382.overview: SDSS J094257.81-004705.2 is a blazar and active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 1.36 [arXiv:1805.03888]. Its optical spectrum exhibits C III] and Mg II emission lines, as well as an intervening Mg II absorption system at z = 0.819 [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J094257.81-004705.2 at z=1.36 using C III] and Mg II emission lines and also detects an intervening Mg II absorption system.

quotes.quote: SDSS J094257.81-004705.2 : In our optical spectrum (SNR = 35) we detect two emission lines at 4509 and 6610 that we identify as CIII ] and Mg II at z = 1.36 . This confirms the previous tentative redshift based only on one spectral feature plotkin10 . In addition, we also measure an intervening Mg II absorption system at z = 0.819 .

gmw_00013383.name: SDSS J101115.63+010642.5
gmw_00013383.overview: SDSS J101115.63+010642.5 is a blazar and BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1805.03888]. Although previously assigned higher tentative redshifts, new spectral data reveals emission lines that place the active galactic nucleus at a more modest redshift of z = 0.857 [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper overturns the high-redshift interpretation for SDSS J101115.63+010642.5, identifying multiple emission lines that instead place it at z=0.857.

quotes.quote: SDSS J101115.63+010642.5 : A single broad emission feature at 9200 is visible in the SDSS spectrum. It was erroneously interpreted as Ly- by SDSS automatic procedure leading to the tentative redshift z 5 . In the work of the authors suggest a tentative redshift of z = 1.479 on the basis of narrow feature at 6930 . In our new data (SNR = 50) we detect the two features at 6929 and 9209 which we interpret as [ O II ] and [ O III ] doublet. Moreover, other fainter emission lines due to [ Ne V ] and H that are all consistent at more modest redshift z = 0.857 are revealed.

gmw_00013384.name: SDSS J121037.35+525341.9
gmw_00013384.overview: SDSS J121037.35+525341.9 is a BL Lacertae object and active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.916 [arXiv:1805.03888]. Its spectrum exhibits a faint broad magnesium II emission line as well as oxygen II emission [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J121037.35+525341.9 at z=0.916 by verifying the broad feature and detecting [O II] emission in new GTC-OSIRIS data.

quotes.quote: SDSS J121037.35+525341.9 : A faint broad emission line at 5350 is visible in the SDSS spectrum. If identified as MgII (2800 ) a tentative redshift z = 0.916 is derived plotkin10 . The newly GTC-OSIRIS data (SNR = 40) confirm the presence of the feature at same wavelength and in addition we detect clearly the [ O II ] (3727 ) emission line at 7142 .

gmw_00013385.name: SDSS J124030.93+344527.5
gmw_00013385.overview: SDSS J124030.93+344527.5 is a BL Lacertae object with a tentative redshift of z = 1.642 [arXiv:1805.03888]. It features an intervening absorption system at z = 1.449 and a complex optical continuum shape that cannot be described by a single power-law [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper confirms the weak feature in SDSS J124030.93+344527.5, finds an intervening absorption system, notes unidentified narrow absorptions, and fits its complex continuum.

quotes.quote: SDSS J124030.93+344527.5 : A tentative redshift of z = 1.642 was proposed on the basis of a marginally detected weak emission line at 7390 ascribed to Mg II (see ). We confirm the presence of the feature and we further detect an intervening absorption system at z = 1.449 by measuring absorption lines from Mg II and Fe II all the same redshift. We also note the presence of narrow absorption features in the GTC spectrum (SNR = 50) whose identification is not obvious and could be potentially associated to Fe II intervening systems at z 1.45 . The continuum shape is more complex than usual BL Lac sources and cannot be described by a single power-law in the optical region. We thus adopted a polynomial function to assess a fit of the continuum used to emphasise faint spectral line.

gmw_00013386.name: SDSS J124700.72+442318.8
gmw_00013386.overview: SDSS J124700.72+442318.8 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar [arXiv:1805.03888]. This active galactic nucleus has been detected in the gamma-ray band by the Fermi satellite and has an estimated redshift of z = 0.569 based on its [O III] emission [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper disputes the z=1.812 Mg II interpretation for SDSS J124700.72+442318.8, favoring [O III] at z=0.569 and noting its Fermi gamma-ray detection.

quotes.quote: SDSS J124700.72+442318.8 : The only available spectroscopical redshift estimation of z = 1.812 comes from the SDSS Sky Survey and it is based on a narrow emission line at 7857 ascribed to Mg II (2800 ). We confirm the alleged emission lines in our spectrum (SNR = 60) at the same wavelength, however the proposed interpretation as Mg II appears dubious. A more likely interpretation is emission from [ O III ] (5007 ) at z = 0.569 on the basis of the very small FWHM ( 300 km s ^ 1 ) and EW ( 2.30 ) . The target is also detected in the ray band by FERMI satellite (Acero et. al 2016).

gmw_00013387.name: SDSS J130925.52+430505.5
gmw_00013387.overview: SDSS J130925.52+430505.5 is a BL Lacertae object and active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.693 [arXiv:1805.03888]. Its spectrum exhibits [O II] and [O III] emission lines, a Mg II absorption feature from an associated cool gaseous halo, and a detection in the Fermi 3LAT catalog [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J130925.52+430505.5 at z=0.693 using [O II] and [O III], detects associated Mg II absorption, and notes its Fermi 3LAT detection.

quotes.quote: SDSS J130925.52+430505.5 : proposed a tentative redshift of z = 1.154 but no further indications of spectral features that sustain the measurements are reported. The target has been observed with the Keck telescope (equipped with LRIS) by Shaw et al 2013, where they propose z = 0.69 interpreting the narrow emission line at 6298 as [ O II ] (3727 ). In our higher SNR spectrum (SNR = 100), we confirm the presence of the [ O II ] (3727 ) and we also clearly detect the emission line from [ O III ] (5007 ) consistently at z = 0.693. In addition we also detect an absorption feature that we ascribe to Mg II system at z = 0.690 probably associated to the cool gaseous halo of the BLL. We remark also that this object belongs to the detected sources in FERMI 3LAT catalog (Acero et. al. 2016).

gmw_00013388.name: SDSS J132802.09+112913.6
gmw_00013388.overview: SDSS J132802.09+112913.6 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a BL Lacertae object or blazar [arXiv:1805.03888]. Although previously assigned a tentative redshift of 1.827, newer spectral data confirms the galaxy is located at a redshift of z = 0.580 [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper rejects the previous z=1.827 interpretation for SDSS J132802.09+112913.6 and instead identifies broad Mg II, [Ne V], and [O III] features at z=0.580.

quotes.quote: SDSS J132802.09+112913.6 : , based on a single weak emission line detected at 7915 in the SDSS spectrum and interpreted as Mg II (2800 ), proposed a tentative redshift z = 1.827 . This value is not confirmed by our data (SNR = 30) since we clearly detect two prominent broad emission lines at 4409 (EW = 86 ) ascribed to Mg II and [ Ne V ] consistently at z = 0.580 . The feature at 7915 is indeed due to [ O III ] (5700 ) (see Figure 1).

gmw_00013389.name: SDSS J144050.14+333350.2
gmw_00013389.overview: SDSS J144050.14+333350.2 is a blazar with a revised redshift of z = 1.747 [arXiv:1805.03888]. Its spectrum exhibits C IV, C III], and Mg II emission lines, along with two intervening Mg II absorption systems [arXiv:1805.03888].

sources.summary: The paper revises SDSS J144050.14+333350.2 to z=1.747, identifying C IV, C III], and Mg II emission plus two intervening Mg II absorption systems.

quotes.quote: SDSS J144050.14+333350.2 : From a single broad emission line heavly contamined by strong telluric band of O_2 , a tentative redshift z = 1.774 was suggested by interpreting it as Mg II. In addition to this feature, we also find further emission lines from C IV (1550 ) and C III ] (1909 ) at redshift z = 1.747 that is slightly different than the previously suggested one. In fact, the broad feature identified by and detected also in our data corresponds to transition of Mg II but with its blue wing strongly depressed by telluric lines. Finally, we also detect in our new data (SNR = 30) two intervening Mg II absorption systems at z = 0.578 and z = 1.168.

gmw_00013390.name: RBS0158
gmw_00013390.overview: RBS0158 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar located at a redshift of 0.313 [arXiv:1506.07006]. The source is associated with a radio counterpart and exhibits infrared colors that place it within the blazar strip [arXiv:1506.07006].

sources.summary: The paper associates RBS0158 with 1FHL J0110.0--4023, noting radio fluxes, a BL Lac optical classification at z=0.313, and WISE colors inside the blazar strip.
